# CaseTypeEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

CaseTypeEnum
============

This enumeration type lists the types of cases that may be created by the buyer or seller.

Type that uses CaseTypeEnum:

[CaseDetailsResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetailsResponse.html)

Call that uses one or more values of CaseTypeEnum:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| ITEM\_NOT\_RECEIVED | This enumeration value indicates that the case was created by the buyer because the item wasn't received within the expected delivery window. INR inquiries can also be escalated to INR cases. |
| RETURN | This enumeration value indicates that the case was created after a return request was escalated by the buyer or seller. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# RefundType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

RefundType
==========

This enumeration type contains the possible types of refunds - partial or full.

Type that uses RefundType:

[CaseDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html)

Call that uses one or more values of RefundType:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| FULL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer is scheduled to, or has already received a full refund. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that it is unknown whether the buyer is scheduled to, or has already received a full or a partial refund. |
| PARTIAL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer is scheduled to, or has already received a partial refund |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ReturnHoldStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnHoldStatusEnum
====================

This enumerated type lists the possible states of a return request that has been put on hold.

Type that uses ReturnHoldStatusEnum:

[ReturnHoldInfoType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnHoldInfoType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ReturnHoldStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| HOLD\_CREATION\_SUCCESS | This enumeration value indicates that the hold placed on the return request was created successfully. |
| HOLD\_RELEASE\_FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the attempt to release the hold on the return request failed. |
| HOLD\_RELEASE\_SUCCESS | This enumeration value indicates that the hold on the return request was released successfully. |
| INELIGIBLE\_TO\_RELEASE\_HOLD | This enumeration value indicates that the hold on the return request is not yet eligible to be released. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that the status of the hold on the return request is not classified or is unknown. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ReturnPolicyInfo - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnPolicyInfo
================

Type defining the **returnPolicyInfo** container, which consists of detailed information on a seller's return policy.

Type that uses ReturnPolicyInfo:

[ReturnPolicyProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyProfile.html)

Calls that use ReturnPolicyInfo:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Fields
------

[description](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#description)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[holidayReturns](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#holidayReturns)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[refundOption](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#refundOption)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[restockingFeeValue](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#restockingFeeValue)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[returnsAcceptedOption](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#returnsAcceptedOption)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[returnsWithinOption](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#returnsWithinOption)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[shippingCostPaidByOption](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#shippingCostPaidByOption)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[warrantyDurationOption](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#warrantyDurationOption)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[warrantyOfferedOption](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#warrantyOfferedOption)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[warrantyTypeOption](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html#warrantyTypeOption)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

  
  

description ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field contains the seller's detailed explanation for their return policy and is displayed in the Return Policy section of the View Item page. This field is valid in only the following marketplaces (the field is otherwise ignored):

*   Germany (DE)
*   Spain (ES)
*   France (FR)
*   Italy (IT)

Where valid, sellers can use this field to add details about their return policies. eBay uses this text string as-is in the Return Policy section of the View Item page. Avoid HTML and avoid character entity references (such as &pound; or &#163;). If you include special characters in the return policy description, use the literal UTF-8 or ISO-8559-1 character (e.g. £).

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#description)
 to learn which calls use description.

  

holidayReturns ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is deprecated as of v1.1.0 and any values supplied in this field are ignored. eBay no longer supports holiday returns.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#holidayReturns)
 to learn which calls use holidayReturns.

  

refundOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field indicates how the seller compensates buyers for [remorse returns](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/guides/features-guide/default.html#development/post-order-returns.html#return-reasons)
. You must set this value to `MoneyBack` for all eBay marketplaces except for the US marketplace.  
  
On `EBAY_US`: You can set this value to either `MoneyBack` or `MoneyBackOrReplacement`. If a seller has the depth of inventory to support an exchange for an identical item, you can set `MoneyBackOrReplacement`. Otherwise, eBay recommends you set this value to its default value, `MoneyBack`.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#refundOption)
 to learn which calls use refundOption.

  

restockingFeeValue ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is deprecated as of v1.1.0 and any values supplied in this field are ignored. eBay no longer supports restocking fees for returned items.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#restockingFeeValue)
 to learn which calls use restockingFeeValue.

  

returnsAcceptedOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This required field indicates whether or not the seller accepts returns.  
  
Applicable values are `ReturnsAccepted` or `ReturnsNotAccepted`. When set to `ReturnsAccepted`, this option indicates the seller allows items to be returned. Specify **ReturnsNotAccepted** for an item if the seller does not accept returns.  
  
On the eBay DE, IE, and UK, registered business sellers must accept returns for fixed-price items (including auction items with Buy It Now and any other fixed price formats) when the category requires a return policy. On some European sites, such as eBay Germany (DE), registered business sellers are required to accept returns. Use the Trading call **GetUser** to determine the status of an eBay business seller in DE, IE, and UK. Review the **User.SellerInfo.SellerBusinessType** field in the response.  
  
**Note:** In order for Top-Rated sellers to receive a Top-Rated Plus seal for their listings, returns must be accepted for their items (**returnsAcceptedOption** = `ReturnsAccepted`) and handling time should be set to zero-day (same-day shipping) or one-day shipping. Set the handling time (in days) using the **Item.DispatchTimeMax** field.  
Top-Rated listings qualify for the greatest average boost in Best Match and for the 20 percent Final Value Fee discount. For more information on eBay's Top-Rated seller program, see the [Becoming a Top Rated Seller and qualifying for Top Rated Plus](http://pages.ebay.com/help/sell/top-rated.html)
 page.  
  
**For setSellerProfile:** Updating the values in a business profile can effect the profiles in currently-listed items. In most cases, the updates made to a business policy can be streamlined into existing listings. However, if you update a business policy and the policy is invalid in the category where an item is listed, no error will be thrown and the policy will carry through the regular life of the listing. Sellers will be unable to re-list an item if it refers an invalid policy. Also note eBay will not update the policy's of a description if the listing has bids or sales, or if the listing ends within 12 hours.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#returnsAcceptedOption)
 to learn which calls use returnsAcceptedOption.

  

returnsWithinOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Specifies the amount of time the buyer has to return an item. The return period begins when the item is marked "delivered" at the buyer's specified ship-to location. Most marketplaces and categories support 30-day and 60-day return periods. eBay sites often set 30-days as the default value for this field and sellers are obligated to honor the values that are set for a listing. This value is required if **ReturnsAcceptedOption=ReturnsAccepted**.  
  
**Applicable values:** Retrieve the values supported by a marketplace by calling **GeteBayDetails** with **DetailName** set to `ReturnPolicyDetails`, then review **ReturnPolicyDetails.ReturnsWithin.ReturnsWithinOption** in the response.  
  
**Note:** In version 1.1.0, the options for this field were reduced. An error will be thrown if you attempt to list an item with invalid business policy settings. See the [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef//devzone/business-policies/releasenotes.html#v1.1.0)
 for details.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#returnsWithinOption)
 to learn which calls use returnsWithinOption.

  

shippingCostPaidByOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This option specifies either the buyer or the seller as the party who pays for return shipping charges. Accepted values are `Buyer` or `Seller`. eBay sites often set `Seller` as the default value for this field and sellers are obligated to honor the values that are set for a listing. This value is required if **ReturnsAcceptedOption=ReturnsAccepted**.  
  
Depending on the Seller's return policy and the specifics of a return, either the buyer or the seller can be responsible for the return shipping costs. Note that the seller is always responsible for return shipping costs for SNAD-related issues.  
  
Use **GeteBayDetails** (with **DetailName=ReturnPolicyDetails**) and review the returned **ReturnPolicyDetails.ShippingCostPaidBy** values to see the values supported by a marketplace.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#shippingCostPaidByOption)
 to learn which calls use shippingCostPaidByOption.

  

warrantyDurationOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is deprecated as of v1.1.0 and any values supplied in this field are ignored. eBay no longer supports warranty policies for returns.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#warrantyDurationOption)
 to learn which calls use warrantyDurationOption.

  

warrantyOfferedOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is deprecated as of v1.1.0 and any values supplied in this field are ignored. eBay no longer supports warranty policies for returns.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#warrantyOfferedOption)
 to learn which calls use warrantyOfferedOption.

  

warrantyTypeOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is deprecated as of v1.1.0 and any values supplied in this field are ignored. eBay no longer supports warranty policies for returns.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#warrantyTypeOption)
 to learn which calls use warrantyTypeOption.

---

# ReturnShippingCostType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnShippingCostType
======================

This enumerated type lists the types of shipping costs, including the cost of the shipping label, shipping insurance, and certified shipping.

Type that uses ReturnShippingCostType:

[ItemizedReturnShippingCostType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ItemizedReturnShippingCostType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ReturnShippingCostType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| INSURANCE | This enumeration value indicates that the shipping cost is the price of shipping insurance to ship the return item. |
| LABEL\_COST | This enumeration value indicates that the shipping cost is the price of the shipping label for the return item. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that the shipping cost is not classified or is not known. |
| SIGNATURE\_CONFIRMATION\_COST | This enumeration value indicates that the shipping cost is the price to ship the return item using certified shipping. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ErrorMessage - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)

*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ErrorMessage
============

Information regarding an error or warning that occurred when eBay processed the request. Not returned when the ack value is Success. Run-time errors are not reported here, but are instead reported as part of a SOAP fault.

Type that uses ErrorMessage:

[BaseServiceResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/BaseServiceResponse.html)

Calls that use ErrorMessage:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)
, [getProductDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductDetails.html)

Field
-----

error ( _[ErrorData](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Details about a single error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#error)
 to learn which calls use error.

# ReturnStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnStatusEnum
================

This enumeration type lists the possible statuses for a return request.

Type that uses ReturnStatusEnum:

[ReturnSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnSummaryType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ReturnStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| CLOSED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request has been closed |
| ESCALATED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request has been escalated to a return case. |
| ITEM\_DELIVERED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request item has been delivered to the seller. |
| ITEM\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request item has been shipped by the buyer. |
| LESS\_THAN\_A\_FULL\_REFUND\_ISSUED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial buyer refund was issued by the seller. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_DECLINED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has declined the partial refund amount that the seller has proposed. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the partial refund operation has failed. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial refund to the buyer has been initiated by the seller. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial refund has been requested by the buyer. |
| READY\_FOR\_SHIPPING | This enumeration value indicates that the return request item is ready to be shipped back by the buyer to the seller. |
| REPLACED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has acknowledged the receipt of the replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_CLOSED | This enumeration value indicates that the replacement item has been received and the return request has been closed.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_DELIVERED | This enumeration value indicates that the replacement item has been delivered to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_LABEL\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has requested a shipping label for the replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has requested a replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the replacement item has been shipped to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_STARTED | This enumeration value indicates that the process for the seller to send a replacement item to the buyer has started. |
| REPLACEMENT\_WAITING\_FOR\_RMA | This enumeration value indicates that the seller still needs to provide a Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) number for the replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| RETURN\_LABEL\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has requested a return shipping label. |
| RETURN\_REJECTED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer's return request has been rejected by the seller. |
| RETURN\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that a return request has been initiated by the buyer. |
| RETURN\_REQUESTED\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that a return request has been initiated by the buyer, but the system has timed out. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates that the current status of the return request is unknown. |
| WAITING\_FOR\_RETURN\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the return request is currently waiting for an eBay return shipping label to be available. |
| WAITING\_FOR\_RMA | This enumeration value indicates that the return request is currently waiting for the seller to provide a Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) number to the buyer. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# FileFormatEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

FileFormatEnum
==============

This enumerated type lists the file types that can be used for files that are associated with a return request or return request draft.

Type that uses FileFormatEnum:

[ReturnFileType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of FileFormatEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/files GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_files__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| BMP | This enumeration value indicates that the file format extension of the file is .BMP (Bitmap file). |
| GIF | This enumeration value indicates that the file format extension of the file is .GIF. |
| JPEG | This enumeration value indicates that the file format extension of the file is .JPEG. |
| JPG | This enumeration value indicates that the file format extension of the file is .JPG. |
| PNG | This enumeration value indicates that the file format extension of the file is .PNG (Portable Network Graphics file). |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates that the file format extension of the file isunknown. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# DesiredOutcomeTypeEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

DesiredOutcomeTypeEnum
======================

This enumerated type lists the desired outcomes that the buyer may specify when creating an INR inquiry.

Type that uses DesiredOutcomeTypeEnum:

[CreateInquiryRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CreateInquiryRequest.html)

Call that uses one or more values of DesiredOutcomeTypeEnum:

[/post-order/v2/inquiry POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry__post.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| FULL\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer no longer wants the item, and prefers a full refund from the seller instead. If this value is specified by the buyer, and the order line item still hasn't been shipped, the seller can use the **POST /post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId}/issue\_refund** method to issue a refund to the buyer instead of fulfilling and shipping the order line item. |
| STILL\_WANT\_ITEM | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer still wants the seller to ship the missing order line item. If this value is specified by the buyer, the seller should ship the order line item as soon as possible (to avoid the inquiry being escalated to a case), and then use the **POST /post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId}/provide\_shipment\_info** method to provide shipment tracking information and inform the buyer that the order line item is on its way. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ErrorData - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ErrorData
=========

A container for error details.

Type that uses ErrorData:

[ErrorMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorMessage.html)

Calls that use ErrorData:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)
, [getProductDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductDetails.html)

Fields
------

[category](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#category)
 ( _[ErrorCategory](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorCategory.html)
_ )

[domain](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#domain)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[errorId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#errorId)
 ( _[long](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long)
_ )

[exceptionId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#exceptionId)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[message](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#message)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[parameter](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#parameter)
 ( _[ErrorParameter](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorParameter.html)
 ([string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
)_ )

[severity](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#severity)
 ( _[ErrorSeverity](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorSeverity.html)
_ )

[subdomain](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#subdomain)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

category ( _[ErrorCategory](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorCategory.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

There are three categories of errors: request errors, application errors, and system errors.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#category)
 to learn which calls use category.

  

domain ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Name of the domain in which the error occurred.

**domain values:**

Marketplace

A business or validation error occurred in the service.

SOA

An exception occurred in the Service Oriented Architecture (SOA) framework.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#domain)
 to learn which calls use domain.

  

errorId ( _[long](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A unique code that identifies the particular error condition that occurred. Your application can use error codes as identifiers in your customized error-handling algorithms.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#errorId)
 to learn which calls use errorId.

  

exceptionId ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Unique identifier for an exception associated with an error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#exceptionId)
 to learn which calls use exceptionId.

  

message ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A detailed description of the condition that caused in the error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#message)
 to learn which calls use message.

  

parameter ( _[ErrorParameter](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorParameter.html)
 ([string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
)_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Various warning and error messages return one or more variables that contain contextual information about the error. This is often the field or value that triggered the error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#parameter)
 to learn which calls use parameter.

  

severity ( _[ErrorSeverity](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorSeverity.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Indicates whether the reported problem is fatal (an error) or is less- severe (a warning). Review the error message details for information on the cause.  
  
If the request fails and the application is the source of the error (for example, a required element is missing), update the application before you retry the request. If the problem is due to incorrect user data, alert the end-user to the problem and provide the means for them to correct the data. Once the problem in the application or data is resolved, re-send the request to eBay.  
  
If the source of the problem is on eBay's side, you can retry the request a reasonable number of times (eBay recommends you try the request twice). If the error persists, contact Developer Technical Support. Once the problem has been resolved, you can resend the request in its original form.  
  
If a warning occurs, warning information is returned in addition to the business data. Normally, you do not need to resend the request (as the original request was successful). However, depending on the cause of the warning, you might need to contact the end user, or eBay, to effect a long term solution to the problem.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#severity)
 to learn which calls use severity.

  

subdomain ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Name of the subdomain in which the error occurred.

**subdomain values:**

MarketplaceCatalog

The error is specific to the Marketplace Catalog subdomain.

MarketplaceCommon

The error is common to all Marketplace services.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#subdomain)
 to learn which calls use subdomain.

---

# InquirySummaryType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

InquirySummaryType
==================

This type is used to provide a summary of information on each INR inquiry that is returned in the **GET /post-order/v2/inquiry/search** response.

Type that uses InquirySummaryType:

[InquirySearchResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySearchResponse.html)

Call that uses InquirySummaryType:

[/post-order/v2/inquiry/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry_search__get.html)

Fields
------

[buyer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#buyer)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[claimAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#claimAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

[creationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#creationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[inquiryId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#inquiryId)
 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

[inquiryStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#inquiryStatusEnum)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[itemId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#itemId)
 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

[lastModifiedDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#lastModifiedDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[respondByDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#respondByDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[seller](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#seller)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[transactionId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquirySummaryType.html#transactionId)
 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

  
  

buyer ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The eBay user ID of the buyer involved in the INR inquiry.  
  
**Note:** Effective September 26, 2025, select developers will no longer receive username data for U.S. users through this field. Instead, an immutable user ID will be returned in its place. For more information, please refer to [Data Handling Compliance](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//api-docs/static/data-handling-update.html)
.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#buyer)
 to learn which calls use buyer.

  

claimAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container displays the dollar value of the order line item associated with the INR inquiry, which is generally the amount of the refund that the buyer will receive if the buyer is looking for a refund, or if the seller voluntarily refunds the buyer.  
  
**Note:** There's a possibility that this value can be inaccurate if the buyer passed in an inaccurate value into the **claimQuantity** field when the INR inquiry was created.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#claimAmount)
 to learn which calls use claimAmount.

  

creationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This timestamp indicates when the INR inquiry was created by the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#creationDate)
 to learn which calls use creationDate.

  

inquiryId ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique identifier of the INR inquiry.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#inquiryId)
 to learn which calls use inquiryId.

  

inquiryStatusEnum ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the current status of the INR inquiry.  
  
If the value is `WAITING_SELLER_RESPONSE` or `WAITING_BUYER_RESPONSE`, the seller or buyer, respectively, should make sure they respond no later than the date and time found in the **respondByDate** container.  
  
The list of enumeration values that may be returned in this field are defined in the [InquiryStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/types/InquiryStatusEnum.html)
 type definition.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#inquiryStatusEnum)
 to learn which calls use inquiryStatusEnum.

  

itemId ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique identifier of the eBay listing where the line item was purchased. This field is used in conjunction with the **transactionId** field to identify a line item that has not been received by the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#itemId)
 to learn which calls use itemId.

  

lastModifiedDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This timestamp indicates when the last action was performed on the INR inquiry.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#lastModifiedDate)
 to learn which calls use lastModifiedDate.

  

respondByDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This timestamp indicates the deadline for when a buyer or a seller must perform the next action on the INR inquiry. This field will only be returned if there is a deadline associated with the next action.  
  
This 'deadline' will apply to the seller if the **inquiryStatusEnum** value is `WAITING_SELLER_RESPONSE`, or will apply to the buyer if the **inquiryStatusEnum** value is `WAITING_BUYER_RESPONSE`.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#respondByDate)
 to learn which calls use respondByDate.

  

seller ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The eBay user ID of the seller involved in the INR inquiry.  
  
**Note:** Effective September 26, 2025, select developers will no longer receive username data for U.S. users through this field. Instead, an immutable user ID will be returned in its place. For more information, please refer to [Data Handling Compliance](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//api-docs/static/data-handling-update.html)
.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#seller)
 to learn which calls use seller.

  

transactionId ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique identifier of the purchase transaction. This value is created right when the buyer is committed to buying the item, whether that buyer uses a 'Buy it Now' capability, is the winning bidder of an auction, or the buyer's Best Offer is accepted by the seller. This field is used in conjunction with the **itemId** field to identify a line item that has not been received by the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#transactionId)
 to learn which calls use transactionId.

---

# PropertyNameValue - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)

*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

PropertyNameValue
=================

Container type for property name value pairs.

Types that use PropertyNameValue:

[GetProductSearchValuesResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/GetProductSearchValuesResponse.html)
, [PropertyNameValue](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/PropertyNameValue.html)

Calls that use PropertyNameValue:

[getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

------

childPropertyNameValue ( _[PropertyNameValue](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/PropertyNameValue.html)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Property name value pairs that are children to other properties. Only returned when the response is returned as a propertyValuesTree.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#childPropertyNameValue)
 to learn which calls use childPropertyNameValue.

 to learn which calls use propertyName.

 to learn which calls use value.

# FreightShipping - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

FreightShipping
===============

Type defining the **freightShipping** container, which consists of details related to freight shipping. This container is required to be set in a shipping policy if the seller offers freight shipping.

Type that uses FreightShipping:

[ShippingPolicyInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyInfo.html)

Calls that use FreightShipping:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Fields
------

[commodityType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/FreightShipping.html#commodityType)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[destPickupInside](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/FreightShipping.html#destPickupInside)
 ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ )

[destPickupLocationType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/FreightShipping.html#destPickupLocationType)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[freightShippingClass](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/FreightShipping.html#freightShippingClass)
 ( _[double](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#double)
_ )

[originPickupInside](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/FreightShipping.html#originPickupInside)
 ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ )

[originPickupLocationType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/FreightShipping.html#originPickupLocationType)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[packagingHelpRequired](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/FreightShipping.html#packagingHelpRequired)
 ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ )

  
  

commodityType ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A string value classifying the freight item to be shipped. Valid values for this field include:

*   MACHINERY\_USED
*   MACHINERY\_NEW
*   FRAGILE\_HOUSEHOLD\_USED
*   FRAGILE\_HOUSEHOLD\_NEW
*   NON\_FRAGILE\_HOUSEHOLD\_USED
*   NON\_FRAGILE\_HOUSEHOLD\_NEW
*   RESTAURANT\_EQUIPMENT\_USED
*   RESTAURANT\_EQUIPMENT\_NEW
*   COMPUTER\_ELECTRONICS\_USED
*   COMPUTER\_ELECTRONICS\_NEW
*   VENDING\_MACHINE\_USED
*   VENDING\_MACHINE\_NEW
*   MOTORCYCLE\_USED
*   MOTORCYCLE\_NEW

This is a required field if freight shipping is included in the shipping policy.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#commodityType)
 to learn which calls use commodityType.

  

destPickupInside ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Flag indicating if the freight item will be dropped off inside or outside of the delivery location. If this value is 'true', the item will be dropped off inside of the delivery location, and if 'false', the item will be dropped off outside of the delivery location.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#destPickupInside)
 to learn which calls use destPickupInside.

  

destPickupLocationType ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

String value indicating whether the freight item is being delivered to a residence or to a commercial location. Valid values are 'Residential' and 'Commercial'.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#destPickupLocationType)
 to learn which calls use destPickupLocationType.

  

freightShippingClass ( _[double](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#double)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Value indicating the shipping class of the freight item. The shipping class corresponds to the weight of the item (in pounds). Valid values include '50.0', '55.0', '60.0', '65.0', '70.0', '77.5', '85.0', '92.5', '100.0', '110.0', '125.0', '150.0', '175.0', '200.0', '225.0', '250.0', '300.0', '400.0', and '500.0'.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#freightShippingClass)
 to learn which calls use freightShippingClass.

  

originPickupInside ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Flag indicating if the freight item will be picked up inside or outside of the pickup location. If this value is 'true', the item will be picked up inside of the pickup location, and if 'false', the item will be picked up outside of the pickup location.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#originPickupInside)
 to learn which calls use originPickupInside.

  

originPickupLocationType ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

String value indicating whether the freight item is being picked up from a residence or from a commercial location. Valid values are 'Residential' and 'Commercial'.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#originPickupLocationType)
 to learn which calls use originPickupLocationType.

  

packagingHelpRequired ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Flag indicating if help is required to pick up the freight item. If this value is 'true', help picking up the item is required, and if 'false', help picking up the item is not required.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#packagingHelpRequired)
 to learn which calls use packagingHelpRequired.

---

# FileStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

FileStatusEnum
==============

This enumerated type contains the possible states that a return-associated file can be in.

Type that uses FileStatusEnum:

[ReturnFileType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of FileStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/files GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_files__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| CREATED | This enumeration value indicates that the file has been created, but has yet to be associated with a return request or draft. |
| DELETED | This enumeration value indicates that the file has been removed, or unassociated with a return request or draft. |
| PUBLISHED | This enumeration value indicates that the file has been associated with a return request or draft. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates that the status of the file is unknown. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# UserPartyEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

UserPartyEnum
=============

This enumerated type lists the types of users who can possibly perform an action on an INR inquiry or a case.

Types that use UserPartyEnum:

[CaseDetailsResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetailsResponse.html)
, [ClaimHistoryEventModel](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ClaimHistoryEventModel.html)
, [InquiryDetailsResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetailsResponse.html)

Calls that use one or more values of UserPartyEnum:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| BUYER | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action is the buyer. |
| CSR | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action is an eBay customer support representative. |
| SELLER | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action is the seller. |
| SYSTEM | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action is the eBay system. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action cannot be determined. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# PaginationOutput - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

PaginationOutput
================

Shows the pagination data for the item search. Child elements include the page number returned, the maximum entries returned per page, the total number of pages that can be returned, and the total number of entries that match the search criteria.

Types that use PaginationOutput:

[GetProductCompatiblitiesResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/GetProductCompatiblitiesResponse.html)
, [ProductResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductResponse.html)

Calls that use PaginationOutput:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)

Fields
------

entriesPerPage ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The maximum number of items that can be returned in the response. This number is always equal to the value input for paginationInput.entriesPerPage.  
  
The end of the result set has been reached if the number specified for entriesPerPage is greater than the number of items found on the specified pageNumber. In this case, there will be fewer items returned than the number specified in entriesPerPage. This can be determined by comparing the entriesPerPage value with the value returned in the count attribute for the searchResult field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#entriesPerPage)
 to learn which calls use entriesPerPage.

  

pageNumber ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The subset of item data returned in the current response. Search results are divided into sets, or "pages," of item data. The number of pages is equal to the total number of entries matching the search criteria divided by the value specified for entriesPerPage in the request. The response for a request contains one "page" of data.  
  
This returned value indicates the page number of data returned (a subset of the complete result set). If this field contains 1, the response contains the first page of item data (the default). If the value returned in totalEntries is less than the value for entriesPerPage, pageNumber returns 1 and the response contains the entire result set.  
  
The value of pageNumber is normally equal to the value input for paginationInput.pageNumber. However, if the number input for pageNumber is greater than the total possible pages of output, eBay returns the last page of item data in the result set, and the value for pageNumber is set to the respective (last) page number.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#pageNumber)
 to learn which calls use pageNumber.

  

totalEntries ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The total number of entries found that match the search criteria in your request. Depending on the input value for entriesPerPage, the response might include only a portion (a page) of the entire result set. A value of "0" is returned if eBay does not find any entries that match the search criteria.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#totalEntries)
 to learn which calls use totalEntries.

  

totalPages ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The total number of pages of data that could be returned by repeated search requests. Note that if you modify the value of inputPagination.entriesPerPage in a request, the value output for totalPages will change. A value of "0" is returned if eBay does not find any entries that match the search criteria.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#totalPages)
 to learn which calls use totalPages.

---

# ShippingPolicyInfo - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ShippingPolicyInfo
==================

Type defining the **shippingPolicyInfo** container, which consists of detailed information for a seller's shipping policy.

Type that uses ShippingPolicyInfo:

[ShippingPolicyProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyProfile.html)

Calls that use ShippingPolicyInfo:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Fields
------

[shipToLocations](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyInfo.html#shipToLocations)
