# MarketplaceIdEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

MarketplaceIdEnum
=================

This enumerated type defines the unique eBay-assigned IDs for each of the eBay marketplaces.

Types that use MarketplaceIdEnum:

[CancelDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/CancelDetail.html)
, [CancelSummary](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/CancelSummary.html)
, [ReturnDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnDetailType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of MarketplaceIdEnum:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| EBAY\_AT | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Austria (https://www.ebay.at). |
| EBAY\_AU | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Australia (https://www.ebay.com.au). |
| EBAY\_BE | This enumeration value indicates the French version of the eBay Belgium marketplace. Although the category taxonomy and inventory are more or less identiical, there are technically two eBay marketplaces in Belgium - a French version at https://www.befr.ebay.be/, and a Dutch version at https://www.benl.ebay.be/. There is no enumeration value that directly represents the Dutch version of the eBay Belgium marketplace, but a user can use the **Accept-Language** HTTP header and set its value to `nl-BE` to get applicable response fields returned in Dutch instead of French. |
| EBAY\_CA | This enumeration value indicates the English version of the eBay Canada marketplace. Although the category taxonomy and inventory are more or less identiical, there are technically two eBay marketplaces in Canada - an English version at https://www.ebay.ca/, and a French version at https://www.cafr.ebay.ca/. There is no enumeration value that directly represents the French version of the eBay Canada marketplace, but a user can use the **Accept-Language** HTTP header and set its value to `fr-CA` to get applicable response fields returned in French instead of English. |
| EBAY\_CH | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Switzerland (https://www.ebay.ch). |
| EBAY\_CN | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_CZ | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_DE | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Germany (https://www.ebay.de). |
| EBAY\_DK | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_ES | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Spain (https://www.ebay.es). |
| EBAY\_FI | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_FR | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for France (https://www.ebay.fr). |
| EBAY\_GB | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for the UK (https://www.ebay.co.uk). |
| EBAY\_GR | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_HALF\_US | This enumeration is deprecated since the Half.com site no longer exists. |
| EBAY\_HK | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Hong Kong (https://www.ebay.com.hk). |
| EBAY\_HU | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_ID | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_IE | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Ireland (https://www.ebay.ie). |
| EBAY\_IL | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_IN | This enumeration value is not currently applicable, as there is no longer an eBay marketplace in India. |
| EBAY\_IT | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Italy (https://www.ebay.it). |
| EBAY\_JP | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_MOTORS\_US | This enumeration value indicates the parent category for "Auto Parts and Vehicles" on the EBAY\_US marketplace (https://www.ebay.com/motors). |
| EBAY\_MY | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Malaysia (https://www.ebay.com.my). |
| EBAY\_NL | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for the Netherlands (https://www.ebay.nl). |
| EBAY\_NO | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_NZ | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_PE | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_PH | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for the Philippines (https://www.ebay.ph). |
| EBAY\_PL | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Poland (https://www.ebay.pl). |
| EBAY\_PR | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_PT | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_RU | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_SE | Reserved for future use. |
| EBAY\_SG | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Singapore (https://www.ebay.com.sg). |
| EBAY\_TH | This enumeration value is not currently applicable, as there is no longer an eBay marketplace in Thailand. |
| EBAY\_TW | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for Taiwan (https://www.ebay.com.tw). |
| EBAY\_US | This enumeration value indicates the eBay marketplace for the United States (https://www.ebay.com). |
| EBAY\_VN | This enumeration value is not currently applicable, as there is no longer an eBay marketplace in Vietnam. |
| EBAY\_ZA | Reserved for future use. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ReturnResponseDueType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnResponseDueType
=====================

This type is used to provide the type of response due from the buyer or seller on a return request, and when this response is due.

Type that uses ReturnResponseDueType:

[ReturnSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnSummaryType.html)

Calls that use ReturnResponseDueType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

activityDue ( _[ActivityOptionEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActivityOptionEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value informs the buyer or seller what the next action is to move the return request to the next stage.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#activityDue)
 to learn which calls use activityDue.

  

respondByDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the deadline for when a buyer or seller must perform the next action (indicated in **activityDue** field) on the return request. This field will only be returned if there is a deadline associated with the next action.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#respondByDate)
 to learn which calls use respondByDate.

---

# ReturnReasonTypeEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnReasonTypeEnum
====================

This enumeration type lists the broad categories about why a buyer opens a return request.

Type that uses ReturnReasonTypeEnum:

[ReturnCreationInfoType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnCreationInfoType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ReturnReasonTypeEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| CANCEL | This enumeration value is returned if a cancellation request was made by the buyer after the item was already shipped by the seller. |
| INSTORE | This enumeration value is returned if the return item was part of an In-Store Pickup or a Click and Collect order, and the buyer is returning the item to a local brick-and-mortar store. |
| REMORSE | This enumeration value is returned if the buyer is returning the item for a _buyer's remorse_ reason, which means that there is nothing actually wrong with the item. |
| SNAD | This enumeration value is returned if the buyer found the item to be Significantly Not As Described, so the buyer wants to return the item. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value is returned if the return reason category is not known. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ReturnShippingCostType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnShippingCostType
======================

This enumerated type lists the types of shipping costs, including the cost of the shipping label, shipping insurance, and certified shipping.

Type that uses ReturnShippingCostType:

[ItemizedReturnShippingCostType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ItemizedReturnShippingCostType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ReturnShippingCostType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| INSURANCE | This enumeration value indicates that the shipping cost is the price of shipping insurance to ship the return item. |
| LABEL\_COST | This enumeration value indicates that the shipping cost is the price of the shipping label for the return item. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that the shipping cost is not classified or is not known. |
| SIGNATURE\_CONFIRMATION\_COST | This enumeration value indicates that the shipping cost is the price to ship the return item using certified shipping. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# AppealGrantStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

AppealGrantStatusEnum
=====================

This enumeration type contains the possible outcomes of a case decision that has been appealed by the buyer or seller.

Type that uses AppealGrantStatusEnum:

[AppealDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/AppealDetails.html)

Calls that use one or more values of AppealGrantStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| DENIED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer's or seller's appeal was denied. |
| GRANTED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer's or seller's appeal was granted. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that the outcome of the buyer's or seller's appeal is unknown. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# RefundFundingType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

RefundFundingType
=================

This type is used to provide more information about the seller's funding source used for a buyer's refund in the case of a returned item or order cancellation.

Types that use RefundFundingType:

[ActualRefundDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetail.html)
, [ActualRefundDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html)

Calls that use RefundFundingType:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

amount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the amount debited/charged to the seller's funding source for the buyer refund.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#amount)
 to learn which calls use amount.

  

date ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the seller's funding source was debited/charged for the buyer refund.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#date)
 to learn which calls use date.

  

fundingSource ( _[FundingSourceType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FundingSourceType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides information about the seller's funding source for the buyer refund. A funding source can be available seller payout funds, a bank account on file, or a credit card on file.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fundingSource)
 to learn which calls use fundingSource.

  

fundingStatus ( _[FundingStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FundingStatusEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The enumeration value returned in this field indicates if the debit/charge to the funding source was successful or not.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fundingStatus)
 to learn which calls use fundingStatus.

---

# FileStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

FileStatusEnum
==============

This enumerated type contains the possible states that a return-associated file can be in.

Type that uses FileStatusEnum:

[ReturnFileType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of FileStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/files GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_files__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| CREATED | This enumeration value indicates that the file has been created, but has yet to be associated with a return request or draft. |
| DELETED | This enumeration value indicates that the file has been removed, or unassociated with a return request or draft. |
| PUBLISHED | This enumeration value indicates that the file has been associated with a return request or draft. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates that the status of the file is unknown. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# IsoCurrencyCode - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)

IsoCurrencyCode ( [token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
 )
=======================================================================================================================

This enumeration type defines the values that can be set as a currencyId attribute for Business Policies Management elements that use **Amount** type, such as **depositDetails.depositAmount** in payment policies, or **shippingServiceCost** in shipping policies.

Type that uses IsoCurrencyCode:

Calls that use one or more values of IsoCurrencyCode:

, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |

| CHF | Swiss Franc; country - Switzerland; ISO currency code - 756 |
| CNY | Chinese Yuan Renminbi; country - China; ISO currency code - 156 |

| SGD | Singapore Dollar; country - Singapore; ISO currency code - 702 |
| TWD | New Taiwan Dollar; country - Taiwan; ISO currency code - 901 |
| USD | U.S. Dollar; country - United States; ISO currency code - 840 |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

# MoneyMovementEntry - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

MoneyMovementEntry
==================

This type is used to provide details on every monetary transaction that occurs in regards to an Item Not Received inquiry or eBay case.

Type that uses MoneyMovementEntry:

[MoneyMovementEntries](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/MoneyMovementEntries.html)

Calls that use MoneyMovementEntry:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

[amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/MoneyMovementEntry.html#amount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

_ )

_ )

 ( _[FundingStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FundingStatusEnum.html)
_ )

amount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the amount of the monetary transaction.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#amount)
 to learn which calls use amount.

  

date ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the date/time of the monetary transaction.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#date)
 to learn which calls use date.

  

fundingSource ( _[FundingSource](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FundingSource.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides details about the seller's funding source to issue a buyer refund, such as available seller payout funds, a bank account, or a credit card on file. This container is only applicable and returned if the monetary transaction is related to a buyer refund.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fundingSource)
 to learn which calls use fundingSource.

  

fundingStatus ( _[FundingStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FundingStatusEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates if eBay was able to successfully 'fund' a refund to the buyer using the seller's preferred method of payment for buyer refunds. This field is only applicable and returned if the monetary transaction is related to a buyer refund.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fundingStatus)
 to learn which calls use fundingStatus.

  

type ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value indicates the type of monetary transaction, such as a refund.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#type)
 to learn which calls use type.

# PaymentProviderEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

PaymentProviderEnum
===================

This token type lists the external payment system used for the monetary transaction.

Type that uses PaymentProviderEnum:

[MoneyMovementDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/MoneyMovementDetailType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of PaymentProviderEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| PAYPAL | **THIS VALUE IS DEPRECATED**. |
| UNKNOWN | This value indicates that the payment system used for the monetary transaction is unknown. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# StatusType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

StatusType
==========

This enumerated type lists the values that can be returned in the responses of the **GET /post-order/v2/return/check\_eligibility** and **POST /post-order/v2/return** methods. See the enumeration descriptions for more information about what these values indicate based on which response these values are returned in.

Type that uses StatusType:

[EligibilityResultType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/EligibilityResultType.html)

Call that uses one or more values of StatusType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| FAILED | If this value is returned in the **GET /post-order/v2/return/check\_eligibility** or **POST /post-order/v2/return** responses, it means that the return eligibility check or the creation of return request failed. This generally indicates that there was either an issue with the request payload or there was a system error. |
| SELLER\_APPROVAL | If this value is returned in the **GET /post-order/v2/return/check\_eligibility** or **POST /post-order/v2/return** responses, it indicates that the buyer is eligible to create a return request for the order line item, but the return request will have to be accepted by the seller. |
| SUCCESS | If this value is returned in the **GET /post-order/v2/return/check\_eligibility** response, it indicates that the buyer is eligible to create a return request for the order line item. If this value is returned in the **POST /post-order/v2/return** response, it indicates that the return request for the order line item was created successfully. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value may get returned in the **GET /post-order/v2/return/check\_eligibility** or **POST /post-order/v2/return** responses if the status of the return eligibility check or the creation of the return request is unknown. |
| WARNING | If this value is returned in the **GET /post-order/v2/return/check\_eligibility** or **POST /post-order/v2/return** responses, it means that the return eligibility check or the creation of return request succeeded, but with a warning. The user should check for a **eligibilityErrorDetail** container to possibly get more information about the warning |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ReturnStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnStatusEnum
================

This enumeration type lists the possible statuses for a return request.

Type that uses ReturnStatusEnum:

[ReturnSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnSummaryType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ReturnStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| CLOSED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request has been closed |
| ESCALATED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request has been escalated to a return case. |
| ITEM\_DELIVERED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request item has been delivered to the seller. |
| ITEM\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request item has been shipped by the buyer. |
| LESS\_THAN\_A\_FULL\_REFUND\_ISSUED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial buyer refund was issued by the seller. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_DECLINED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has declined the partial refund amount that the seller has proposed. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the partial refund operation has failed. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial refund to the buyer has been initiated by the seller. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial refund has been requested by the buyer. |
| READY\_FOR\_SHIPPING | This enumeration value indicates that the return request item is ready to be shipped back by the buyer to the seller. |
| REPLACED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has acknowledged the receipt of the replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_CLOSED | This enumeration value indicates that the replacement item has been received and the return request has been closed.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_DELIVERED | This enumeration value indicates that the replacement item has been delivered to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_LABEL\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has requested a shipping label for the replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has requested a replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the replacement item has been shipped to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_STARTED | This enumeration value indicates that the process for the seller to send a replacement item to the buyer has started. |
| REPLACEMENT\_WAITING\_FOR\_RMA | This enumeration value indicates that the seller still needs to provide a Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) number for the replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| RETURN\_LABEL\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has requested a return shipping label. |
| RETURN\_REJECTED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer's return request has been rejected by the seller. |
| RETURN\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that a return request has been initiated by the buyer. |
| RETURN\_REQUESTED\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that a return request has been initiated by the buyer, but the system has timed out. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates that the current status of the return request is unknown. |
| WAITING\_FOR\_RETURN\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the return request is currently waiting for an eBay return shipping label to be available. |
| WAITING\_FOR\_RMA | This enumeration value indicates that the return request is currently waiting for the seller to provide a Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) number to the buyer. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# NoSellerUploadLabelReasonEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

NoSellerUploadLabelReasonEnum
=============================

This enumerated type lists the possible values that a seller can provide in the **noLabelReason** field of the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label** to indicate the reason why a shipping label is not being supplied to the buyer.

Type that uses NoSellerUploadLabelReasonEnum:

[ProvideLabelRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ProvideLabelRequest.html)

Call that uses one or more values of NoSellerUploadLabelReasonEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_add_shipping_label__post.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| ITEM\_TOO\_LARGE | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if the item is too large to be shipped using a return shipping label. Many shipping carriers have a weight limit. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if there is another reason why the seller is not providing a return shipping label to the buyer. If this value is used, it is good idea if the seller includes the **comments** container of the request to provide more details on the reason why no shipping label is being provided. |
| REIMBURSE | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if the seller will not provide a return shipping label to the buyer, but will reimburse the buyer for the return shipping cost. |
| RETURN\_TO\_INTL\_LOCATION | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if a return shipping label is not being provided to the buyer because the return address is located in another country. |
| RETURN\_TO\_STORE | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if the seller is asking the buyer to return the item to the store. This value is applicable for In-Store Pickup and Click and Collect orders. |
| WORK\_WITH\_BUYER | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if the seller will work with the buyer to determine how return shipping costs will be handled. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ScanDetailType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ScanDetailType
==============

This type is used by the **scanHistory** container that is returned in the **GET /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/tracking** response to provide details on each time a shipment is scanned by the shipping carrier. The **scanHistory** container will be returned if shipment tracking is active for the order line item, and at least one scan has been made on the package.

Type that uses ScanDetailType:

[GetTrackingHistoryResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/GetTrackingHistoryResponse.html)

Call that uses ScanDetailType:

[/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/tracking GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_tracking__get.html)

Fields
------

[eventCity](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ScanDetailType.html#eventCity)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[eventCode](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ScanDetailType.html#eventCode)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[eventDesc](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ScanDetailType.html#eventDesc)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[eventPostalCode](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ScanDetailType.html#eventPostalCode)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[eventStateOrProvince](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ScanDetailType.html#eventStateOrProvince)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[eventStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ScanDetailType.html#eventStatus)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[eventTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ScanDetailType.html#eventTime)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

  
  

eventCity ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value is the city in which the shipment event occurred. This field is always returned with the **scanHistory** container.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eventCity)
 to learn which calls use eventCity.

  

eventCode ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value represents the shipment event that occurred. This field is returned if available.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eventCode)
 to learn which calls use eventCode.

  

eventDesc ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field contains the description of the shipment event that occurred. This field is returned if available.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eventDesc)
 to learn which calls use eventDesc.

  

eventPostalCode ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value is the postal code for the scan location. This field is always returned with the **scanHistory** container.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eventPostalCode)
 to learn which calls use eventPostalCode.

  

eventStateOrProvince ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value is the state or province in which the shipment event occurred. This field is always returned with the **scanHistory** container.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eventStateOrProvince)
 to learn which calls use eventStateOrProvince.

  

eventStatus ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field contains shows the latest delivery status of the shipment based on the event (scan) that just occurred. This field is always returned with the **scanHistory** container.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eventStatus)
 to learn which calls use eventStatus.

  

eventTime ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the date and time when the shipment event occurred. This field is always returned with the **scanHistory** container.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eventTime)
 to learn which calls use eventTime.

# ShippingPolicyProfile - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ShippingPolicyProfile ( [SellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html)
 )
=================================================================================================================================

Type defining the **shippingPolicyProfile** container, which is the container used to define one shipping policy for a seller.

Types that use ShippingPolicyProfile:

[AddSellerProfileRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/AddSellerProfileRequest.html)
, [AddSellerProfileResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/AddSellerProfileResponse.html)
, [SetSellerProfileRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SetSellerProfileRequest.html)
, [SetSellerProfileResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SetSellerProfileResponse.html)
, [ShippingPolicyProfileList](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyProfileList.html)

Calls that use ShippingPolicyProfile:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Field
-----

shippingPolicyInfo ( _[ShippingPolicyInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyInfo.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container consists of detailed shipping information for a seller's shipping policy. This container is conditionally required if the caller is creating a new shipping policy or modifying an existing shipping policy.  
  
This container is returned by **getSellerProfiles** if one or more shipping policies match the input criteria in the call request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#shippingPolicyInfo)
 to learn which calls use shippingPolicyInfo.

---

# InternationalReturnPolicyInfo - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

InternationalReturnPolicyInfo
=============================

Populate this complex object if your international return policy differs from your domestic return policy (by default, international return policies inherit their settings from the settings in the domestic return policy).  
  
For example, you might want to offer free returns for domestic shipments, but have a return policy where the buyer pays for returns from non-domestic regions.

Type that uses InternationalReturnPolicyInfo:

[ReturnPolicyProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyProfile.html)

Calls that use InternationalReturnPolicyInfo:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Fields
------

refundOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field indicates how the seller compensates buyers for [remorse returns](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/guides/features-guide/default.html#development/post-order-returns.html#return-reasons)
. You must set this value to `MoneyBack` for all eBay marketplaces except for the US marketplace. This value is required for international returns when **ReturnsAcceptedOption=ReturnsAccepted**.  
  
Depending on the Seller's return policy and the specifics of a return, either the buyer or the seller can be responsible for the return shipping costs.  
  
On `EBAY_US`: You can set this value to either `MoneyBack` or `MoneyBackOrReplacement`. If a seller has the depth of inventory to support an exchange for an identical item, you can set `MoneyBackOrReplacement`. Otherwise, eBay recommends you set this value to its default value, `MoneyBack`.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#refundOption)
 to learn which calls use refundOption.

  

returnsAcceptedOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This optional field indicates whether or not the seller accepts international returns (returns that need to be shipped via an international shipping service).  
  
Applicable values are `ReturnsAccepted` or `ReturnsNotAccepted`. When set to `ReturnsAccepted`, this option indicates the seller allows international returns. If the seller does not accept international returns, they can specify **ReturnsNotAccepted**.  
  
On the eBay DE, IE, and UK, registered business sellers must accept returns for fixed-price items (including auction items with Buy It Now and any other fixed price formats) when the category requires a return policy. On some European sites, such as eBay Germany (DE), registered business sellers are required to accept returns. Use the Trading call **GetUser** to determine the status of an eBay business seller in DE, IE, and UK. Review the **User.SellerInfo.SellerBusinessType** field in the response.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#returnsAcceptedOption)
 to learn which calls use returnsAcceptedOption.

  

returnsWithinOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Specifies the amount of time the buyer has to return an item. The return period begins when the item is marked "delivered" at the buyer's specified ship-to location. Most marketplaces and categories support 30-day and 60-day return periods. eBay sites often set 30-days as the default value for this field and sellers are obligated to honor the values that are set for a listing. This value is required for international returns when **ReturnsAcceptedOption=ReturnsAccepted**.  
  
**Applicable values:** Retrieve the values supported by a marketplace by calling **GeteBayDetails** with **DetailName** set to `ReturnPolicyDetails`, then review **ReturnPolicyDetails.ReturnsWithin.ReturnsWithinOption** in the response.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#returnsWithinOption)
 to learn which calls use returnsWithinOption.

  

shippingCostPaidByOption ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This option specifies either the buyer or the seller as the party who pays for return shipping charges for international returns. Accepted values are `Buyer` or `Seller`. This value is required for international returns when **ReturnsAcceptedOption=ReturnsAccepted**.  
  
Depending on the Seller's return policy and the specifics of a return, either the buyer or the seller can be responsible for the return shipping costs. Note that the seller is always responsible for return shipping costs for SNAD-related issues.  
  
Use **GeteBayDetails** (with **DetailName=ReturnPolicyDetails**) and review the returned **ReturnPolicyDetails.ShippingCostPaidBy** values to see the values supported by a marketplace.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#shippingCostPaidByOption)
 to learn which calls use shippingCostPaidByOption.

---

# PaymentProviderEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

PaymentProviderEnum
===================

This token type lists the external payment system used for the monetary transaction.

Type that uses PaymentProviderEnum:

[MoneyMovementDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/MoneyMovementDetailType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of PaymentProviderEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| PAYPAL | **THIS VALUE IS DEPRECATED**. |
| UNKNOWN | This value indicates that the payment system used for the monetary transaction is unknown. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# RelistStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

RelistStatusEnum
================

This enumerated type defines the different possible response values that can be returned to indicate the relisting status of an item that was involved in an order cancellation or return request.

Type that uses RelistStatusEnum:

[ItemDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ItemDetailType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of RelistStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the attempt to relist the item has failed. |
| RELIST\_ELIGIBLE | This enumeration value indicates that the item is eligible to be relisted automatically by eBay upon the successful cancellation of an order or once a returned item is marked as received by the seller. |
| SUCCESS | This enumeration value indicates that the item has been successfully relisted upon the successful cancellation of an order, or after a returned item has been marked as received by the seller. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

ReturnPolicyProfileList

Type defining the **returnPolicyProfileList** container, which consists of one or more return policies that match the input criteria in the **getSellerProfiles** request.

[GetSellerProfilesResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/GetSellerProfilesResponse.html)

[getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)

-----

ReturnPolicyProfile ( _[ReturnPolicyProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyProfile.html)
_ ) [\[1..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

 to learn which calls use ReturnPolicyProfile.

# FundingStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

FundingStatusEnum
=================

This enumerated type is used to indicate if eBay was able to successfully 'fund' a refund to the buyer using the seller's preferred method of payment for refunds.

Types that use FundingStatusEnum:

[MoneyMovementEntry](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/MoneyMovementEntry.html)
, [RefundFundingType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundFundingType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of FundingStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the refund was not successfully funded, which can mean there were insufficient funds in the account, or that the transaction failed for another reason. |
| FUNDED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer refund was successfully funded. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# StockPhotoURL - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

StockPhotoURL
=============

Thumbnail and standard stockphoto URLs.

Type that uses StockPhotoURL:

[Product](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html)

Calls that use StockPhotoURL:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)
, [getProductDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductDetails.html)

Fields
------

standard ( _[URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Fully qualified URL for a stock image (if any) that is associated with a catalog product. The URL is for the image eBay usually displays in product search results (usually 70px tall). It may be helpful to calculate the dimensions of the photo programmatically before displaying it. Only returned if a URL is available for the product.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#standard)
 to learn which calls use standard.

  

thumbnail ( _[URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Fully qualified URL for the thumbnail of a stock image (if any) that is associated with a catalog product. The URL is for the image eBay usually displays in product search results (usually 40px tall). It may be helpful to calculate the dimensions of the photo programmatically before displaying it. Only returned if a URL is available for the product.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#thumbnail)
 to learn which calls use thumbnail.

---

# TotalRefundAmountType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

TotalRefundAmountType
=====================

This type is used by the **buyerTotalRefund** and **sellerTotalRefund** containers, which provide the estimated and actual refund amounts due to the buyer.

Type that uses TotalRefundAmountType:

[ReturnSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnSummaryType.html)

Calls that use TotalRefundAmountType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

actualRefundAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the actual refund amount paid to the buyer. Is is only returned after a refund has been issued to the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#actualRefundAmount)
 to learn which calls use actualRefundAmount.

  

estimatedRefundAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the estimated refund amount expected to be paid to the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#estimatedRefundAmount)
 to learn which calls use estimatedRefundAmount.
