# AckValue - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

AckValue ( [string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
 )
========================================================================================================

Indicates whether the error is a severe error (causing the request to fail) or an informational error (a warning) that should be communicated to the user.

Type that uses AckValue:

[BaseServiceResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/BaseServiceResponse.html)

Calls that use one or more values of AckValue:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)
, [getProductDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductDetails.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| Failure | The request was processed successfully, but something occurred that may affect your application or the user. |
| PartialFailure | The request that triggered the error was processed successfully but with some warnings. |
| Success | The request that triggered the error was not processed successfully. When a serious application-level error occurs, the error is returned instead of the business data. |
| Warning | The request that triggered the error was processed successfully but with some warnings. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ShipmentTrackingDetailType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ShipmentTrackingDetailType
==========================

This type is used to show detailed shipment tracking information if an INR item is being shipped to the buyer, or if the buyer is returning an item to the seller.

Type that uses ShipmentTrackingDetailType:

[ClaimHistoryResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ClaimHistoryResponse.html)

Calls that use ShipmentTrackingDetailType:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

Fields
------

[carrier](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingDetailType.html#carrier)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[currentStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingDetailType.html#currentStatus)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[estimateFromDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingDetailType.html#estimateFromDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[estimateToDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingDetailType.html#estimateToDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[trackingNumber](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingDetailType.html#trackingNumber)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[trackingURL](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingDetailType.html#trackingURL)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

carrier ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The name of the shipping carrier handling the shipment. This field is generally always returned with the **shipmentTrackingDetails** container unless the tracking number has yet to be provided for the order line item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#carrier)
 to learn which calls use carrier.

  

currentStatus ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The current status of the shipment, such as delivered or still in-transit. This field is returned if it is known and available.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#currentStatus)
 to learn which calls use currentStatus.

  

estimateFromDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The date and time in this container is the earliest date that the shipment is estimated to be delivered, based on the shipping service being used, the stated handling time, and when the package was shipped. This container is returned if it is known and available.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#estimateFromDate)
 to learn which calls use estimateFromDate.

  

estimateToDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The date and time in this container is the latest date that the shipment is estimated to be delivered, based on the shipping service being used, the stated handling time, and when the package was shipped. If the package is not received by this date, the buyer or seller should contact their order partner to get status, and then ask eBay to step in if the problem cannot be resolved. This container is returned if it is known and available.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#estimateToDate)
 to learn which calls use estimateToDate.

  

trackingNumber ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A unique identifier used to track the shipment of the order line item. This field is generally always returned with the **shipmentTrackingDetails** container unless the tracking number has yet to be provided for the order line item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#trackingNumber)
 to learn which calls use trackingNumber.

  

trackingURL ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field provides the URL to the shipping carrier's shipment tracking page. This field is returned if available.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#trackingURL)
 to learn which calls use trackingURL.

---

# OrderType - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

OrderType ( [string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
 )
=========================================================================================================

Defines the order type as Ascending or Descending

Type that uses OrderType:

[SortOrder](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/SortOrder.html)

Calls that use one or more values of OrderType:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| Ascending | The results will be sorted by the specified attribute in ascending (low to high) order. |
| Descending | The results will be sorted by the specified attribute in descending (high to low) order. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# PaymentInfo - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

PaymentInfo
===========

Type defining the **PaymentInfo** container, which contains payment information related to the corresponding payment policy.

Type that uses PaymentInfo:

[PaymentProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentProfile.html)

Calls that use PaymentInfo:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Fields
------

[acceptedPaymentMethod](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentInfo.html#acceptedPaymentMethod)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[daysToFullPayment](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentInfo.html#daysToFullPayment)
 ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ )

[depositDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentInfo.html#depositDetails)
 ( _[DepositDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/DepositDetails.html)
_ )

[immediatePay](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentInfo.html#immediatePay)
 ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ )

[paymentInstructions](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentInfo.html#paymentInstructions)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[paypalEmailAddress](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentInfo.html#paypalEmailAddress)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

  
  

acceptedPaymentMethod ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

**Note:**This field applies only when the seller needs to specify one or more offline payment methods. eBay now manages the electronic payment options available to buyers to pay for the item. This field specifys one or more offline payment methods that will be accepted for payment that occurs off of eBay's platform. If you specify multiple **acceptedPaymentMethod** fields, the repeating fields must be contiguous. **Note:** Required or allowed payment methods vary by site and category. To retrieve a list of valid payment methods for your site and category, call **GetCategoryFeatures**, specifying 'PaymentMethods' as a **FeatureID** value in the call request, and then look for the **Category.PaymentMethod** values in the call response. In order for a buyer to make a full payment on an US or CA motor vehicle, at least one of the following **acceptedPaymentMethod** values must be specified:

*   CashOnPickup
*   LoanCheck
*   MOCC (money order or cashier's check)
*   PersonalCheck

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#acceptedPaymentMethod)
 to learn which calls use acceptedPaymentMethod.

  

daysToFullPayment ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This integer value indicates the number of days that a buyer has to make their full payment to the seller and close the remaining balance on a motor vehicle transaction. This container must be specified for motor vehicles listings. Valid values are '3', '7' (default), '10', and '14'.  
  
In order for a buyer to make a full payment on a US or CA motor vehicle, at least one of the following **acceptedPaymentMethod** values must be specified for the corresponding payment business policy:

*   CashOnPickup
*   LoanCheck
*   MOCC (money order or cashier's check)
*   PersonalCheck

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#daysToFullPayment)
 to learn which calls use daysToFullPayment.

  

depositDetails ( _[DepositDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/DepositDetails.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This value indicates the initial deposit amount required from the buyer in order to purchase a motor vehicle. This value can be as high as $2,000.00 if immediate payment is not required, and up to $500.00 if immediate payment is required. This container is only applicable if the **categoryGroup.name**field is set to 'MOTORS\_VEHICLE'.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#depositDetails)
 to learn which calls use depositDetails.

  

immediatePay ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field should be included and set to `true` if the seller wants to require immediate payment from the buyer for:

*   A fixed-price item
*   An auction item where the buyer is using the 'Buy it Now' option
*   A deposit for a motor vehicle listing

**Note:** In the Trading API calls that return the **AutoPay** field (**immediatePay** equivalent), be aware that the field's appearance in the output does not necessarily indicate that the listing qualifies for immediate payment, but only that the seller attempted to create (by including and setting **immediatePay** to 'true' in the payment policy) an immediate payment requirement.  
To successfully enable the immediate payment requirement, the seller must also perform the following actions through the API call:

*   seller must specify all related costs to the buyer, since the buyer will not be able to use the Buyer Request Total feature in an immediate payment listing; these costs include flat-rate shipping costs for each domestic and international shipping service offered, package handling costs, and any shipping surcharges;
*   seller must include and set the **shippingProfileDiscountInfo** container values if promotional shipping discounts will be used;

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#immediatePay)
 to learn which calls use immediatePay.

  

paymentInstructions ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

**Important:** DO NOT USE THIS FIELD. Payment instructions are no longer supported by payment business policies.

This free-form string field allows the seller to give payment instructions to the buyer. These instructions will appear on eBay's View Item and Checkout pages. This field allows 1000 characters.

It is recommended that the seller use this field for motor vehicles (eBay Motors US and CA) payment policies to clarify the specifics on the deposit (if required), pickup/delivery arrangements, and full payment details on the vehicle.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#paymentInstructions)
 to learn which calls use paymentInstructions.

  

paypalEmailAddress ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

**Important:** This field is deprecated. Do not use this field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#paypalEmailAddress)
 to learn which calls use paypalEmailAddress.

---

# ReturnStateEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnStateEnum
===============

This enumerated type lists the possible states for a return request. These state values are returned in the responses of the **GET /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}** and **POST /post-order/v2/return** methods.

Types that use ReturnStateEnum:

[ReturnResponseHistoryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnResponseHistoryType.html)
, [ReturnSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnSummaryType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ReturnStateEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| AUTO\_REFUND\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that the auto refund to the buyer has been initiated. |
| CLOSED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request has been closed. |
| INITIAL | This enumeration value indicates the return request is in the initial stages. |
| ITEM\_DELIVERED | This enumeration value indicates the return item has been received by the seller. |
| ITEM\_KEPT | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has decided to keep the item instead of returning it to the seller. This requires the seller's approval. |
| ITEM\_READY\_TO\_SHIP | This enumeration value indicates the return item is ready to be shipped back to the seller. |
| ITEM\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates the return item has been shipped by the buyer. |
| LESS\_THAN\_A\_FULL\_REFUND\_ISSUED | This enumeration value indicates that a full refund was expected, but only a partial refund has been issued. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_AS\_PAYOUT\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial refund to be funded by the seller's payout was initiated. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_DECLINED | This enumeration value indicates that the partial refund offer was declined. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the partial refund operation failed. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial refund was initiated. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_NON\_PAYPAL\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial refund was initiated. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUND\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial refund was requested. |
| PARTIAL\_REFUNDED | This enumeration value indicates that a partial refund was issued to the buyer. |
| PAYOUT\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that the payout to the seller has been initiated. |
| REFUND\_AS\_PAYOUT\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer's refund has been initiated through the seller's payout funds. |
| REFUND\_FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the refund transaction failed. |
| REFUND\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that a buyer refund was initiated. |
| REFUND\_SENT\_PENDING\_CONFIRMATION | This enumeration value indicates that a refund was sent to the buyer and it awaiting confirmation from the buyer that it was received. |
| REFUND\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the seller attempted a refund action to the buyer, but the refund action timed out. |
| REPLACED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has received a replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_CLOSED | This enumeration value indicates that the replacement item has been received and the return request has been closed.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_DELIVERED | This enumeration value indicates that the replacement item has been delivered to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_LABEL\_PENDING | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer is currently waiting for the shipping label of a replacement item to be generated. This value is only applicable if a replacement item is being sent to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_LABEL\_PENDING\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer is currently waiting for the shipping label of a replacement item to be generated, but the request timed out. This value is only applicable if a replacement item is being sent to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_LABEL\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has requested a shipping label for the replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_LABEL\_REQUESTED\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has requested a shipping label for the replacement item, but the request has timed out.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_OFFER\_PENDING | This enumeration value indicates that the seller is awaiting a response from the buyer in regards to a replacement offer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_REQUEST\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has requested a replacement item, but the request has timed out.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has requested a replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_RMA\_PENDING | This enumeration value indicates that the seller still needs to provide a Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) for the replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the replacement item has been sent to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_STARTED | This enumeration value indicates that the process for the seller to send a replacement item to the buyer has started.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| RETURN\_LABEL\_PENDING | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer is currently waiting for a return shipping label to be generated/provided. |
| RETURN\_LABEL\_PENDING\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer is currently waiting for a return shipping label to be generated, but the operation timed out. |
| RETURN\_LABEL\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that a return shipping label was requested by the buyer. |
| RETURN\_LABEL\_REQUESTED\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that a return shipping label request by the buyer timed out. |
| RETURN\_REJECTED | This enumeration value indicates that a return request was rejected by seller. |
| RETURN\_REQUEST\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that buyer attempted to initiate a return request, but the request timed out. |
| RETURN\_REQUESTED | This enumeration value indicates that a return request was initiated by the buyer, and is waiting for the seller's response. |
| RMA\_PENDING | This enumeration value indicates the return request is currently pending, awaiting for the seller to provide a Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) to the buyer. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates the current state of the return request is unknown. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# CloseReturnReasonEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

CloseReturnReasonEnum
=====================

This enumerated type lists all possible reasons why a return request is closed. A buyer would typically close a return request after they receive a refund or if they initiated the return request by mistake.

Type that uses CloseReturnReasonEnum:

[ReturnCloseInfoType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnCloseInfoType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of CloseReturnReasonEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| AUTO\_PARTIAL\_REFUNDED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed after eBay automatically issued a partial refund to the buyer. |
| AUTO\_REFUNDED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed after eBay automatically issued a full refund to the buyer. |
| BUYER\_ACCEPT\_LESS\_THAN\_FULL\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed after the buyer accepted a partial refund. |
| BUYER\_ACCEPT\_LESS\_THAN\_FULL\_REFUND\_AS\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed after the buyer accepted a partial refund as a payout. |
| BUYER\_CLOSED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed by the buyer. |
| BUYER\_NO\_ACTION | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because there was no evidence of activity from the buyer in the system. |
| BUYER\_PARTIAL\_REFUND\_AS\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the system issued a partial refund to the buyer. |
| BUYER\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the buyer was paid out. |
| BUYER\_PAYOUT\_FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the payout to the buyer failed. |
| BUYER\_REFUND\_AS\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the system paid out the refund amount to the buyer. |
| CHARGEBACK\_DISPUTE\_FILED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the buyer created a chargeback dispute. |
| ESCALATED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the buyer escalated the return request into a case. |
| FULL\_REFUNDED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed after the buyer received a full refund from the seller. |
| FULL\_REFUNDED\_BY\_DISPOSITION\_RULE | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the buyer was auto-refunded by eBay. |
| ITEM\_REJECTED | This enumeration value indicates that the returned item was rejected by eBay and no refund issued to buyer. |
| LVT\_IMMEDIATE\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the system issued an immediate refund. |
| MARK\_PARTIAL\_REFUND\_RECEIVED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the buyer marked the partial refund as received. |
| MARK\_REFUND\_RECEIVED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the buyer marked the full refund as received. |
| MARK\_REFUND\_RECEIVED\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the buyer attempted to mark the refund as received, but the operation timed out. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed due to an unknown reason. |
| PARTIALLY\_REFUNDED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed after the buyer received a mutually agreed upon partial refund. |
| REFUND\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the system timed out waiting for the seller to refund the buyer. |
| REPLACED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the seller offered and the buyer accepted the seller's offer of a replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REPLACEMENT\_PAYOUT\_RECOUP | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed after eBay paid out the seller for the auto-refund after the seller sent the buyer a replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| REQUEST\_DECLINED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the request was declined. |
| REQUEST\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the request timed out. |
| RETURN\_REJECTED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the request was rejected by seller, or possibly eBay if the order line item was not eligible for a return request. |
| SELLER\_INVOICED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the seller was invoiced so eBay could recoup the buyer refund money from seller. |
| SELLER\_PAYOUT\_FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the payout to the seller failed. |
| SHIPMENT\_TIMEOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the return request was closed because the system timed out waiting for return shipment information from the buyer. |
| STORE\_CREDIT\_REFUNDED | **THIS ENUMERATION VALUE IS DEPRECATED**. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# Amount - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Amount ( [double](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#double)
 )
================================================================================================================

Monetary amount. This type is used in several locations in payment policies and return policies.

Types that use Amount:

[DepositDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/DepositDetails.html)
, [Insurance](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/Insurance.html)
, [ShippingPolicyInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyInfo.html)
, [ShippingPolicyInfoService](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyInfoService.html)

Calls that use Amount:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Fields
------

This type has no fields.

Attribute
---------

currencyId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

This attribute identifies the currency in which the monetary amount is specified. For a full list of 3-digit ISO 4217 currency codes, see the Trading API's [CurrencyCodeType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/XML/docs/Reference/eBay/types/CurrencyCodeType.html)
.

---

# FilePurposeEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

FilePurposeEnum
===============

This enumerated type contains the list of purposes for which a file is uploaded and attached to a return request or draft.

Types that use FilePurposeEnum:

[ReturnFileType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html)
, [UploadFileRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/UploadFileRequest.html)

Calls that use one or more values of FilePurposeEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/file/upload POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_file_upload__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/files GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_files__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| ITEM\_RELATED | This enumeration value indicates that the file is intended to provide more information about the item. For example, in a case where the buyer and seller are in disagreement over the condition of the item, the buyer or seller may provide a photo of the item that visually shows the condition of the item. |
| LABEL\_RELATED | This enumeration value indicates that the file is intended to provide more information about a shipping label or shipment tracking information. For example, in a case where the buyer and seller are in disagreement over whether shipment tracking information has been provided or not, the buyer or seller may provide a photo of the shipping label itself, or they may provide a file that proves a package has been shipped or delivered. |
| REFUND\_RELATED | This enumeration value indicates that the file is intended to provide more information about a buyer refund. For example, in a case where the buyer is saying that they have not recieved their refund, the seller may provide a file that proves that a refund has been issued. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value should not be used in a Submit/Upload File request, but it is possible that this value may get returned in a response if the purpose of the file is not known. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ReturnPayoutRecoupInfoType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnPayoutRecoupInfoType
==========================

This type provides the amount of money that eBay needs to recoup from the seller to fund a buyer refund and the unique identifier of the seller payout that is associated with the purchase of the line item.

Type that uses ReturnPayoutRecoupInfoType:

[ReturnDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnDetailType.html)

Calls that use ReturnPayoutRecoupInfoType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

amountToRecoup ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the amount of money that eBay needs to recoup from the seller to fund a buyer refund.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#amountToRecoup)
 to learn which calls use amountToRecoup.

  

payoutId ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A unique eBay-assigned ID that identifies the seller payout that is associated with the purchase of the line item.  
  
The seller payout that is associated with the purchase of the line item will often by used as the funding source if payout funds are still available. The seller's funding source for a buyer refund is shown in the **fundingSource.type** field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#payoutId)
 to learn which calls use payoutId.

---

# ReturnPolicyProfile - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnPolicyProfile ( [SellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html)
 )
===============================================================================================================================

Type defining the **returnPolicyProfile** container, which is the container used to define one return policy for a seller.

Types that use ReturnPolicyProfile:

[AddSellerProfileRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/AddSellerProfileRequest.html)
, [AddSellerProfileResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/AddSellerProfileResponse.html)
, [ReturnPolicyProfileList](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyProfileList.html)
, [SetSellerProfileRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SetSellerProfileRequest.html)
, [SetSellerProfileResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SetSellerProfileResponse.html)

Calls that use ReturnPolicyProfile:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Fields
------

internationalReturnPolicyInfo ( _[InternationalReturnPolicyInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/InternationalReturnPolicyInfo.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container consists of detailed information on a seller's international return policy (returns that require an international shipping service to ship). This container is optional and allows for a seller to establish an international return policy that differs from their domestic return policy.  
  
This container is returned by **getSellerProfiles** if one or more return policies match the input criteria in the call request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#internationalReturnPolicyInfo)
 to learn which calls use internationalReturnPolicyInfo.

  

returnPolicyInfo ( _[ReturnPolicyInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyInfo.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container consists of detailed information on a seller's return policy. This container is conditionally required if the caller is creating a new return policy or modifying an existing return policy.  
  
This container is returned by **getSellerProfiles** if one or more return policies match the input criteria in the call request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#returnPolicyInfo)
 to learn which calls use returnPolicyInfo.

---

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

URIValue
========

This class holds a URL value

Type that uses URIValue:

[Value](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/Value.html)

Calls that use URIValue:

[getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

value ( _[anyURI](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#anyURI)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

As part of request, this will be the URL to download media file. As part of response, this will be the URL where the media file is stored on eBay.

 to learn which calls use value.

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)

OrderType ( [string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
 )
==================================================================================================================

\[NOT USED BY ANY CALLS\] Orphan type.

Type that uses OrderType:

[SortOrder](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/SortOrder.html)

Calls that use one or more values of OrderType:

[getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| Ascending | Sorts results in ascending (low to high) order. |
| Descending | Sorts results in descending (high to low) order. |

 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

# InquiryDetails - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

InquiryDetails
==============

Type used by the **inquiryDetails** container of the **GET /post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId}** response. The **inquiryDetails** container provides details about a specific INR inquiry that is identified through a path parameter in the request.

Type that uses InquiryDetails:

[InquiryDetailsResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetailsResponse.html)

Call that uses InquiryDetails:

[/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

Fields
------

[appealDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetails.html#appealDetails)
 ( _[AppealDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/AppealDetails.html)
_ )

[creationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetails.html#creationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[escalationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetails.html#escalationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[expirationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetails.html#expirationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[lastBuyerRespDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetails.html#lastBuyerRespDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[refundAmounts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetails.html#refundAmounts)
 ( _[RefundAmounts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundAmounts.html)
_ )

[refundChargeAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetails.html#refundChargeAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

[refundDeadlineDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetails.html#refundDeadlineDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[totalAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetails.html#totalAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

  
  

appealDetails ( _[AppealDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/AppealDetails.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container consists of information related to the appeal of a case decision. This container is only returned for an INR inquiry that was escalated into a case and the case decision was appealed by the buyer or seller.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#appealDetails)
 to learn which calls use appealDetails.

  

creationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the INR inquiry was created by the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#creationDate)
 to learn which calls use creationDate.

  

escalationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the INR inquiry was escalated to a case. This container is not returned if the inquiry was not escalated.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#escalationDate)
 to learn which calls use escalationDate.

  

expirationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the INR inquiry will naturally expire if no further action is taken.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#expirationDate)
 to learn which calls use expirationDate.

  

lastBuyerRespDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the buyer last responded to, or performed an action on the INR inquiry.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#lastBuyerRespDate)
 to learn which calls use lastBuyerRespDate.

  

refundAmounts ( _[RefundAmounts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundAmounts.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container consists of details related to the expected and actual buyer refund amounts related to an INR inquiry or case. This container is only returned if a refund is scheduled to, or has been issued to the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundAmounts)
 to learn which calls use refundAmounts.

  

refundChargeAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the total amount charged to the seller for this buyer refund. The value will be in the seller's default currency. This container is only applicable and is only returned if a refund has been issued to the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundChargeAmount)
 to learn which calls use refundChargeAmount.

  

refundDeadlineDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the seller is required to issue a refund for the INR inquiry. This container is only returned if the buyer is seeking a full refund and no longer wants the missing item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundDeadlineDate)
 to learn which calls use refundDeadlineDate.

  

totalAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the total cost of the order, including the item price and shipping fees. This will generally be the amount that the buyer will expect to receive if the buyer is seeking a full refund for the missing item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#totalAmount)
 to learn which calls use totalAmount.

---

# SortOrder - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

SortOrder
=========

Sort order input for the result set. Any of the searchable properties can be used for property name and order can be either Ascending or Descending.

Type that uses SortOrder:

[GetProductSearchValuesBaseRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/GetProductSearchValuesBaseRequest.html)

Calls that use SortOrder:

[getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

Fields
------

order ( _[OrderType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/OrderType.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Order of the sort, either Ascending or Descending.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#order)
 to learn which calls use order.

  

propertyName ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Output can be sorted by one or more (for bulk calls) property names. You can look up valid values using either the **getProductSearchNames** call or the **getCompatibilitySearchNames** call.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#propertyName)
 to learn which calls use propertyName.

---

# InquiryDetailsResponse - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

InquiryDetailsResponse
======================

The base response type for the **GET /post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId}** method. This type provides full details on an INR inquiry.

Call that uses InquiryDetailsResponse:

[/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

Fields
------

[claimAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetailsResponse.html#claimAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )
