# CategoryGroups - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

CategoryGroups
==============

Type defining the **categoryGroups** container, which contains one or more **categoryGroup** containers.

Types that use CategoryGroups:

[PaymentProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentProfile.html)
, [ReturnPolicyProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyProfile.html)
, [ShippingPolicyProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyProfile.html)

Calls that use CategoryGroups:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Field
-----

categoryGroup ( _[CategoryGroup](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/CategoryGroup.html)
_ ) [\[1..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container indicates the category group to which the payment policy, return policy, or shipping policy applies to. The only two valid category groups are 'MOTORS\_VEHICLE' (for motor vehicle listings) and 'ALL' (for non-motor vehicle listings). The 'MOTORS\_VEHICLE' category group is not valid for return policies, as return policies cannot be used with motor vehicle listings. These enumeration values are case-sensitive. Each business policy can be associated with more than one category group.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#categoryGroup)
 to learn which calls use categoryGroup.

---

# MarkAsReceivedRequest - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

MarkAsReceivedRequest
=====================

The base request type of the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/mark\_as\_received** method, which is used to mark a return item as received by the seller.

Call that uses MarkAsReceivedRequest:

[/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/mark\_as\_received POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_mark_as_received__post.html)

Field
-----

comments ( _[Text](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Text.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container can be used to provide any related comments about marking the order line item as received.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#comments)
 to learn which calls use comments.

---

# ItemizedReturnShippingCostType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ItemizedReturnShippingCostType
==============================

This type is used by the **itemizedReturnShippingCost** container, which consists of the estimated or actual costs to ship an item. An **itemizedReturnShippingCost** container is required for each shipping cost type, such as label cost, shipping insurance, or the signature confirmation service.

Type that uses ItemizedReturnShippingCostType:

[ReturnShippingCostDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnShippingCostDetailType.html)

Calls that use ItemizedReturnShippingCostType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

amount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the amount for the shipping cost type shown in the **returnShippingCostType** field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#amount)
 to learn which calls use amount.

  

returnShippingCostType ( _[ReturnShippingCostType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnShippingCostType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumerated value indicates the type of shipping cost, such as the cost of the shipping label, shipping insurance, or the signature confirmation service.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#returnShippingCostType)
 to learn which calls use returnShippingCostType.

---

# NumericValue - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

NumericValue
============

This type holds a numeric value.

Type that uses NumericValue:

[Value](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/Value.html)

Calls that use NumericValue:

[getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

Fields
------

unitOfMeasurement ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Unit of measure for a numeric value.

**Allowed values:**

bps

Bits per second. A unit of data transmission.

kbps

Kilobits per second. A unit of data transmission.

bit

Binary digit. A unit of information storage.

byte

An ordered collection of bits. A unit of information storage equal to 8 bits in most contexts.

kb

Kilobit. A unit of information storage equal to 1024 bits.

MB

Megabyte. A unit of information storage equal to one million bytes.

mW

Megawatt. A unit of power equal to one million watts.

W

Watt. The base SI unit of power.

Hz

Hertz. A unit of frequency equal to one cycle per second.

kHz

Kilohertz. A unit of frequency equal to 1,000 cycles per second.

m

Meter. The base unit of distance.

mm

Megameter. A unit of distance equal to one million meters.

rph

Revolutions per hour. A unit of frequency.

hr

Hour. A unit of time equal to 3,600 seconds.

min

Minute. A unit of time equal to 60 seconds.

ms

Microsecond. A unit of time equal to one millionth of a second.

ns

Nanosecond. A unit of time equal to 10\-9 seconds.

sec

Seconds. The base unit of time.

g

Grams. A unit of mass equal to one one-thousandth of the SI base unit, the kilogram.

lbs

Pounds. The base Imperial unit of mass.

The allowed units of measure do not apply to all numeric values. For example, when specifying the number of doors, unitOfMeasurement is not needed.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#unitOfMeasurement)
 to learn which calls use unitOfMeasurement.

  

value ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A number value.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#value)
 to learn which calls use value.

---

# ReturnRefundInitiatorEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnRefundInitiatorEnum
=========================

This enumerated type lists the values that indicate the initiator of the refund.

Type that uses ReturnRefundInitiatorEnum:

[ActualRefundDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ReturnRefundInitiatorEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| AUTO\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that eBay automatically refunded the buyer. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the refund is unknown. |
| SELLER\_INITIATED | This enumeration value indicates that the refund was initiated by the seller. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ActivityOptionEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

�

ActivityOptionEnum
==================

This enumerated type lists all possible activities that may occur during the return process.  
  
If these values are returned in the **activityDue** fields of the **buyerResponseDue** or **sellerResponseDue** containers, it is the buyer's or seller's next expected action on the return request. If these values are returned under the **responseHistory** container, it is a return request action that has already occurred.

Types that use ActivityOptionEnum:

[AvailableOptionType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/AvailableOptionType.html)
, [ReturnResponseDueType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnResponseDueType.html)
, [ReturnResponseHistoryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnResponseHistoryType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ActivityOptionEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| AUTO\_APPROVE\_REMORSE | This enumeration value indicates that the auto-approve disposition rule for a remorse return has been activated by the system. |
| BUYER\_ACCEPT\_REPLACEMENT\_OFFER | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has accepted, or has the option to accept the offered replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| BUYER\_ACCEPTS\_NON\_PAYPAL\_PARTIAL\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer accepted the seller's partial refund offer. |
| BUYER\_ACCEPTS\_PARTIAL\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer accepted the seller's partial refund offer. |
| BUYER\_CLOSE\_RETURN | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer closed the return request. |
| BUYER\_CREATE\_RETURN | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer created a return request. |
| BUYER\_DECLINE\_PARTIAL\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer declined the seller's partial refund offer. |
| BUYER\_DECLINE\_REPLACEMENT\_OFFER | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has declined, or has the option to decline the offered replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| BUYER\_ESCALATE | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer escalated the return request to a return case. |
| BUYER\_MARK\_AS\_RECEIVED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has marked, or is expected to mark a replacement item as received.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| BUYER\_MARK\_REFUND\_RECEIVED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has marked, or is expected to mark a refund as received. |
| BUYER\_MARK\_RETURN\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has marked, or is expected to mark the return item as shipped. |
| BUYER\_PRINT\_SHIPPING\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer's next action is to print the return shipping label that is available. |
| BUYER\_PROVIDE\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has provided, or is expected to provide a return shipping label. |
| BUYER\_PROVIDE\_TRACKING\_INFO | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer has provided, or is expected to provide shipment tracking information for the item that is being returned to the seller. |
| BUYER\_SEND\_MESSAGE | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer's next possible action is to send a message to the seller and/or eBay. |
| BUYER\_UPDATE\_TRACKING | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer's next possible action is to update the shipment tracking information. |
| BUYER\_VOID\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer voided the shipping label that was available. |
| CHARGEBACK\_OPEN | This enumeration value indicates that system cancelled the return request because the buyer opened a chargeback request. |
| DELETE\_FILE | This enumeration value indicates that a file associated with a return request was deleted. |
| EBAY\_RULE\_AUTO\_APPROVE | This enumeration value indicates that eBay's automated business rules have approved a return request. |
| EMAIL\_SHIPPING\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer's or seller's next action is to email the link to a shipping label for themselves or for their order partner. |
| EXTERNAL\_CLAIM\_OPENED | This enumeration value indicates that the system has been notified that an external claim has been opened. |
| GENERAL\_TIME\_OUT | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out. |
| NOTIFIED\_DELIVERED | This enumeration value indicates that the eBay system has been notified that a return item has been delivered to the recipient. |
| NOTIFIED\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the eBay system has been notified that a return item has been shipped. |
| NOTIFY\_REPLACEMENT\_DELIVERED | This enumeration value indicates that the system has been notified that a replacement item has been delivered to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| NOTIFY\_REPLACEMENT\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the system has been notified that a replacement item has been shipped by the seller.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| NOTIFY\_UPDATE\_REFUND\_STATUS | This enumeration value indicates that the system has been notified that the refund status has to be updated. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that action or next possible action cannot be determined. |
| REMINDER\_BUYER\_TO\_RESPOND\_RE\_OFFER | This enumeration value indicates that the system was reminded that the buyer has to respond to a reply to a seller's offer to resolve the return request. |
| REMINDER\_BUYER\_TO\_SHIP | This enumeration value indicates that the system was reminded that the buyer has to return ship the item. |
| REMINDER\_FOR\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the system was reminded that the seller must refund the buyer. |
| REMINDER\_FOR\_REFUND\_NO\_SHIPPING | This enumeration value indicates that the system was reminded that the seller must refund the buyer and that the buyer is not expected to return ship the item. |
| REMINDER\_FOR\_RMA | This enumeration value indicates that the system was reminded that a Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) is associated with the return request. |
| REMINDER\_FOR\_SHIPPING | This enumeration value indicates that the system was reminded that an item must be shipped. |
| REMINDER\_SELLER\_TO\_RESPOND | This enumeration value indicates that the system was reminded that the seller has to make a response on the return request. |
| REMINDER\_SELLER\_TO\_SHIP | This enumeration value indicates that the system was reminded that the seller has to ship the replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| SELLER\_APPROVE\_REQUEST | This enumeration value is returned if the seller has approved a return request, or if approving the return request is one of their next options. |
| SELLER\_DECLINE\_REQUEST | This enumeration value is returned if the seller has declined a return request, or if declining the return request is one of their next options. |
| SELLER\_ESCALATE | This enumeration value indicates that the seller escalated the return request to a return case. |
| SELLER\_ISSUE\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has issued a refund. |
| SELLER\_ISSUE\_STORE\_CREDIT\_REFUND | **THIS ENUMERATION VALUE IS DEPRECATED.** |
| SELLER\_MARK\_AS\_RECEIVED | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has marked, or is expected to mark a return item as received. |
| SELLER\_MARK\_REFUND\_SENT | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has marked, or is expected to mark the refund as sent. |
| SELLER\_MARK\_REPLACEMENT\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has marked, or is expected to mark the replacement item as shipped.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| SELLER\_OFFER\_PARTIAL\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has offered, or has the option to offer a partial refund to the buyer. |
| SELLER\_OFFER\_REPLACEMENT | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has offered, or has the option to offer a replacement item to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| SELLER\_OFFER\_RETURNS | This enumeration value indicates that the seller offer returns to buyers. |
| SELLER\_PRINT\_SHIPPING\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the seller's next action is to print the shipping label that is available. |
| SELLER\_PROVIDE\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has provided, or is expected to provide a shipping label. |
| SELLER\_PROVIDE\_RMA | This enumeration value indicates that the seller's next possible action is to provide a return merchandise authorization (RMA) number to the buyer. |
| SELLER\_PROVIDE\_TRACKING\_INFO | This enumeration value indicates that the seller has provided, or is expected to provide shipment tracking information for the replacement item that is being sent to the buyer.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| SELLER\_RETRY\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the seller's next possible action is to retry the refund operation. |
| SELLER\_SEND\_MESSAGE | This enumeration value indicates that the seller's next possible action is to send a message to the buyer and/or eBay. |
| SELLER\_UPDATE\_TRACKING | This enumeration value indicates that the seller's next possible action is to update the shipment tracking information. |
| SELLER\_VOID\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the seller voided the shipping label that was available. |
| SUBMIT\_FILE | This enumeration value indicates that a file was associated with a return request. |
| SYSTEM\_BUYER\_PARTIAL\_REFUND\_AS\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the system paid the buyer a partial refund. |
| SYSTEM\_BUYER\_REFUND\_AS\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the payment system paid the buyer the refund amount. |
| SYSTEM\_CLOSE\_RETURN | This enumeration value indicates that the system closed the return request. |
| SYSTEM\_CREATE\_RETURN | This enumeration value indicates that the system created the return request. |
| SYSTEM\_IMMEDIATE\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the payment system initiated an immediate refund to the buyer. |
| SYSTEM\_INITIATED\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the system has automatically initiated a refund to the buyer. |
| SYSTEM\_SELLER\_CHARGE | This enumeration value indicates that eBay has automatically initiated a refund from the seller for the return. |
| TIME\_OUT\_AFTER\_DECLINE | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out after the return request was declined. |
| TIME\_OUT\_AFTER\_REFUNDED | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out after the refund. |
| TIME\_OUT\_AFTER\_REFUNDED\_AS\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out after the system paid out the buyer. |
| TIME\_OUT\_AFTER\_REPLACED | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out after the replacement item operation.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_AUTHENTICATION | For [Returns with Authenticity Guarantee](https://www.ebay.com/help/selling/selling-tools/selling-authenticity-guarantee?id=4644&st=3&pos=2&query=Selling%20with%20Authenticity%20Guarantee&intent=Authenticity&lucenceai=lucenceai&docId=HELP1344#section3)<br> items, this enumeration value indicates the authenticator took too long to authenticate the returned item. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_AUTHORIZE | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the seller to authorize the return request. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_AUTO\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the auto refund operation. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_BUYER\_RECOUP | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the buyer recoupment operation. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_DELIVERY | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out for delivery. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_ESCALATION | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the return request to be escalated. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_ESCALATION\_AFTER\_SMIR | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the return request to be escalated after the 'Seller make it right' period has ended. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_ITEM\_REPLACED | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the replacement item operation.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_MARK\_REFUND\_RECEIVED | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out for mark refund received operation. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_MARK\_REFUND\_SENT | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out for mark refund sent operation. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_MASS\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the mass payout operation. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the seller payout operation. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_PROVIDE\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the buyer or seller to create/provide a shipping label. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_REFUND | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while trying to process a refund operation. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_REPLACEMENT\_DELIVERED\_ITEM\_NOT\_RETURNED | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the replacement item to be marked as delivered.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_REPLACEMENT\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the seller to mark the replacement item as shipped.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_RETURN\_SHIPPING | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for return shipping operation. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_RMA | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the seller to provide the Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA). |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_SELLER\_INVOICE | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the seller to be invoiced for the buyer refund. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_SELLER\_PAYOUT | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for the seller payout operation. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_SHIPMENT\_TRACKING | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out for shipment tracking. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_SHIPPING | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for shipment tracking information. |
| TIME\_OUT\_FOR\_SHIPPING\_SYSTEM\_CLOSE | This enumeration value indicates that the system timed out while waiting for shipment tracking system to close the tracking number. |

��\* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# Appeal Case Decision - eBay Post-Order API Reference

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Appeal Case Decision
====================

POST /post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/appeal
--------------------------------------------------

Buyers and sellers can use this method to appeal a case decision that was not ruled in their favor by eBay customer support. Optionally, the buyer or seller can include an explanation of why they appealing the case decision through the **comments** container.  

This method is not supported in the Sandbox environment.

Input
-----

See also [Samples](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_appeal__post.html#Samples)
.

#### Resource URI (production)

POST https://api.ebay.com/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/appeal

#### URI parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required? | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **caseId** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Required | The unique identifier of a case. This URI parameter is required in order to identify the case for which an appeal will be filed against. The case ID value is passed in as part of the call URI. |

  
  

#### HTTP request headers

All requests made to eBay REST operations require you to provide the `authorization` HTTP header for authentication.  
See [HTTP request headers](https://developer.ebay.com/ebay-rest/content/rest-request-components.html#HTTP)
 for details.

  
  

#### Authorization

This call uses standard authorization tokens. See [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
 for details.

#### Payload model

The following lists _all fields that could be included_ in the request.

{ /\* _[AppealRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/AppealRequest.html)
_ \*/
"[comments](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_appeal__post.html#Request.comments)
":
    { /\* [Text](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Text.html)
 \*/
    "[content](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_appeal__post.html#Request.comments.content)
": [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
,
    "[language](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_appeal__post.html#Request.comments.language)
": [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
,
    "[translatedFromContent](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_appeal__post.html#Request.comments.translatedFromContent)
": [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
,
    "[translatedFromLanguage](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_appeal__post.html#Request.comments.translatedFromLanguage)
": [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
    }
}

#### Request field descriptions

  
  

| Input Container/Field | Type | Occurrence | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **comments** | [Text](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Text.html) | Optional | This container is used if the buyer or seller wants to add a note or explanation about why an appeal is being made against the case decision. |
| comments.**content** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | This field displays the actual textual content in the language specified in the **language** field. This field is always used for containers using the **Text** type. |
| comments.**language** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | This two-letter code indicates the language used to display the content in the **content** field. The language will default to the language used on the eBay site if a specific language is not specified through the **Accept-Language** HTTP header. This field is always used for containers using the **Text** type.  <br>  <br>The full list of language enumeration values are defined in the [LanguageEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/LanguageEnum.html)<br> type definition.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**: See [LanguageEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/LanguageEnum.html) |
| comments.**translatedFromContent** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | If language translation/localization is required, this field displays the actual textual content in the language specified in the **translatedFromLanguage** field. If language translation was not required, this field is not applicable. |
| comments  <br>  .**translatedFromLanguage** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | If language translation/localization is required, this two-letter code indicates the language used to display the content in the **translatedFromContent** field. If language translation was not required, this field is not applicable.  <br>  <br>The full list of language enumeration values are defined in the [LanguageEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/LanguageEnum.html)<br> type definition.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**: See [LanguageEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/LanguageEnum.html) |

  

Output
------

See also [Samples](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_appeal__post.html#Samples)
.

#### Payload model

This call has no response payload.

null

  
  

Samples
-------

New to making API calls? Please see [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
.

Note: Some item IDs, user IDs, or other data in these samples might no longer be active on eBay. If necessary, you can substitute current eBay data in your requests.

Sample: Appeal a Case Decision

This operation files a case decision appeal for a buyer or a seller.

**Description**

Use this operation to file an appeal on behalf of the buyer or the seller when eBay customer support rules against them in a case decision.

**Input**

The **caseId** path parameter identifies the INR or Return case being appealed, and the optional **comments** container enables the user to provide the desired degree of detail.

**URL format**. See also the [non-wrapped](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/Samples/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid_appeal__post_basic_in_url_json.txt)
 version of this URL.

POST https://api.ebay.com/post-order/v2/casemanagement/5\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*7/appeal
{
    "comments":{
      "content": "I am not happy with this case decision."
    }
    
}

**Output**

There is no output payload with this response. A successful call contains an HTTP status code of 200.

**JSON format**.

  
  

Change History
--------------

| Change Date | Description |
| --- | --- |

# ReturnCreationInfoType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnCreationInfoType
======================

This type is used by the **creationInfo** container, which provides details about the buyer's return request, including the order line item (and quantity) that is being returned and the reason for the return.

Type that uses ReturnCreationInfoType:

[ReturnSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnSummaryType.html)

Calls that use ReturnCreationInfoType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

[comments](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnCreationInfoType.html#comments)
 ( _[Text](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Text.html)
_ )

[creationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnCreationInfoType.html#creationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[item](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnCreationInfoType.html#item)
 ( _[ReturnItemType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnItemType.html)
_ )

[reason](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnCreationInfoType.html#reason)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[reasonType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnCreationInfoType.html#reasonType)
 ( _[ReturnReasonTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnReasonTypeEnum.html)
_ )

[type](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnCreationInfoType.html#type)
 ( _[ReturnTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnTypeEnum.html)
_ )

  
  

comments ( _[Text](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Text.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

If provided by the buyer at return creation time, this container provides more information to the seller about why a return is being requested. This container is only returned if the buyer made a comment at the time of creating the return request or draft.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#comments)
 to learn which calls use comments.

  

creationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the date/time when the return request was created.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#creationDate)
 to learn which calls use creationDate.

  

item ( _[ReturnItemType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnItemType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides information on the return item, including listing ID, listing title, order line item ID, and the quantity of the line item that is being returned.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#item)
 to learn which calls use item.

  

reason ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumerated value indicates the buyer's reason for creating a return request or draft.  
  
The supported enumeration values representing valid return reasons are defined in the [ReturnReasonEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/types/ReturnReasonEnum.html)
 type definition.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#reason)
 to learn which calls use reason.

  

reasonType ( _[ReturnReasonTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnReasonTypeEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This value indicates the broad reason why the buyer is opening the return request, such as `SNAD` or `REMORSE`.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#reasonType)
 to learn which calls use reasonType.

  

type ( _[ReturnTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnTypeEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumerated value indicates the buyer's desired outcome.

**Note:** Currently, the only supported value is `MONEY_BACK`.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#type)
 to learn which calls use type.

# ErrorSeverity - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ErrorSeverity ( [token](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
 )
====================================================================================================================

Indicates whether the error is a fatal error (causing the request to fail) or a less severe error (a warning) that should be communicated to the user.

Type that uses ErrorSeverity:

[ErrorData](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ErrorSeverity:

[getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchNames](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchNames.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| Error | eBay encountered a fatal error during the processing of the request, causing the request to fail. When eBay encounters an error, it returns error data instead of the requested business data. Inspect the error details and resolve the problem before resubmitting the request. |
| Warning | The request was successfully processed, but eBay encountered a non-fatal error during the processing that could affect the data returned. For example, eBay might have changed the value of an input field. In this case, eBay returns a successful response, but it also returns a warning. For best results, requests should return without warnings. Inspect the warning details and resolve the problem before resubmitting the request. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ItemDetailType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ItemDetailType
==============

This type is used by the **itemDetail** container, which consists of information about the order line item, including listing ID, transaction ID, price, transaction date, and quantity being returned.

Type that uses ItemDetailType:

[ReturnDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnDetailType.html)

Calls that use ItemDetailType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

------

_ )

 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

 ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ )

 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

[relistedItemId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ItemDetailType.html#relistedItemId)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[relistStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ItemDetailType.html#relistStatus)
 ( _[RelistStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RelistStatusEnum.html)
_ )

[returnQuantity](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ItemDetailType.html#returnQuantity)
 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

[transactionDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ItemDetailType.html#transactionDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[transactionId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ItemDetailType.html#transactionId)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

ebayPlusTxn ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field indicates if the order line item was part of an eBay Plus order. eBay Plus is currently available on the eBay Australia and eBay Germany marketplaces.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#ebayPlusTxn)
 to learn which calls use ebayPlusTxn.

  

itemId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique identifier for the eBay listing where the item was purchased. This field is used in conjunction with the **transactionId** field to identify a line item within an order.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#itemId)
 to learn which calls use itemId.

  

itemOnHold ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field indicates the item related to the return is hidden (the respective content is not returned). This field is returned as 'true' if the corresponding item is on hold due to eBay policy violation; false otherwise.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#itemOnHold)
 to learn which calls use itemOnHold.

  

itemPicUrl ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The URL of the gallery image for the listing. This field is returned when available. If the item is on hold, the returned value will be masked.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#itemPicUrl)
 to learn which calls use itemPicUrl.

  

itemPrice ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Shows the price of the item. If currency conversion was needed, the original currency and the exchange rate used are also shown.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#itemPrice)
 to learn which calls use itemPrice.

  

itemTitle ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The title of the eBay listing. If the item is on hold, the returned value will be masked.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#itemTitle)
 to learn which calls use itemTitle.

  

listingSiteId ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This integer value indicates the eBay marketplace where the sale occurred. The site IDs used for each eBay marketplace can be found in the [eBay Site ID to Global ID Mapping](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/merchandising/docs/concepts/siteidtoglobalid.html)
 page.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#listingSiteId)
 to learn which calls use listingSiteId.

  

relistedItemId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is only returned if the returned item is relisted as a new eBay listing, and this value will be different than the value in the **itemId** field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#relistedItemId)
 to learn which calls use relistedItemId.

  

relistStatus ( _[RelistStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RelistStatusEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value either indicates if the order line item is eligible to be relisted, or in a case where the seller wants to relist the item, the value returned here will indicate if relisting was successful or if it failed.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#relistStatus)
 to learn which calls use relistStatus.

  

returnQuantity ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This integer value indicates the quantity of the order line item being returned. This number is typically `1`, unless the buyer bought multiple quantity of the same line item in a multiple-quantity, fixed-price listing.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#returnQuantity)
 to learn which calls use returnQuantity.

  

transactionDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This timestamp indicates when the sales transaction occurred.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#transactionDate)
 to learn which calls use transactionDate.

  

transactionId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique identifier for the purchase of the item. This value is created right when the buyer is committed to buying the item, whether that buyer uses a 'Buy it Now' capability, is the winning bidder of an auction, or the buyer's Best Offer is accepted by the seller. This field is used in conjunction with the **itemId** field to identify a line item within an order.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#transactionId)
 to learn which calls use transactionId.

---

# ClaimHistoryEventModel - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)

*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ClaimHistoryEventModel
======================

This type is used to provide information about each event or action that has occurred with a particular INR inquiry or case.

Type that uses ClaimHistoryEventModel:

[ClaimHistoryResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ClaimHistoryResponse.html)

Calls that use ClaimHistoryEventModel:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

------

[action](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ClaimHistoryEventModel.html#action)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

 ( _[UserPartyEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/UserPartyEnum.html)
_ )

 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

 ( _[MoneyMovementEntries](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/MoneyMovementEntries.html)
_ )

  
  

action ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value indicates the action initiated by one of the parties involved with the INR inquiry or case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#action)
 to learn which calls use action.

  

actor ( _[UserPartyEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/UserPartyEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the party that took this action.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#actor)
 to learn which calls use actor.

  

date ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#date)
 to learn which calls use date.

  

description ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A text-based description of this event or action. This field is returned if available.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#description)
 to learn which calls use description.

  

moneyMovement ( _[MoneyMovementEntries](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/MoneyMovementEntries.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides details on one or more monetary transactions that were involved with the event or action. This container is not returned if no monetary transaction was involved with the event or action.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#moneyMovement)
 to learn which calls use moneyMovement.

# EscalateRequest - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

EscalateRequest
===============

The base request type of the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/escalate** method, which is used to escalate a return request to a case.

Call that uses EscalateRequest:

[/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/escalate POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_escalate__post.html)

Fields
------

comments ( _[Text](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Text.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container is used if the buyer or seller wants to add a note or explanation about why the return request is being escalated to a case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#comments)
 to learn which calls use comments.

  

reason ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is used to specify the reason why the buyer or seller is escalating the specified return request into a case.  
  
One of the enumeration values defined in the [EscalateReasonEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/types/EscalateReasonEnum.html)
 type definition must be used here. Some values are only applicable to the buyer escalating the return request, some values are only applicable to the seller escalating the return request, and some values are relevant to either party.  
  
Although this field is not required, it is recommended that it is used by the buyer or seller.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#reason)
 to learn which calls use reason.

---

# Close Case - eBay Post-Order API Reference

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Close Case
==========

POST /post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/close
-------------------------------------------------

**Note:** This call has been deprecated and will be decommissioned on March 2, 2026.

This method can be used on behalf of buyers to close an open case. Buyers can close an INR case after the item arrives, and they might close a Return case if they decide to keep the item, or have otherwise worked out an agreement with the seller.

This method is not supported in the Sandbox environment.

Input
-----

See also [Samples](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_close__post.html#Samples)
.

#### Resource URI (production)

POST https://api.ebay.com/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/close

#### URI parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required? | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **caseId** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Required | The unique identifier of a case. This URI parameter is required in order to identify the case that is being closed. The case ID value is passed in as part of the call URI. |

  
  

#### HTTP request headers

All requests made to eBay REST operations require you to provide the `authorization` HTTP header for authentication.  
See [HTTP request headers](https://developer.ebay.com/ebay-rest/content/rest-request-components.html#HTTP)
 for details.

  
  

#### Authorization

This call uses standard authorization tokens. See [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
 for details.

#### Payload model

The following lists _all fields that could be included_ in the request.

{ /\* _[BuyerCloseCaseRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/BuyerCloseCaseRequest.html)
_ \*/
"[closeReason](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_close__post.html#Request.closeReason)
": [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
,
"[comments](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_close__post.html#Request.comments)
":
    { /\* [Text](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Text.html)
 \*/
    "[content](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_close__post.html#Request.comments.content)
": [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
,
    "[language](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_close__post.html#Request.comments.language)
": [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
,
    "[translatedFromContent](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_close__post.html#Request.comments.translatedFromContent)
": [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
,
    "[translatedFromLanguage](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_close__post.html#Request.comments.translatedFromLanguage)
": [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
    }
}

#### Request field descriptions

  
  

| Input Container/Field | Type | Occurrence | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **closeReason** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Required | This field is used to provide a reason why the buyer is closing the case. One of the values in the **BuyerCloseReasonEnum** type (shown below) must be used. If the `OTHER` value is passed in, the buyer should use the **comments** container to provide some more details on why the case is being closed.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values are from [BuyerCloseReasonEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/BuyerCloseReasonEnum.html)<br>**:  <br>  <br><br>ITEM\_ARRIVED<br><br>This enumeration value indicates that the buyer closed the INR inquiry or case because the item arrived.<br><br>OTHER<br><br>This enumeration value indicates that the buyer closed the INR inquiry or case for an undisclosed reason.<br><br>WORKED\_OUT\_WITH\_SELLER<br><br>This enumeration value indicates that the buyer closed the INR inquiry or case after working out a solution with the seller.<br><br>WOULD\_RATHER\_KEEP\_THE\_ITEM<br><br>This enumeration value indicates that the case is being closed because the buyer has decided to keep the item. This value cannot be used as a **closeReason** in a **POST /post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId}/close** request. |
| **comments** | [Text](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Text.html) | Optional | This container can be used if the buyer wants to provide more details on why the case is being closed. |
| comments.**content** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | This field displays the actual textual content in the language specified in the **language** field. This field is always used for containers using the **Text** type. |
| comments.**language** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | This two-letter code indicates the language used to display the content in the **content** field. The language will default to the language used on the eBay site if a specific language is not specified through the **Accept-Language** HTTP header. This field is always used for containers using the **Text** type.  <br>  <br>The full list of language enumeration values are defined in the [LanguageEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/LanguageEnum.html)<br> type definition.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**: See [LanguageEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/LanguageEnum.html) |
| comments.**translatedFromContent** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | If language translation/localization is required, this field displays the actual textual content in the language specified in the **translatedFromLanguage** field. If language translation was not required, this field is not applicable. |
| comments  <br>  .**translatedFromLanguage** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | If language translation/localization is required, this two-letter code indicates the language used to display the content in the **translatedFromContent** field. If language translation was not required, this field is not applicable.  <br>  <br>The full list of language enumeration values are defined in the [LanguageEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/LanguageEnum.html)<br> type definition.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**: See [LanguageEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/LanguageEnum.html) |

  

Output
------

See also [Samples](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_close__post.html#Samples)
.

#### Payload model

This call has no response payload.

null

  
  

Samples
-------

New to making API calls? Please see [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
.

Note: Some item IDs, user IDs, or other data in these samples might no longer be active on eBay. If necessary, you can substitute current eBay data in your requests.

Sample: Basic Call

Closing a case

**Description**

This call allows a buyer or seller to close a case before eBay customer support makes a decision on the case. In this particular sample, it is the buyer that is closing an INR case after finally receiving the item from the seller.

**Input**

The buyer passes in the case ID value as a path parameter to identify the case that is being closed. The **closeReason** field is required and the seller's reason for closing the case is `ITEM_ARRIVED`. The buyer also includes the optional **comments** container to provide a little more detail.

**URL format**. See also the [non-wrapped](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/Samples/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid_close__post_basic_in_url_json.txt)
 version of this URL.

POST https://api.ebay.com/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/close
{ 
"closeReason": "ITEM\_ARRIVED",
"comments":
    { 
    "content": "I am closing the case because the item has arrived."
    }
}

**Output**

There is no output payload with this response. A successful call contains an HTTP status code of 200.

**JSON format**.

--------------

| Change Date | Description |
| --- | --- |

# Product Services APIs

Product API

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Product Services APIs
=====================

**Note:** eBay Product Services is no longer just for retrieving product-related information for Parts Compatibility (aka vehicle fitment). The Product and Product Metadata APIs can now support Product Creation, the ability to submit products for inclusion in the eBay catalogs.

The Prouct API and Product Metadata API support eBay's Parts Compatibility feature:

| API | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Product API](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/CallRef/index.html) | This API lets you retrieve product information for categories that are catalog enabled. It also allows you to add new products to an eBay catalog where needed. |
| [Product Metadata API](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/index.html) | This API retrieves metadata used to list items with Parts Compatibility or add products to an eBay catalog (Product Creation). |

---

# ReturnHoldInfoType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnHoldInfoType
==================

This type is used by the **holdInfo** container, which provides information on a hold that has been initiated on a return request. The **holdInfo** container and its child elements are only returned if a hold has been placed on the return request.

Type that uses ReturnHoldInfoType:

[ReturnDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnDetailType.html)

Calls that use ReturnHoldInfoType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

[holdCreationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnHoldInfoType.html#holdCreationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[holdReferenceId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnHoldInfoType.html#holdReferenceId)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[holdReleaseDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnHoldInfoType.html#holdReleaseDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[holdStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnHoldInfoType.html#holdStatus)
 ( _[ReturnHoldStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnHoldStatusEnum.html)
_ )

[holdTransactionId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnHoldInfoType.html#holdTransactionId)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

holdCreationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the date/time when the hold on the return request was initiated.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#holdCreationDate)
 to learn which calls use holdCreationDate.

  

holdReferenceId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value is the unique eBay-assigned ID of the hold on the return request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#holdReferenceId)
 to learn which calls use holdReferenceId.

  

holdReleaseDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the date/time when the hold on the return request is expected to get released.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#holdReleaseDate)
 to learn which calls use holdReleaseDate.

  

holdStatus ( _[ReturnHoldStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnHoldStatusEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumerated value indicates the current status of the hold on the return request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#holdStatus)
 to learn which calls use holdStatus.

  

holdTransactionId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value is the unique eBay-assigned ID of the transaction that is a part of the return request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#holdTransactionId)
 to learn which calls use holdTransactionId.
