# ShippingMethodEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ShippingMethodEnum
==================

This enumerated type lists the values that indicate whether the buyer/seller used an eBay shipping label or arranged for shipping on their own.

Type that uses ShippingMethodEnum:

[ShipmentTrackingType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ShippingMethodEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| SELF\_MAIL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer or seller handled the shipping of an item on their own, and did not use an eBay shipping label. |
| SHIPPING\_LABEL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer or seller used an eBay shipping label to ship an item. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates it is unknown whether the buyer or seller used an eBay shipping label to ship an item, or handled the shipping on their own. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# NoSellerUploadLabelReasonEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

NoSellerUploadLabelReasonEnum
=============================

This enumerated type lists the possible values that a seller can provide in the **noLabelReason** field of the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label** to indicate the reason why a shipping label is not being supplied to the buyer.

Type that uses NoSellerUploadLabelReasonEnum:

[ProvideLabelRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ProvideLabelRequest.html)

Call that uses one or more values of NoSellerUploadLabelReasonEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_add_shipping_label__post.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| ITEM\_TOO\_LARGE | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if the item is too large to be shipped using a return shipping label. Many shipping carriers have a weight limit. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if there is another reason why the seller is not providing a return shipping label to the buyer. If this value is used, it is good idea if the seller includes the **comments** container of the request to provide more details on the reason why no shipping label is being provided. |
| REIMBURSE | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if the seller will not provide a return shipping label to the buyer, but will reimburse the buyer for the return shipping cost. |
| RETURN\_TO\_INTL\_LOCATION | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if a return shipping label is not being provided to the buyer because the return address is located in another country. |
| RETURN\_TO\_STORE | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if the seller is asking the buyer to return the item to the store. This value is applicable for In-Store Pickup and Click and Collect orders. |
| WORK\_WITH\_BUYER | This enumeration value is used in the **noLabelReason** field if the seller will work with the buyer to determine how return shipping costs will be handled. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# EligibilityResultType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

EligibilityResultType
=====================

This type is used by the **eligibilityInfo** container, which includes the results of the return eligibility check, including any errors that may have occurred with the eligibility check.

Type that uses EligibilityResultType:

[ReturnEligibilityItemPerCheckTypeResult](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnEligibilityItemPerCheckTypeResult.html)

Call that uses EligibilityResultType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)

Fields
------

[caseId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/EligibilityResultType.html#caseId)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[eligibilityErrorDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/EligibilityResultType.html#eligibilityErrorDetail)
 ( array of _[ReturnEligibilityErrorDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnEligibilityErrorDetailType.html)
_ )

[eligibilityStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/EligibilityResultType.html#eligibilityStatus)
 ( _[StatusType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/StatusType.html)
_ )

[eligibleStartDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/EligibilityResultType.html#eligibleStartDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[returnCreationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/EligibilityResultType.html#returnCreationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[returnDeadline](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/EligibilityResultType.html#returnDeadline)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[returnId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/EligibilityResultType.html#returnId)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

caseId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This value is the unique eBay-assigned ID of an eBay case associated with the return request. This field is only returned if the return request was escalated into an eBay case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#caseId)
 to learn which calls use caseId.

  

eligibilityErrorDetail ( array of _[ReturnEligibilityErrorDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnEligibilityErrorDetailType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This array may consist of one or more errors that may have occurred with the return eligibility check. This array is only returned if one or more return eligibility check errors occurred.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eligibilityErrorDetail)
 to learn which calls use eligibilityErrorDetail.

  

eligibilityStatus ( _[StatusType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/StatusType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the success or failure of a return eligibility check. See **StatusType** for the description of the supported values.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eligibilityStatus)
 to learn which calls use eligibilityStatus.

  

eligibleStartDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the buyer is first eligible to create a return request for a item. This field is not returned if the buyer is not eligible to return the item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eligibleStartDate)
 to learn which calls use eligibleStartDate.

  

returnCreationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the date/time when the return request was created. This field is only returned if a return request was successfully created.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#returnCreationDate)
 to learn which calls use returnCreationDate.

  

returnDeadline ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the deadline for which the buyer must file a return request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#returnDeadline)
 to learn which calls use returnDeadline.

  

returnId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique eBay-assigned ID of the newly-created return request. This field is only returned if a return request was successfully created.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#returnId)
 to learn which calls use returnId.

---

# LabelActionEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

LabelActionEnum
===============

This enumerated type lists the actions that may be specified in the **labelAction** field in the request of the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label** method.

Type that uses LabelActionEnum:

[ProvideLabelRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ProvideLabelRequest.html)

Call that uses one or more values of LabelActionEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_add_shipping_label__post.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| AU\_LABEL | This value is used if an Australian seller has created an eBay shipping label for the return shipment. Australian sellers are billed for eBay shipping labels on their monthly invoice. |
| EBAY\_LABEL | This value is used if the seller has created an eBay shipping label for the return shipment. |
| MARK\_AS\_SENT | This value is used if the seller is using the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label** method to mark an eBay-generated label as being sent to the buyer. |
| NO\_LABEL | This value is used if the seller is not providing an eBay shipping label to the buyer. If this value is used, the seller must also include the **noLabelReason** field and provide the reason why no label is being provided.  <br>  <br>It could be the seller is providing their own shipping label to the buyer (and not an eBay shipping label). In the case where the seller provides their own label, they must populate the **labelId** field with the shipment tracking number of the shipping label they're supplying to the buyer. |
| PURCHASE\_LABEL | This value is used if the seller has purchased their own (non-eBay) shipping label for the return shipment. |
| UNKNOWN | Indicates the label action is not known. |
| UPLOAD\_LABEL | This value is used if the seller is providing their own shipping label to the buyer (and not an eBay shipping label). |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ErrorParameter - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ErrorParameter ( [string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
 )
=======================================================================================================================

Contextual data associated with an error.

Type that uses ErrorParameter:

[ErrorData](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html)

Calls that use ErrorParameter:

[getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchNames](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchNames.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

Fields
------

This type has no fields.

Attribute
---------

name ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

The name of the input parameter returned with the error. Inspecting the parameter (or its input value) will often aid in understanding the cause of the error. Not all error messages contain this value.

# RefundType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

RefundType
==========

This enumeration type contains the possible types of refunds - partial or full.

Type that uses RefundType:

[CaseDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html)

Call that uses one or more values of RefundType:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| FULL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer is scheduled to, or has already received a full refund. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that it is unknown whether the buyer is scheduled to, or has already received a full or a partial refund. |
| PARTIAL | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer is scheduled to, or has already received a partial refund |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# PartyEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

PartyEnum
=========

This enumeration type lists the eBay user types that can create or perform an action against an order cancellation request.

Types that use PartyEnum:

[CancelActivityHistory](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CancelActivityHistory.html)
, [CancelDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CancelDetail.html)
, [CancelSummary](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CancelSummary.html)

Calls that use one or more values of PartyEnum:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| BUYER | This enumeration value is returned if the buyer created or performed an action on an order cancellation request. |
| SELLER | This enumeration value is returned if the seller created or performed an action on an order cancellation request. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value is returned if eBay cannot determine which eBay party created or performed an action on an order cancellation request. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# SellerProfile - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

SellerProfile
=============

This type defines the common elements used by all business policies.

Types that use SellerProfile:

[AddSellerProfileRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/AddSellerProfileRequest.html)
, [AddSellerProfileResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/AddSellerProfileResponse.html)
, [PaymentProfileList](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentProfileList.html)
, [ReturnPolicyProfileList](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyProfileList.html)
, [SetSellerProfileRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SetSellerProfileRequest.html)
, [SetSellerProfileResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SetSellerProfileResponse.html)
, [ShippingPolicyProfileList](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyProfileList.html)

Calls that use SellerProfile:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Fields
------

[categoryGroups](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html#categoryGroups)
 ( _[CategoryGroups](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/CategoryGroups.html)
_ )

[deletedDate](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html#deletedDate)
 ( _[dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime)
_ )

[forceDuplicate](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html#forceDuplicate)
 ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ )

[profileDesc](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html#profileDesc)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[profileId](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html#profileId)
 ( _[long](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long)
_ )

[profileName](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html#profileName)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[profileType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html#profileType)
 ( _[ProfileType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ProfileType.html)
_ )

[profileVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html#profileVersion)
 ( _[long](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long)
_ )

[siteId](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/SellerProfile.html#siteId)
 ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ )

  
  

categoryGroups ( _[CategoryGroups](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/CategoryGroups.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
This container consists of one or more **categoryGroup** containers. One or more category groups are linked to each business policy.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#categoryGroups)
 to learn which calls use categoryGroups.

  

deletedDate ( _[dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
This timestamp indicates when a business policy was deleted. This field is only returned in **getSellerProfiles** for business policies that have been deleted. For business policies that have been deleted by the seller, **deletedDate** is the only field that will be returned for the matching policy (in addition to the identifying fields like **profileId** and **profileName**).

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#deletedDate)
 to learn which calls use deletedDate.

  

forceDuplicate ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
This value is for future use.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#forceDuplicate)
 to learn which calls use forceDuplicate.

  

profileDesc ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
This field is used by the seller to provide a description of the business policy. The **profileDesc** field is optional in the **addSellerProfile** and **setSellerProfile** calls. This field is only returned for a business policy if a description exists for that policy.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#profileDesc)
 to learn which calls use profileDesc.

  

profileId ( _[long](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
The unique identifier of a business policy. This value is created by eBay when a business policy is created (on the site or through the API). A **profileId** value is required when using the **setSellerProfile** and **removeSellerProfile** calls. It can be used as a filter in a **getSellerProfiles** to identify a specific business policy to retrieve. This field is always returned with all Business Policies Management calls.  
  
A **profileId** value returned in the response of an **addSellerProfile** call indicates that the business policy was successfully created.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#profileId)
 to learn which calls use profileId.

  

profileName ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
This string value indicates the name of the business policy. This policy name must be unique among all of seller's business policies. A **profileName** value is required when using the **addSellerProfile** and **setSellerProfile** calls.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#profileName)
 to learn which calls use profileName.

  

profileType ( _[ProfileType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ProfileType.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
This field indicates the type of the business policy. A **profileType** value is required when using the **addSellerProfile** and **setSellerProfile** calls. It can be used as a filter in a **getSellerProfiles** call to identify and retrieve business policies of a specific type.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#profileType)
 to learn which calls use profileType.

  

profileVersion ( _[long](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
This field is for internal use only.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#profileVersion)
 to learn which calls use profileVersion.

  

siteId ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
Unique identifier of the eBay site. This value is always returned in the **getSellerProfiles** call. It is optional in the **addSellerProfile** and **setSellerProfile** calls. If it is not used in an **addSellerProfile** or **setSellerProfile** call, the **siteId** value defaults to the seller's eBay registration site.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#siteId)
 to learn which calls use siteId.

---

# FundingSource - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

FundingSource
=============

This type shows the seller's funding source used for the buyer refund, or in the case where eBay issued the refund to the buyer, the seller's funding source used by eBay to recoup this refund money from the seller.

Type that uses FundingSource:

[MoneyMovementEntry](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/MoneyMovementEntry.html)

Calls that use FundingSource:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

Fields
------

brand ( _[FundingMethodBrandEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/FundingMethodBrandEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The enumeration value here indicates the brand of credit card used to fund the refund. This will be the seller's credit card on file with eBay.  
  
This field is only returned if the funding source type is `CREDIT_CARD`.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#brand)
 to learn which calls use brand.

  

memo ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This provides a note about the funding source. This field is only returned if available, or it might get returned as an empty string.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#memo)
 to learn which calls use memo.

  

type ( _[FundingMethodTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/FundingMethodTypeEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

he enumeration value here indicates the type of funding source, such as bank account, credit card, or available seller payout funds.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#type)
 to learn which calls use type.

---

# Price | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/price.html#mainContent)

Every new listing must have a starting price:

*   For an auction listing, the starting price is the price at which the progressive bidding starts.
*   For a fixed-price listing, the starting price is the static price at which a buyer can purchase the item.

Sellers can also add a [Buy It Now](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/buy-it-now.html)
 option to an auction listing, or assign a reserve price to the listing. If a reserve price is set, the item will not sell unless the bid of the winning bidder is equal to or higher than this price.

**Argument:** [Item.StartPrice](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html#Request.Item.StartPrice)

Currency
--------

This required three-digit enumeration value defines the currency associated with the listing marketplace. The item price and all costs passed in the call request will be using this currency.

Similarly, the listing fees and other cost-related data will use this currency.

For a list of which currencies can be specified as input to listing calls such as [AddItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html)
 for a given marketplace, call [GeteBayDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/XML/docs/Reference/eBay/GeteBayDetails.html)
, pass in the appropriate [siteID](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/site-id.html)
 in the header, and set the value of [DetailName](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/GeteBayDetails.html#Request.DetailName)
 to `CurrencyDetails`.

**Important!** Calling [GetItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/GetItem.html)
 will return the item's original currency regardless of the marketplace to which the [GetItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/GetItem.html)
 request is sent.

**Argument:** [Item.Currency](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html#Request.Item.Currency)

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.

---

# ErrorCategory - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ErrorCategory ( [token](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
 )
====================================================================================================================

There are three categories of error: request errors, application errors, and system errors. Request and application errors are often caused by passing invalid data in the request. System errors are primarily caused by application failures and cannot be corrected by changing request values.

Type that uses ErrorCategory:

[ErrorData](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ErrorCategory:

[getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchNames](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchNames.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| Application | An error occurred due to a problem with the request, with the most likely source being the application sending the request. For example, the request is missing a required data element or it contains an invalid field. The problem must be corrected before the request can be resent. Inspect the error message to find the cause of the problem. If the problem is due to an application error, modify the application and resend the request. If the error is due to invalid data, the source of the data must be corrected before you resend the resend request to eBay. |
| Request | An error occurred due to a problem with the request, with the most likely source being missing or invalid data in the request. The problem must be corrected before the request can be retried. Inspect the error message to find the cause of the problem. If the problem is a result of end-user data, alert the end-user to the problem and provide the means for them to correct the problem. Once the problem is resolved, resend the request to eBay. |
| System | Indicates that an error has occurred on the eBay system side. For example, a database or server could be down. Inspect the error message to find the cause of the problem. If the problem is on the eBay side, an application can retry the request as-is a reasonable number of times (eBay recommends twice). If the error persists, contact Developer Technical Support. Once the problem has been resolved, the request may be resent in its original form. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# BaseResponse - API Reference - Return Management API

eBay Return Management APIVersion 1.1.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/ReturnManagementAPIGuide.html)

*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

BaseResponse
============

This is the base class for the response container for all service operations. Returns error information associated with the request.

Calls that use BaseResponse:

[getActivityOptions](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/getActivityOptions.html)
, [getReturnDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/getReturnDetail.html)
, [getReturnMetadata](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/getReturnMetadata.html)
, [getUserReturns](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/getUserReturns.html)
, [getVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/getVersion.html)
, [issueRefund](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/issueRefund.html)
, [provideSellerInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/provideSellerInfo.html)
, [provideTrackingInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/provideTrackingInfo.html)
, [setItemAsReceived](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/setItemAsReceived.html)

Fields
------

[ack](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/BaseResponse.html#ack)
 ( _[AckValue](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/AckValue.html)
_ )

 ( _[ErrorMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/ErrorMessage.html)
_ )

 ( _[dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime)
_ )

[version](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/BaseResponse.html#version)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

ack ( _[AckValue](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/AckValue.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A token representing the application-level acknowledgement code that indicates the response status, such as "Success". The **AckValue** enumeration type specifies the possible values for **ack**.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/fieldindex.html#ack)
 to learn which calls use ack.

  

errorMessage ( _[ErrorMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/ErrorMessage.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Information for an error or warning that occurred when eBay processed the request. This field is not returned if the **ack** value is **Success**.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/fieldindex.html#errorMessage)
 to learn which calls use errorMessage.

  

extension ( _ExtensionType_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Not used by any call.  
  
Reserved for future use.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/fieldindex.html#extension)
 to learn which calls use extension.

  

timestamp ( _[dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This value represents the date and time when eBay processed the request. The time zone of this value is GMT and the format is the ISO 8601 date and time format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ). See Time Values in the eBay Features Guide for information about this time format and converting to and from the GMT time zone.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/fieldindex.html#timestamp)
 to learn which calls use timestamp.

  

version ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The version of the response payload schema. Indicates the version of the schema that eBay used to process the request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/fieldindex.html#version)
 to learn which calls use version.

# Unknown

Product API

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/product/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Simple Schema Types
===================

The eBay Schema makes use of many simple schema types. Further, some eBay-defined types are based on simple schema types.

Except where specified, the data types in the table below follow the conventions for XML Schema simple types as described by the World Wide Web Consortium in [XML Schema Part 2: Datatypes](http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/)
.

| Data Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| anyURI | Specifies a Uniform Resource Identifier (URI), a compact sequence of characters that identifies an abstract or physical resource.<br><br>The anyURI type is used most often to specify Uniform Resource Locators (URLs), such as a web page URL (e.g., ViewItemURL) or a file location URL (e.g., PictureURL), but can also be used for email addresses and other valid URI schemes. |
| boolean | Specifies binary-valued logic (true/false). Possible input values are true, false, 1, and 0. Possible output values are true and false only (not 1 or 0). |
| dateTime | A specific instant of time.<br><br>Date-time values are in the ISO 8601 date and time format. Hours are in 24-hour format (e.g., 2:00:00pm is 14:00:00). Unless otherwise specified, all date-time values are recorded in Universal Coordinated Time (UTC), also known as Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) or Zulu (because the time portion of the time stamp ends with a Z). That is, time values do not reflect the time zone shown on the eBay Web site. Here is an example of the date-time format:<br><br>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ (e.g., 2004-08-04T19:09:02.768Z)<br><br>See [Time Values](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#basics/DataTypes.html#TimeValues)<br> for information about how to convert between local time zones and GMT.<br><br>We use the dateTime data type to convey start and end times, the official eBay time, and other time values. |
| decimal | A decimal value of arbitrary precision.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type decimal can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Decimal values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>A whole number (i.e., without a period) can be passed as a decimal value.<br><br>We typically use the decimal data type to convey values related to units of measure. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type decimal. |
| double | A double-precision 64-bit floating point type.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type double can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Double values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>We typically use the double data type to convey values related to monetary amounts.<br><br>In some cases, a whole number (i.e., without a period) can be passed or returned as a double value. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type double. |
| duration | A length of time.<br><br>Duration values are in the ISO 8601 duration format, where P represents "Period", nY represents the number of years, nM the number of months, nD the number of days, T is the date/time separator, nH the number of hours, nM the number of minutes, and nS the number of seconds. So P2DT23H32M51S means "A period of 2 days, 23 hours, 32 minutes, and 51 seconds".<br><br>PnYnMnDTnH nMnS (e.g., P2DT23H32M51S)<br><br>We use the duration data type to convey values like the time left until a listing ends. For ended listings, the time left is P0DT0H0M0S (zero days, zero hours, zero minutes, and zero seconds). |
| float | A single-precision 32-bit floating point type.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type float can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Float values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>We typically use the float data type to convey values related to percentages. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type float. |
| int | A 32-bit integer value between -2147483648 and 2147483647.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type int can contain digits 0-9, and hyphen to indicate negative numbers. Some input fields restrict the input to particular values or a range of values. These cases are noted in the associated topic for the specific input field.<br><br>We typically use the int data type for enumerations, counts (e.g., how many items are returned), quantities, certain kinds of IDs (other IDs are strings), and similar concepts. |
| long | A value between -9223372036854775808 and 9223372036854775807. |
| string | A string of characters. All characters in the Unicode character set are supported.<br><br>Character entity references are used for escaping markup-significant characters. For example, use &amp; for the & (ampersand) character, &lt; for the < (less-than sign) character, and &gt; for the > (greater-than sign) character. It is a good idea to escape such characters in requests instead of using a CDATA construct. Responses from eBay do not include CDATA constructs.<br><br>Note that messages from eBay may contain HTML or XML markup within the message text. In this case, the markup elements within the text are escaped (e.g., &lt;!\[CDATA\[ ... \]\]&gt;).<br><br>Some string input fields do not have a known set of possible values. For these fields, the documentation specifies the maximum permitted length in characters. If you are using a URL for your request, and you need to use the & (ampersand) character as part of a string value, use &amp;.<br><br>xs:string is part of the XML schema namespace defined as:<br><br>xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" |
| token | A set of normalized strings.<br><br>In the API, this type of string contains no white space, by convention.<br><br>We typically use token to constrain a field to a specified set of normalized string values. For such a field, we define these values in an enumeration that uses token as its base type. |

Repeatable (Unbounded) Fields
-----------------------------

If the multiplicity of an element is defined as maxOccurs="unbounded" or a value greater than 1 in the schema, it means you can specify the field multiple times in a request (like you are specifying an array), or eBay can return the field multiple times in the response. In this case, the fields must be contiguous. That is, in a request, you cannot insert other fields between the repeating fields. Otherwise, some of the data will be dropped in an unpredictable manner.

Multiplicity Notation
---------------------

| Sample XML notation | Corresponding doc notation | Meaning § |
| --- | --- | --- |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" | \[0..\*\] | Zero or more |
| minOccurs="0" | \[0..1\] | Zero or one |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" | \[0..1\] | Zero or one |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="6" | \[0..6\] | Zero to six |

§  While this is the general meaning, be sure to read the description of the particular field: it may state an exception for a particular use.

# EscalateInquiryRequest - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

EscalateInquiryRequest
======================

This is the base request type of the **POST /post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId}/escalate** method, which is used to escalate an INR inquiry into a case.

Call that uses EscalateInquiryRequest:

[/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId}/escalate POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid_escalate__post.html)

Fields
------

comments ( _[Text](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Text.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The buyer or seller escalating the INR inquiry is required to use this container to provide more details about why the INR inquiry is being escalated to a case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#comments)
 to learn which calls use comments.

  

escalateInquiryReason ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is used to specify the reason why the buyer or seller is escalating the specified INR inquiry into a case.  
  
One of the enumeration values defined in the [InquiryEscalationReasonEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/types/InquiryEscalationReasonEnum.html)
 type definition must be used here. Some values are only applicable to the buyer escalating the inquiry, some values are only applicable to the seller escalating the inquiry, and some values are relevant to either party.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#escalateInquiryReason)
 to learn which calls use escalateInquiryReason.

---

# TrackingStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

TrackingStatusEnum
==================

This enumerated type lists the values that indicate whether the return item was delivered or still in-transit.

Type that uses TrackingStatusEnum:

[ShipmentTrackingType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of TrackingStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| CANCELLED | This enumeration value indicates that the current shipment tracking information has been cancelled. |
| CREATED | This enumeration value indicates that shipment tracking has been initiated. |
| DELIVERED | This enumeration value indicates that the item was delivered according to the shipment tracking history. |
| IN\_TRANSIT | This enumeration value indicates that the item is in-transit to the recipient according to the shipment tracking history. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates that shipment tracking status of the item is unknown. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# PaginationInput - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

PaginationInput
===============

Controls the pagination of the result set. Child elements specify the maximum number of item listings to return per call and which page of data to return. Controls which listings are returned in the response, but does not control the details associated with the returned items.

Types that use PaginationInput:

[GetProductCompatibilitiesRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/GetProductCompatibilitiesRequest.html)
, [ProductRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html)

Calls that use PaginationInput:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)

Fields
------

entriesPerPage ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Specifies the maximum number of entries to return in a single call. If the number of entries found on the specified pageNumber is less than the value specified here, the number of items returned will be less than the value of entriesPerPage. This indicates the end of the result set.  
  
If entriesPerPage is set to a number greater than 100, the default value, 100, will be used.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#entriesPerPage)
 to learn which calls use entriesPerPage.

  

pageNumber ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Specifies which subset of data (or "page") to return in the call response. The number of data pages is determined by the total number of items matching the request search criteria (returned in paginationOutput.totalEntries) divided by the number of entries to display in each response (entriesPerPage). You can return up to the first 100 pages of the result set by issuing multiple requests and specifying, in sequence, the pages to return.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#pageNumber)
 to learn which calls use pageNumber.

---

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

IssueRefundRequest
==================

The base request type for the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/issue\_refund** method, which is used to issue a refund to the buyer for a returned item.

Call that uses IssueRefundRequest:

[/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/issue\_refund POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_issue_refund__post.html)

Fields
------

comments ( _[Text](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Text.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container is used if the seller wants to provide more information to the buyer about the refund that is being issued.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#comments)
 to learn which calls use comments.

  

refundDetail ( _[RefundDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundDetailType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides details on one or more refunds received by the buyer. An **itemizedRefundDetail** container is returned for each refund type (refund on purchase price, refund on original shipping, or any other costs paid by the buyer). The amount(s) in the **itemizedRefundDetail.amount** field(s) should equal the amount in the **refundDetail.totalAmount** field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundDetail)
 to learn which calls use refundDetail.

  

relistItem ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field should be included and set to `true` if the seller wants to relist/restock the returned item automatically after the refund operation succeeds.  
  
Sellers can check the eligibility for an item to be relisted by calling [Get Return](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnId__get.html#Response.detail.itemDetail.relistStatus)
. In the response, check the **itemDetail.relistStatus** field for eligibility. This field will also show the status of a relist request as either `SUCCESS` (the relist succeeded) or `FAILED` (the relist request did not succeed).  
  
If the relist request succeeds, call [Get Return](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnId__get.html#Response.detail.itemDetail.relistedItemId)
 and check the **itemDetail.relistedItemId** to get the listing ID of the newly relisted item (the ID should match the ID of the original item listing).

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#relistItem)
 to learn which calls use relistItem.

---

# Product - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Product
=======

Container type for product data.

Types that use Product:

[GetProductCompatiblitiesResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/GetProductCompatiblitiesResponse.html)
, [GetProductDetailsResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/GetProductDetailsResponse.html)
, [ProductResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductResponse.html)

Calls that use Product:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)
, [getProductDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductDetails.html)

Fields
------

[compatibilityCount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html#compatibilityCount)
 ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ )

[notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html#notes)
 ( _[Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Notes.html)
_ )

[productDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html#productDetails)
 ( _[PropertyValue](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/PropertyValue.html)
_ )

[productIdentifier](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html#productIdentifier)
 ( _[ProductIdentifier](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductIdentifier.html)
_ )

[productStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html#productStatus)
 ( _[ProductStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductStatus.html)
_ )

[stockPhotoURL](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html#stockPhotoURL)
 ( _[StockPhotoURL](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/StockPhotoURL.html)
_ )

[type](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html#type)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

compatibilityCount ( _[int](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The quantity of compatibilities that have been specified for the product. When returned, indicates that the product is Parts Compatibility eanbled.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#compatibilityCount)
 to learn which calls use compatibilityCount.

  

notes ( _[Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Notes.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Optional notes that provide additional information about the product, such as the placement of the part or accessory on a vehicle or the type of vehicle a part fits.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#notes)
 to learn which calls use notes.

  

productDetails ( _[PropertyValue](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/PropertyValue.html)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Details about the product in the form of name-value pairs.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#productDetails)
 to learn which calls use productDetails.

  

productIdentifier ( _[ProductIdentifier](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductIdentifier.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The eBay produdt ID (ePID) or external IDs associated with the product. Use the Reference value as input to search for the same product in the future, or use the ISBN, EAN, or UPC value (if returned). The ISBN, EAN, and UPC values can also be useful as keys if your application is comparing products across different sites. Always returned when Product is returned.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#productIdentifier)
 to learn which calls use productIdentifier.

  

productStatus ( _[ProductStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductStatus.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Indicates whether or not a product is disabled for selling, product reviews, or use on Half.com.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#productStatus)
 to learn which calls use productStatus.

  

stockPhotoURL ( _[StockPhotoURL](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/StockPhotoURL.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Thumbnail and standard stockphoto URLs.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#stockPhotoURL)
 to learn which calls use stockPhotoURL.

  

type ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Indicates if a product is the top or main product in a family (Head) or a variant of the head product (Member). If a product is not part of a product family, it is also the Head.  
  
Sometimes several versions (or editions) of a product may be available. All versions of the same product are considered to be part of the same product family. Some product families may contain only one product; others may contain many products.

**Allowed values:**

Head

The product is the the head of the product family.

Member

The product is a variant of the head product.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#type)
 to learn which calls use type.

---

# RequestTypeEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

RequestTypeEnum
===============

This enumerated type allows a user to differentiate between a return request and a return request draft.

Type that uses RequestTypeEnum:

[ReturnRequestType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnRequestType.html)

Call that uses one or more values of RequestTypeEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| CREATE | Indicates the request is for a return request draft. |
| REQUEST | Indicates the request is for a return request. |
| UNKNOWN | Indicates that it's undetermined if the request is for a return request or a return request draft. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# RefundInfo - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)

*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

RefundInfo
==========

This type is used to provide more information about a buyer refund that was issued, or is scheduled to be issued for a cancelled order.

Type that uses RefundInfo:

[CancelDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CancelDetail.html)

Call that uses RefundInfo:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)

Field
-----

actualRefundDetail ( _[ActualRefundDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetail.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides detailed information about a buyer refund that was issued, or is scheduled to be issued for a cancelled order.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#actualRefundDetail)
 to learn which calls use actualRefundDetail.

# ReturnSortOrderType - API Reference - Return Management API

eBay Return Management APIVersion 1.1.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/ReturnManagementAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnSortOrderType ( [token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
 )
===========================================================================================================================

Enumerated type that determines whether returns in the **getUserReturns** response are displayed in ascending or descending order.

Type that uses ReturnSortOrderType:

[getUserReturnsRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/getUserReturnsRequest.html)

Call that uses one or more values of ReturnSortOrderType:

[getUserReturns](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/getUserReturns.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| Ascending | This value is passed into the **sortOrderType** field of the **getUserReturns** call to display all retrieved returns in ascending order. See the **ReturnSortType** documentation to get an idea about how **Ascending** affects the four different sort types. |
| Descending | This value is passed into the **sortOrderType** field of the **getUserReturns** call to display all retrieved returns in descending order. See the **ReturnSortType** documentation to get an idea about how **Descending** affects the four different sort types. **Descending** is the default value and will be used if the **sortOrderType** field is not included in the **getUserReturns** request. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ProductStatus - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ProductStatus
=============

This will indicate if the product is disabled for the specific application like selling flow, review flow or half sell flow.

Types that use ProductStatus:

[GetProductCompatibilitiesRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/GetProductCompatibilitiesRequest.html)
, [Product](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html)
, [ProductDetailsRequestType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductDetailsRequestType.html)
, [ProductRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html)

Calls that use ProductStatus:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)
, [getProductDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductDetails.html)

Fields
------

excludeForeBayReviews ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Product cannot be used in product review flows.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#excludeForeBayReviews)
 to learn which calls use excludeForeBayReviews.

  

excludeForeBaySelling ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Product cannot be used in selling flows on eBay.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#excludeForeBaySelling)
 to learn which calls use excludeForeBaySelling.

  

excludeForHalfSelling ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Product cannot be used in selling flows on half.com.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#excludeForHalfSelling)
 to learn which calls use excludeForHalfSelling.
