 ( _[ReturnStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnStatusEnum.html)
_ )

[timeoutDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnSummaryType.html#timeoutDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

  
  

buyerAvailableOptions ( array of _[AvailableOptionType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/AvailableOptionType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This array consists one or more options that are available to the buyer to move the return request to the next stage. If the return request is currently waiting for a response from the seller, this array may not appear at all.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#buyerAvailableOptions)
 to learn which calls use buyerAvailableOptions.

  

buyerLoginName ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value is the eBay user name of the buyer.  
  
**Note:** Effective September 26, 2025, select developers will no longer receive username data for U.S. users through this field. Instead, an immutable user ID will be returned in its place. For more information, please refer to [Data Handling Compliance](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//api-docs/static/data-handling-update.html)
.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#buyerLoginName)
 to learn which calls use buyerLoginName.

  

buyerResponseDue ( _[ReturnResponseDueType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnResponseDueType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container indicates the next action the buyer is responsible for, and the 'due date' for this action. This container might not be returned if there is currently no action due from the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#buyerResponseDue)
 to learn which calls use buyerResponseDue.

  

buyerTotalRefund ( _[TotalRefundAmountType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/TotalRefundAmountType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container returns the total refund amount for the buyer. This container will either show the estimated amount due if the buyer refund has yet to be issued, or will show the actual amount paid if buyer refund has been issued.  
  
In some cases, the **buyerTotalRefund** differs from the **sellerTotalRefund** because the buyer may receive refunds from eBay instead of from the seller.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#buyerTotalRefund)
 to learn which calls use buyerTotalRefund.

  

creationInfo ( _[ReturnCreationInfoType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnCreationInfoType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides details about the buyer's return request, including the order line item (and quantity) that is being returned and the reason for the return.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#creationInfo)
 to learn which calls use creationInfo.

  

currentType ( _[ReturnTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnTypeEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumerated value indicates the buyer's desired outcome.

**Note:** Currently, the only supported value is `MONEY_BACK`.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#currentType)
 to learn which calls use currentType.

  

escalationInfo ( _[EscalationInfoType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/EscalationInfoType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides information on whether or not the buyer or seller is eligible to escalate a return request to a case, and if a return request was escalated, a **caseId** can be found in this container.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#escalationInfo)
 to learn which calls use escalationInfo.

  

orderId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique eBay-assigned ID of the order being returned.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#orderId)
 to learn which calls use orderId.

  

returnId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique eBay-assigned ID of the return request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#returnId)
 to learn which calls use returnId.

  

returnPolicy ( _[ReturnPolicyType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnPolicyType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container indicates whether or not the seller is required to provide an RMA (return merchandise authorization) number to the buyer. The seller can use the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/decide** method to provide the buyer with the RMA number.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#returnPolicy)
 to learn which calls use returnPolicy.

  

sellerAvailableOptions ( array of _[AvailableOptionType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/AvailableOptionType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This array consists one or more options that are available to the seller to move the return request to the next stage. If the return request is currently waiting for a response from the buyer, this array may not appear at all.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#sellerAvailableOptions)
 to learn which calls use sellerAvailableOptions.

  

sellerLoginName ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value is the eBay user name of the seller.  
  
**Note:** Effective September 26, 2025, select developers will no longer receive username data for U.S. users through this field. Instead, an immutable user ID will be returned in its place. For more information, please refer to [Data Handling Compliance](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//api-docs/static/data-handling-update.html)
.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#sellerLoginName)
 to learn which calls use sellerLoginName.

  

sellerResponseDue ( _[ReturnResponseDueType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnResponseDueType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container indicates the next action the seller is responsible for, and the 'due date' for this action. This container might not be returned if there is currently no action due from the seller.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#sellerResponseDue)
 to learn which calls use sellerResponseDue.

  

sellerTotalRefund ( _[TotalRefundAmountType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/TotalRefundAmountType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container returns the total buyer refund amount due from the seller. This container will either show the estimated amount due if the buyer refund has yet to be issued, or will show the actual amount paid if buyer refund has been issued.  
  
In some cases, the **buyerTotalRefund** differs from the **sellerTotalRefund** because the buyer may receive refunds from eBay instead of from the seller.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#sellerTotalRefund)
 to learn which calls use sellerTotalRefund.

  

state ( _[ReturnStateEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnStateEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the current state of the return request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#state)
 to learn which calls use state.

  

status ( _[ReturnStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnStatusEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the current status of the return request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#status)
 to learn which calls use status.

  

timeoutDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the date and time when eBay may administratively close the return request is either a buyer's or seller's response is nor made by this deadline.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#timeoutDate)
 to learn which calls use timeoutDate.

---

# Unknown

Product API

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Simple Schema Types
===================

The eBay Schema makes use of many simple schema types. Further, some eBay-defined types are based on simple schema types.

Except where specified, the data types in the table below follow the conventions for XML Schema simple types as described by the World Wide Web Consortium in [XML Schema Part 2: Datatypes](http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/)
.

| Data Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| anyURI | Specifies a Uniform Resource Identifier (URI), a compact sequence of characters that identifies an abstract or physical resource.<br><br>The anyURI type is used most often to specify Uniform Resource Locators (URLs), such as a web page URL (e.g., ViewItemURL) or a file location URL (e.g., PictureURL), but can also be used for email addresses and other valid URI schemes. |
| boolean | Specifies binary-valued logic (true/false). Possible input values are true, false, 1, and 0. Possible output values are true and false only (not 1 or 0). |
| dateTime | A specific instant of time.<br><br>Date-time values are in the ISO 8601 date and time format. Hours are in 24-hour format (e.g., 2:00:00pm is 14:00:00). Unless otherwise specified, all date-time values are recorded in Universal Coordinated Time (UTC), also known as Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) or Zulu (because the time portion of the time stamp ends with a Z). That is, time values do not reflect the time zone shown on the eBay Web site. Here is an example of the date-time format:<br><br>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ (e.g., 2004-08-04T19:09:02.768Z)<br><br>See [Time Values](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/guides/features-guide/default.html#basics/DataTypes.html#TimeValues)<br> for information about how to convert between local time zones and GMT.<br><br>We use the dateTime data type to convey start and end times, the official eBay time, and other time values. |
| decimal | A decimal value of arbitrary precision.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type decimal can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Decimal values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>A whole number (i.e., without a period) can be passed as a decimal value.<br><br>We typically use the decimal data type to convey values related to units of measure. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type decimal. |
| double | A double-precision 64-bit floating point type.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type double can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Double values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>We typically use the double data type to convey values related to monetary amounts.<br><br>In some cases, a whole number (i.e., without a period) can be passed or returned as a double value. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type double. |
| duration | A length of time.<br><br>Duration values are in the ISO 8601 duration format, where P represents "Period", nY represents the number of years, nM the number of months, nD the number of days, T is the date/time separator, nH the number of hours, nM the number of minutes, and nS the number of seconds. So P2DT23H32M51S means "A period of 2 days, 23 hours, 32 minutes, and 51 seconds".<br><br>PnYnMnDTnH nMnS (e.g., P2DT23H32M51S)<br><br>We use the duration data type to convey values like the time left until a listing ends. For ended listings, the time left is P0DT0H0M0S (zero days, zero hours, zero minutes, and zero seconds). |
| float | A single-precision 32-bit floating point type.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type float can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Float values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>We typically use the float data type to convey values related to percentages. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type float. |
| int | A 32-bit integer value between -2147483648 and 2147483647.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type int can contain digits 0-9, and hyphen to indicate negative numbers. Some input fields restrict the input to particular values or a range of values. These cases are noted in the associated topic for the specific input field.<br><br>We typically use the int data type for enumerations, counts (e.g., how many items are returned), quantities, certain kinds of IDs (other IDs are strings), and similar concepts. |
| long | A value between -9223372036854775808 and 9223372036854775807. |
| string | A string of characters. All characters in the Unicode character set are supported.<br><br>Character entity references are used for escaping markup-significant characters. For example, use &amp; for the & (ampersand) character, &lt; for the < (less-than sign) character, and &gt; for the > (greater-than sign) character. It is a good idea to escape such characters in requests instead of using a CDATA construct. Responses from eBay do not include CDATA constructs.<br><br>Note that messages from eBay may contain HTML or XML markup within the message text. In this case, the markup elements within the text are escaped (e.g., &lt;!\[CDATA\[ ... \]\]&gt;).<br><br>Some string input fields do not have a known set of possible values. For these fields, the documentation specifies the maximum permitted length in characters. If you are using a URL for your request, and you need to use the & (ampersand) character as part of a string value, use &amp;.<br><br>xs:string is part of the XML schema namespace defined as:<br><br>xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" |
| token | A set of normalized strings.<br><br>In the API, this type of string contains no white space, by convention.<br><br>We typically use token to constrain a field to a specified set of normalized string values. For such a field, we define these values in an enumeration that uses token as its base type. |

Repeatable (Unbounded) Fields
-----------------------------

If the multiplicity of an element is defined as maxOccurs="unbounded" or a value greater than 1 in the schema, it means you can specify the field multiple times in a request (like you are specifying an array), or eBay can return the field multiple times in the response. In this case, the fields must be contiguous. That is, in a request, you cannot insert other fields between the repeating fields. Otherwise, some of the data will be dropped in an unpredictable manner.

Multiplicity Notation
---------------------

| Sample XML notation | Corresponding doc notation | Meaning § |
| --- | --- | --- |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" | \[0..\*\] | Zero or more |
| minOccurs="0" | \[0..1\] | Zero or one |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" | \[0..1\] | Zero or one |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="6" | \[0..6\] | Zero to six |

§  While this is the general meaning, be sure to read the description of the particular field: it may state an exception for a particular use.

---

# CreateReturnRequest - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

CreateReturnRequest
===================

The base request type of the **POST /post-order/v2/return** method, which allows you to create a return request from scratch or to convert a return request draft into a return request.

Call that uses CreateReturnRequest:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)

Fields
------

draftId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique eBay-assigned ID of a return request draft. This field is only applicable (and required) if the buyer is converting a return request draft into an actual return request with the **POST /post-order/v2/return** method.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#draftId)
 to learn which calls use draftId.

  

returnRequest ( _[ReturnRequestType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnRequestType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container is used to provide details about the buyer's return request, including the order line item (and quantity) that is being returned or the reason for the return.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#returnRequest)
 to learn which calls use returnRequest.

---

# ConsolidateShippingProfilesResponse - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ConsolidateShippingProfilesResponse ( [BaseResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/BaseResponse.html)
 )
=============================================================================================================================================

The response container for the **consolidateShippingProfiles** call.

Call that uses ConsolidateShippingProfilesResponse:

[consolidateShippingProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/consolidateShippingProfiles.html)

Field
-----

Job ( _[ConsolidationJob](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ConsolidationJob.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Container consisting of details related to the shipping policies consolidation job, including a unique ID, the status of the job, and the eBay site ID.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#Job)
 to learn which calls use Job.

# ActionDeadlines - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ActionDeadlines
===============

This type is used by the **actionDeadlines** container, which provides the number of remaining days that a buyer or seller has before being required to take action on an INR or Return case.

Type that uses ActionDeadlines:

[CaseDetailsResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetailsResponse.html)

Call that uses ActionDeadlines:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)

Fields
------

[daysForBuyerToProvideProofOfShipment](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ActionDeadlines.html#daysForBuyerToProvideProofOfShipment)
 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

[daysToExpireWithoutResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ActionDeadlines.html#daysToExpireWithoutResponse)
 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

[daysToExpireWithResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ActionDeadlines.html#daysToExpireWithResponse)
 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

[daysToReturnItem](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ActionDeadlines.html#daysToReturnItem)
 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

[maxDaysToFileClaim](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ActionDeadlines.html#maxDaysToFileClaim)
 ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ )

  
  

daysForBuyerToProvideProofOfShipment ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This integer value indicates the number of days that the buyer has to provide proof of return shipment before the case expires. This field is only returned for a Return case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#daysForBuyerToProvideProofOfShipment)
 to learn which calls use daysForBuyerToProvideProofOfShipment.

  

daysToExpireWithoutResponse ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This integer value indicates the number of days that a user (buyer or seller) has to make a required response on the case before the case expires.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#daysToExpireWithoutResponse)
 to learn which calls use daysToExpireWithoutResponse.

  

daysToExpireWithResponse ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This integer value indicates the number of days that remain before the case expires.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#daysToExpireWithResponse)
 to learn which calls use daysToExpireWithResponse.

  

daysToReturnItem ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This integer value indicates the number of remaining days that the buyer has to return an item and still expect a refund from the seller. This field is only returned for a Return case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#daysToReturnItem)
 to learn which calls use daysToReturnItem.

  

maxDaysToFileClaim ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This integer value indicates the number of remaining days that the buyer has to file a claim.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#maxDaysToFileClaim)
 to learn which calls use maxDaysToFileClaim.

# RelistStatusEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

RelistStatusEnum
================

This enumerated type defines the different possible response values that can be returned to indicate the relisting status of an item that was involved in an order cancellation or return request.

Type that uses RelistStatusEnum:

[ItemDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ItemDetailType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of RelistStatusEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| FAILED | This enumeration value indicates that the attempt to relist the item has failed. |
| RELIST\_ELIGIBLE | This enumeration value indicates that the item is eligible to be relisted automatically by eBay upon the successful cancellation of an order or once a returned item is marked as received by the seller. |
| SUCCESS | This enumeration value indicates that the item has been successfully relisted upon the successful cancellation of an order, or after a returned item has been marked as received by the seller. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# LabelActionEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

LabelActionEnum
===============

This enumerated type lists the actions that may be specified in the **labelAction** field in the request of the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label** method.

Type that uses LabelActionEnum:

[ProvideLabelRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ProvideLabelRequest.html)

Call that uses one or more values of LabelActionEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_add_shipping_label__post.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| AU\_LABEL | This value is used if an Australian seller has created an eBay shipping label for the return shipment. Australian sellers are billed for eBay shipping labels on their monthly invoice. |
| EBAY\_LABEL | This value is used if the seller has created an eBay shipping label for the return shipment. |
| MARK\_AS\_SENT | This value is used if the seller is using the **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/add\_shipping\_label** method to mark an eBay-generated label as being sent to the buyer. |
| NO\_LABEL | This value is used if the seller is not providing an eBay shipping label to the buyer. If this value is used, the seller must also include the **noLabelReason** field and provide the reason why no label is being provided.  <br>  <br>It could be the seller is providing their own shipping label to the buyer (and not an eBay shipping label). In the case where the seller provides their own label, they must populate the **labelId** field with the shipment tracking number of the shipping label they're supplying to the buyer. |
| PURCHASE\_LABEL | This value is used if the seller has purchased their own (non-eBay) shipping label for the return shipment. |
| UNKNOWN | Indicates the label action is not known. |
| UPLOAD\_LABEL | This value is used if the seller is providing their own shipping label to the buyer (and not an eBay shipping label). |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# Merchandising API | eBay Developers Program

Merchandising API

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/merchandising/docs/concepts/MerchandisingAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/merchandising/docs/concepts/MerchandisingAPI_FormatOverview.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/merchandising/docs/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/merchandising/docs/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/merchandising/docs/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

eBay Site ID to Global ID Mapping
=================================

Each eBay site maps to a unique [eBay global ID](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/merchandising/docs/CallRef/Enums/GlobalIdList.html)
. You must specify a global ID in the `X-EBAY-SOA-GLOBAL-ID` HTTP header for each API call. The global ID you use must correspond to one of the eBay site IDs in the following table:

| eBay Site ID | Site Name | Global ID |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0   | eBay United States | EBAY-US |
| 2   | eBay Canada (English) | EBAY-ENCA |
| 3   | eBay UK | EBAY-GB |
| 15  | eBay Australia | EBAY-AU |
| 16  | eBay Austria | EBAY-AT |
| 23  | eBay Belgium (French) | EBAY-FRBE |
| 71  | eBay France | EBAY-FR |
| 77  | eBay Germany | EBAY-DE |
| 100 | eBay Motors | EBAY-MOTOR |
| 101 | eBay Italy | EBAY-IT |
| 123 | eBay Belgium (Dutch) | EBAY-NLBE |
| 146 | eBay Netherlands | EBAY-NL |
| 186 | eBay Spain | EBAY-ES |
| 193 | eBay Switzerland | EBAY-CH |
| 201 | eBay Hong Kong | EBAY-HK |
| 205 | eBay Ireland | EBAY-IE |
| 207 | eBay Malaysia | EBAY-MY |
| 210 | eBay Canada (French) | EBAY-FRCA |
| 211 | eBay Philippines | EBAY-PH |
| 212 | eBay Poland | EBAY-PL |
| 216 | eBay Singapore | EBAY-SG |

  
  

* * *

---

# Unknown

Product API

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/Product/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/Product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/Product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Simple Schema Types
===================

The eBay Schema makes use of many simple schema types. Further, some eBay-defined types are based on simple schema types.

Except where specified, the data types in the table below follow the conventions for XML Schema simple types as described by the World Wide Web Consortium in [XML Schema Part 2: Datatypes](http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/)
.

| Data Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| anyURI | Specifies a Uniform Resource Identifier (URI), a compact sequence of characters that identifies an abstract or physical resource.<br><br>The anyURI type is used most often to specify Uniform Resource Locators (URLs), such as a web page URL (e.g., ViewItemURL) or a file location URL (e.g., PictureURL), but can also be used for email addresses and other valid URI schemes. |
| boolean | Specifies binary-valued logic (true/false). Possible input values are true, false, 1, and 0. Possible output values are true and false only (not 1 or 0). |
| dateTime | A specific instant of time.<br><br>Date-time values are in the ISO 8601 date and time format. Hours are in 24-hour format (e.g., 2:00:00pm is 14:00:00). Unless otherwise specified, all date-time values are recorded in Universal Coordinated Time (UTC), also known as Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) or Zulu (because the time portion of the time stamp ends with a Z). That is, time values do not reflect the time zone shown on the eBay Web site. Here is an example of the date-time format:<br><br>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ (e.g., 2004-08-04T19:09:02.768Z)<br><br>See [Time Values](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#basics/DataTypes.html#TimeValues)<br> for information about how to convert between local time zones and GMT.<br><br>We use the dateTime data type to convey start and end times, the official eBay time, and other time values. |
| decimal | A decimal value of arbitrary precision.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type decimal can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Decimal values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>A whole number (i.e., without a period) can be passed as a decimal value.<br><br>We typically use the decimal data type to convey values related to units of measure. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type decimal. |
| double | A double-precision 64-bit floating point type.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type double can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Double values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>We typically use the double data type to convey values related to monetary amounts.<br><br>In some cases, a whole number (i.e., without a period) can be passed or returned as a double value. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type double. |
| duration | A length of time.<br><br>Duration values are in the ISO 8601 duration format, where P represents "Period", nY represents the number of years, nM the number of months, nD the number of days, T is the date/time separator, nH the number of hours, nM the number of minutes, and nS the number of seconds. So P2DT23H32M51S means "A period of 2 days, 23 hours, 32 minutes, and 51 seconds".<br><br>PnYnMnDTnH nMnS (e.g., P2DT23H32M51S)<br><br>We use the duration data type to convey values like the time left until a listing ends. For ended listings, the time left is P0DT0H0M0S (zero days, zero hours, zero minutes, and zero seconds). |
| float | A single-precision 32-bit floating point type.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type float can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Float values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>We typically use the float data type to convey values related to percentages. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type float. |
| int | A 32-bit integer value between -2147483648 and 2147483647.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type int can contain digits 0-9, and hyphen to indicate negative numbers. Some input fields restrict the input to particular values or a range of values. These cases are noted in the associated topic for the specific input field.<br><br>We typically use the int data type for enumerations, counts (e.g., how many items are returned), quantities, certain kinds of IDs (other IDs are strings), and similar concepts. |
| long | A value between -9223372036854775808 and 9223372036854775807. |
| string | A string of characters. All characters in the Unicode character set are supported.<br><br>Character entity references are used for escaping markup-significant characters. For example, use &amp; for the & (ampersand) character, &lt; for the < (less-than sign) character, and &gt; for the > (greater-than sign) character. It is a good idea to escape such characters in requests instead of using a CDATA construct. Responses from eBay do not include CDATA constructs.<br><br>Note that messages from eBay may contain HTML or XML markup within the message text. In this case, the markup elements within the text are escaped (e.g., &lt;!\[CDATA\[ ... \]\]&gt;).<br><br>Some string input fields do not have a known set of possible values. For these fields, the documentation specifies the maximum permitted length in characters. If you are using a URL for your request, and you need to use the & (ampersand) character as part of a string value, use &amp;.<br><br>xs:string is part of the XML schema namespace defined as:<br><br>xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" |
| token | A set of normalized strings.<br><br>In the API, this type of string contains no white space, by convention.<br><br>We typically use token to constrain a field to a specified set of normalized string values. For such a field, we define these values in an enumeration that uses token as its base type. |

Repeatable (Unbounded) Fields
-----------------------------

If the multiplicity of an element is defined as maxOccurs="unbounded" or a value greater than 1 in the schema, it means you can specify the field multiple times in a request (like you are specifying an array), or eBay can return the field multiple times in the response. In this case, the fields must be contiguous. That is, in a request, you cannot insert other fields between the repeating fields. Otherwise, some of the data will be dropped in an unpredictable manner.

Multiplicity Notation
---------------------

| Sample XML notation | Corresponding doc notation | Meaning § |
| --- | --- | --- |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" | \[0..\*\] | Zero or more |
| minOccurs="0" | \[0..1\] | Zero or one |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" | \[0..1\] | Zero or one |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="6" | \[0..6\] | Zero to six |

§  While this is the general meaning, be sure to read the description of the particular field: it may state an exception for a particular use.

---

# ActualRefund - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ActualRefund
============

This type is used by the **actualRefund** container, which shows the total amount of the buyer refund that has been issued for the cancelled order.

Type that uses ActualRefund:

[ActualRefundDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetail.html)

Call that uses ActualRefund:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)

Fields
------

donationAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container defines the refund amount for listings that included a charitable donation. This data includes the amount of the charitable donation refund, as well as currency conversion information when necessary including the current currency code, the value in the original currency, and the original currency's code.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#donationAmount)
 to learn which calls use donationAmount.

  

feeCreditAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the total amount of fees credited towards this refund as per eBay policy.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#feeCreditAmount)
 to learn which calls use feeCreditAmount.

  

totalAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the total amount of the refund that has been issued.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#totalAmount)
 to learn which calls use totalAmount.

---

# CreateReturnRequest - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

CreateReturnRequest
===================

The base request type of the **POST /post-order/v2/return** method, which allows you to create a return request from scratch or to convert a return request draft into a return request.

Call that uses CreateReturnRequest:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)

Fields
------

draftId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique eBay-assigned ID of a return request draft. This field is only applicable (and required) if the buyer is converting a return request draft into an actual return request with the **POST /post-order/v2/return** method.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#draftId)
 to learn which calls use draftId.

  

returnRequest ( _[ReturnRequestType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnRequestType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container is used to provide details about the buyer's return request, including the order line item (and quantity) that is being returned or the reason for the return.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#returnRequest)
 to learn which calls use returnRequest.

---

# GetTrackingHistoryResponse - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

GetTrackingHistoryResponse
==========================

The base response for the **GET /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/tracking** method. The information in this response should be available once the shipment information has been uploaded to the eBay system

Call that uses GetTrackingHistoryResponse:

[/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/tracking GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_tracking__get.html)

Fields
------

carrierUsed ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value indicates the shipping carrier used to ship the item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#carrierUsed)
 to learn which calls use carrierUsed.

  

scanHistory ( array of _[ScanDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ScanDetailType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container holds an array of one or more shipment events/scans that have occurred with the item being returned, and each node provides details on each time a shipment is scanned by the shipping carrier.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#scanHistory)
 to learn which calls use scanHistory.

  

trackingNumber ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This value is the tracking number for the shipment. Shipment tracking is mandatory except in countries where shipment tracking is not available, so this number should generally always be returned.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#trackingNumber)
 to learn which calls use trackingNumber.

  

trackingStatus ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value indicates the delivery status of the shipment, such as in-transit or delivered. This field will be returned unless it is not known.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#trackingStatus)
 to learn which calls use trackingStatus.

---

# CaseDetails - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

CaseDetails
===========

This type is used by the **caseDetails** container that is returned in the **GET /post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}** method to provide detailed information about the case.

Type that uses CaseDetails:

[CaseDetailsResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetailsResponse.html)

Call that uses CaseDetails:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)

Fields
------

[buyerFinalAcceptResolution](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#buyerFinalAcceptResolution)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[buyerInitExpectResolution](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#buyerInitExpectResolution)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[creationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#creationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[escalationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#escalationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[expirationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#expirationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[lastBuyerRespDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#lastBuyerRespDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[lastSellerRespDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#lastSellerRespDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[paypalPaid](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#paypalPaid)
 ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ )

[refundAmounts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#refundAmounts)
 ( _[RefundAmounts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundAmounts.html)
_ )

[refundChargeAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#refundChargeAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

[refundDeadlineDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#refundDeadlineDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[refundType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetails.html#refundType)
 ( _[RefundType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundType.html)
_ )

  
  

buyerFinalAcceptResolution ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value indicates what the buyer ultimately agreed to with the seller to resolve the case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#buyerFinalAcceptResolution)
 to learn which calls use buyerFinalAcceptResolution.

  

buyerInitExpectResolution ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value indicates what the buyer initially agreed to with the seller to resolve the case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#buyerInitExpectResolution)
 to learn which calls use buyerInitExpectResolution.

  

creationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the case was created.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#creationDate)
 to learn which calls use creationDate.

  

escalationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the associated INR inquiry or return request was escalated into a case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#escalationDate)
 to learn which calls use escalationDate.

  

expirationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when a case will expire naturally if a required response from the buyer or seller is not made.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#expirationDate)
 to learn which calls use expirationDate.

  

lastBuyerRespDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the buyer last responded to, or performed an action on the case. This field is only returned if the buyer has responded to and/or performed an action on the case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#lastBuyerRespDate)
 to learn which calls use lastBuyerRespDate.

  

lastSellerRespDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the seller last responded to, or performed an action on the case. This field is only returned if the seller has responded to and/or performed an action on the case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#lastSellerRespDate)
 to learn which calls use lastSellerRespDate.

  

paypalPaid ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

**THIS FIELD IS DEPRECATED**.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#paypalPaid)
 to learn which calls use paypalPaid.

  

refundAmounts ( _[RefundAmounts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundAmounts.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container consists of details related to the expected and actual refund amounts. This container is only returned if a refund is scheduled to, or has been issued to the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundAmounts)
 to learn which calls use refundAmounts.

  

refundChargeAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the total amount charged to the seller for this refund. The value will be in the seller's default currency. This container is only applicable and returned if a refund has been issued to the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundChargeAmount)
 to learn which calls use refundChargeAmount.

  

refundDeadlineDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the latest date/time when the buyer is eligible for a refund.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundDeadlineDate)
 to learn which calls use refundDeadlineDate.

  

refundType ( _[RefundType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates whether the buyer is scheduled to, or has already received a a full or partial refund. This field is only returned if a refund is scheduled to, or has been issued to the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundType)
 to learn which calls use refundType.

---

# FundingMethodBrandEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

FundingMethodBrandEnum
======================

This enumeration type lists the possible credit card brands that can be used by eBay to fund a seller's refund to a buyer. This type is only applicable if a buyer refund will be funded using a seller's credit card as the funding source.

Types that use FundingMethodBrandEnum:

[FundingSource](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/FundingSource.html)
, [FundingSourceType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/FundingSourceType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of FundingMethodBrandEnum:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| AMERICAN\_EXPRESS | This enumeration value indicates that a American Express credit card will be used to fund the refund to the buyer. |
| DISCOVER | This enumeration value indicates that a Discover credit card will be used to fund the refund to the buyer. |
| MASTERCARD | This enumeration value indicates that a Mastercard credit card will be used to fund the refund to the buyer. |
| VISA | This enumeration value indicates that a Visa credit card will be used to fund the refund to the buyer. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ErrorData - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ErrorData
=========

A container for error details.

Type that uses ErrorData:

[ErrorMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorMessage.html)

Calls that use ErrorData:

[getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchNames](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchNames.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

Fields
------

[category](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#category)
 ( _[ErrorCategory](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorCategory.html)
_ )

[domain](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#domain)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[errorId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#errorId)
 ( _[long](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long)
_ )

[exceptionId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#exceptionId)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[message](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#message)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[parameter](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#parameter)
 ( _[ErrorParameter](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorParameter.html)
 ([string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
)_ )

[severity](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#severity)
 ( _[ErrorSeverity](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorSeverity.html)
_ )

[subdomain](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html#subdomain)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

category ( _[ErrorCategory](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorCategory.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

There are three categories of errors: request errors, application errors, and system errors.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#category)
 to learn which calls use category.

  

domain ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Name of the domain in which the error occurred.

**domain values:**

Marketplace

A business or validation error occurred in the service.

SOA

An exception occurred in the Service Oriented Architecture (SOA) framework.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#domain)
 to learn which calls use domain.

  

errorId ( _[long](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A unique code that identifies the particular error condition that occurred. Your application can use error codes as identifiers in your customized error-handling algorithms.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#errorId)
 to learn which calls use errorId.

  

exceptionId ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Unique identifier for an exception associated with an error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#exceptionId)
 to learn which calls use exceptionId.

  

message ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A detailed description of the condition that caused in the error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#message)
 to learn which calls use message.

  

parameter ( _[ErrorParameter](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorParameter.html)
 ([string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
)_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Various warning and error messages return one or more variables that contain contextual information about the error. This is often the field or value that triggered the error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#parameter)
 to learn which calls use parameter.

  

severity ( _[ErrorSeverity](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorSeverity.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Indicates whether the reported problem is fatal (an error) or is less- severe (a warning). Review the error message details for information on the cause.  
  
If the request fails and the application is the source of the error (for example, a required element is missing), update the application before you retry the request. If the problem is due to incorrect user data, alert the end-user to the problem and provide the means for them to correct the data. Once the problem in the application or data is resolved, re-send the request to eBay.  
  
If the source of the problem is on eBay's side, you can retry the request a reasonable number of times (eBay recommends you try the request twice). If the error persists, contact Developer Technical Support. Once the problem has been resolved, you can resend the request in its original form.  
  
If a warning occurs, warning information is returned in addition to the business data. Normally, you do not need to resend the request (as the original request was successful). However, depending on the cause of the warning, you might need to contact the end user, or eBay, to effect a long term solution to the problem.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#severity)
 to learn which calls use severity.

  

subdomain ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Name of the subdomain in which the error occurred.

**subdomain values:**

MarketplaceCatalog

The error is specific to the Marketplace Catalog subdomain.

MarketplaceCommon

The error is common to all Marketplace services.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#subdomain)
 to learn which calls use subdomain.

---

# ReturnUserRoleEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnUserRoleEnum
==================

This enumerated type lists all possible roles for parties involved in a return request, such as buyer, seller, and eBay.

Types that use ReturnUserRoleEnum:

[ReturnFileType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html)
, [ReturnResponseHistoryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnResponseHistoryType.html)
, [ShipmentTrackingType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingType.html)
, [ShipmentType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentType.html)

Calls that use one or more values of ReturnUserRoleEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/files GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_files__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| BUYER | This enumeration value indicates that the buyer performed the action on the return request. |
| EBAY | This enumeration value indicates that eBay customer support performed the action on the return request. |
| OTHER | This enumeration value indicates that it is unknown who/what performed the action on the return request. |
| SELLER | This enumeration value indicates that the seller performed the action on the return request. |
| SYSTEM | This enumeration value indicates that the action on the return request was an automated action initiated by eBay's system. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# Global ID Values - Product API

Product API

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Global ID Values ([string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/CallRef/types/simpleTypes.html)
 )
========================================================================================================

The Global ID is a unique identifier for combinations of site, language, and territory. Global ID values are returned in **globalId** and are used as input for the X-EBAY-SOA-GLOBAL-ID header. The global ID you use must correspond to an eBay site with a valid site ID. See [eBay Site ID to Global ID Mapping](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product/Concepts/SiteIDToGlobalID.html)
 for a list of global IDs you can use with the API calls.

**Enumeration Values (active sites only)**

| Global ID | Language | Territory | Site Name | eBay Site ID |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| EBAY-AT | de-AT | AT  | eBay Austria | 16  |
| EBAY-AU | en-AU | AU  | eBay Australia | 15  |
| EBAY-CH | de-CH | CH  | eBay Switzerland | 193 |
| EBAY-DE | en-DE | DE  | eBay Germany | 77  |
| EBAY-ENCA | en-CA | CA  | eBay Canada (English) | 2   |
| EBAY-ES | es-ES | ES  | eBay Spain | 186 |
| EBAY-FR | fr-FR | FR  | eBay France | 71  |
| EBAY-FRBE | fr-BE | BE  | eBay Belgium (French) | 23  |
| EBAY-FRCA | fr-CA | CA  | eBay Canada (French) | 210 |

# ReturnSortType - API Reference - Return Management API

eBay Return Management APIVersion 1.1.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/ReturnManagementAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnSortType ( [token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
 )
======================================================================================================================

Enumerated type that contains the valid values to use in the **sortType** field. This field is used to sort returns in the response based on either return filing date, estimated refund amount, refund due date, or buyer login name. If this field is not used, returns are sorted by filing date.

Type that uses ReturnSortType:

[getUserReturnsRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/getUserReturnsRequest.html)

Call that uses one or more values of ReturnSortType:

[getUserReturns](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/getUserReturns.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| BuyerLoginName | If this value is specified, returns are sorted alphabetically based on the buyer's eBay login name. If **sortOrder** is set to **Ascending**, returns are sorted in alphabetical order according to eBay login name. If **sortOrder** is set to **Descending**, returns are sorted in reverse alphabetical order according to eBay login name. **FilingDate** is the default value, so returns will be sorted by filing date if a **sortOrder** value is not specified in the **getUserReturns** request. |
| EstimatedAmount | If this value is specified, returns are sorted based on the estimated amount of the return. If **sortOrder** is set to **Descending**, the highest value returns will appear first in the results set. If **sortOrder** is set to **Ascending**, the lowest value returns will appear first in the results set. **FilingDate** is the default value, so returns will be sorted by filing date if a **sortOrder** value is not specified in the **getUserReturns** request. |
| FilingDate | If this value is specified, returns are sorted based on the filing date of the return. If **sortOrder** is set to **Descending**, the most recent returns will appear first in the results set. If **sortOrder** is set to **Ascending**, the oldest returns will appear first in the results set. **FilingDate** is the default value, so returns will be sorted by filing date if a **sortOrder** value is not specified in the **getUserReturns** request. |
| RefundDueDate | If this value is specified, returns are sorted based on the refund due date. If **sortOrder** is set to **Descending**, returns with refund due dates the furthest in the future will appear first in the results set. If **sortOrder** is set to **Ascending**, returns with refund due dates the nearest in the future will appear first in the results set. **FilingDate** is the default value, so returns will be sorted by filing date if a **sortOrder** value is not specified in the **getUserReturns** request. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ReturnStatusInputType - API Reference - Return Management API

eBay Return Management APIVersion 1.1.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/ReturnManagementAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnStatusInputType ( [token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
 )
=============================================================================================================================

Enumerated type that defines the return status values that can be passed into the **ReturnStatusFilter** container and used as filters for the **getUserReturns** call.

Type that uses ReturnStatusInputType:

[ReturnStatusFilterType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/ReturnStatusFilterType.html)

Call that uses one or more values of ReturnStatusInputType:

[getUserReturns](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/getUserReturns.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| CLOSED | This value is passed into the **ReturnStatusFilter.ReturnStatus** field of the **getUserReturns** request to retrieve all closed returns. If **CLOSED** is used as a filter, returns in the following states (these values are indicated in the **Returns.ReturnSummary.status** field) are returned:<br><br>*   CLOSED<br>*   CS\_CLOSED<br>*   EXPIRED<br>*   YOU\_CONTACTED\_CS\_ABOUT\_CLOSED\_Return |
| ITEM\_SHIPPED | This value is passed into the **ReturnStatusFilter.ReturnStatus** field of the **getUserReturns** request to retrieve all returns with the status of WAITING\_DELIVERY. Return status values are indicated in the **Returns.ReturnSummary.status** field. |
| MY\_RESPONSE\_DUE | This value is passed into the **ReturnStatusFilter.ReturnStatus** field of the **getUserReturns** request to retrieve all returns in which the seller's response is due. The two applicable return status values returned in the **Returns.ReturnSummary.status** field are MY\_PAYMENT\_DUE and MY\_RESPONSE\_DUE. The MY\_PAYMENT\_DUE value indicates that the seller is expected to issue a refund to the buyer. The MY\_RESPONSE\_DUE value can indicate that the seller must provide an RMA number to the buyer, or the seller must contact eBay customer support. |
| OTHER\_PARTY\_RESPONSE\_DUE | This value is passed into the **ReturnStatusFilter.ReturnStatus** field of the **getUserReturns** request to retrieve all returns in which the other party's response is due. The only applicable return status value returned in **Returns.ReturnSummary.status** field is OTHER\_PARTY\_RESPONSE\_DUE. |
| RETURN\_STARTED | This value is passed into the **ReturnStatusFilter.ReturnStatus** field of the **getUserReturns** request to retrieve all open returns. If **RETURN\_STARTED** is used as a filter, returns in the following states (these values are indicated in the **Returns.ReturnSummary.status** field) are returned:<br><br>*   MY\_PAYMENT\_DUE<br>*   MY\_RESPONSE\_DUE<br>*   OTHER\_PARTY\_CONTACTED\_CS\_AWAITING\_RESPONSE<br>*   OTHER\_PARTY\_RESPONSE\_DUE<br>*   PAID<br>*   WAITING\_DELIVERY<br>*   YOU\_CONTACTED\_CS\_AWAITING\_RESPONSE |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---
