_ )

[eligibleForAppeal](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/AppealDetails.html#eligibleForAppeal)
 ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ )

  
  

appealCloseReasonEnum ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates why the appeal on the case decision was closed. This field will only be returned if an appeal was made on a case decision and this appeal has been closed.  
  
The list of enumeration values that may be returned in this field are defined in the [AppealCloseReasonEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/types/AppealCloseReasonEnum.html)
 type definition.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#appealCloseReasonEnum)
 to learn which calls use appealCloseReasonEnum.

  

appealDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the date and time when the appeal was initiated by buyer or seller. This field will only be returned if an appeal was made on the case decision.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#appealDate)
 to learn which calls use appealDate.

  

appealReasonCode ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This string value is a string representation of why the buyer or seller appealed the case decision. This field is only returned if the buyer or seller made an appeal on the case decision.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#appealReasonCode)
 to learn which calls use appealReasonCode.

  

appealRefundAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the total amount refunded to the seller if the seller wins an appeal on a case decision going against the seller. The value will be in the seller's default currency. This field is only returned if the seller made an appeal on the case decision.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#appealRefundAmount)
 to learn which calls use appealRefundAmount.

  

appealStatus ( _[AppealGrantStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/AppealGrantStatusEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates whether or not eBay has accepted the buyer's or seller's appeal on the case decision. This field is only returned if the buyer or seller has attempted to make an appeal on the case decision.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#appealStatus)
 to learn which calls use appealStatus.

  

appealStatusEnum ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the current status of the appeal against the case decision.  
  
The list of enumeration values that may be returned in this field are defined in the [AppealStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/types/AppealStatusEnum.html)
 type definition.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#appealStatusEnum)
 to learn which calls use appealStatusEnum.

  

eligibleForAppeal ( _[boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#boolean)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This boolean field will be returned as `true` if the buyer or seller is eligible to appeal the decision on the case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eligibleForAppeal)
 to learn which calls use eligibleForAppeal.

---

# Product search feed response definitions | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-response-product-search-feed.html#mainContent)

A product search feed response will include details on matching eBay catalog products if they were found using product identifiers or keywords. If a catalog product was not found, a warning message will be returned that indicates that no matching product was found. The following table explains the elements or fields in the product search feed response file.

Sample response file:

*   [product-search-response.csv](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/res/Resources/images/mip-user/mip-samples/ebay-response-csv/product-search-response.csv)
    
*   XML sample response file not available
    

**Note:** The fields in the table below are ordered according to their order in the CSV response file. The parent is provided for XML fields in order to make it easier to locate that field in the XML response file.

 
| Field name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CSV: `Reference ID`<br><br>XML:  <br>`matchingProducts.referenceID` | The seller-provided reference ID from the request. This can be an eBay Product ID (`ePID`), or from a set of values to set the type of ID being passed in to the **Product Identifier** field (see [Available Reference ID values](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-enum-reference-id-values.html)<br>).<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `Channel ID`<br><br>XML:  <br>`productDetails.channelID` | The eBay site on which the product search should be made.<br><br>The unique identifiers of the selling site, such as `EBAY_US`, `EBAY_GB`, or `EBAY_IT`. If not provided in the request feed, this field defaults to `EBAY_US`. See [Marketplace enumerated values](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-enum-marketplace.html)<br> for full list.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `ePID`<br><br>XML:`productDetails.channelID` | The eBay product identifier (ePID). Identifies a product in the eBay product catalog.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: Not returned in CSV response<br><br>XML:  <br>`productDetails.productVersion` | For XML only, the version number of the catalog product definition. This version number is incremented each time catalog product is updated |
| CSV: `GTIN`<br><br>XML: **type** attribute value of `GTIN` for `productDetails.productIdentifier` | Global Trade Item Number (GTIN). If a EAN, ISBN, or UPC exists for the catalog product, the values are returned (from EAN, ISBN, and UPC). For CSV, any GTIN and Brand/MPN associated with the catalog product is also returned in the applicable column.<br><br>For XML, any GTIN and Brand/MPN associated with the catalog product is returned with the type of identifier indicated through the type attribute value (see [Sample type attribute](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-response-product-search-feed.html#Sample)<br>).<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `UPC`<br><br>XML: **type** attribute value of `UPC` for `productDetails.productIdentifier` | If a Universal Product Codes (UPCs) exists for the catalog product, it will be returned<br><br>For an XML sample, see the [Sample type attribute.](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-response-product-search-feed.html#Sample)<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `ISBN`<br><br>XML: **type** attribute value of `ISBN` for `productDetails.productIdentifier` | If an International Standard Book Numbers (ISBNs) exists for the catalog product, it will be returned<br><br>For an XML sample, see the [Sample type attribute.](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-response-product-search-feed.html#Sample)<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `EAN`<br><br>XML: **type** attribute value of `EAN` for `productDetails.productIdentifier` | If a European Article Numbers (EANs) exists for the catalog product, it will be returned<br><br>For an XML sample, see the [Sample type attribute.](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-response-product-search-feed.html#Sample)<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Brand`<br><br>XML: **type** attribute value of `BRAND` for `productDetails.productIdentifier` | The manufacturer's brand name of the product. It is always returned if an MPN exists for the product. The Brand/MPN combo unique identifies a product.<br><br>For an XML sample, see the [Sample type attribute.](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-response-product-search-feed.html#Sample)<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `MPN`<br><br>XML: **type** attribute value of `MPN` for `productDetails.productIdentifier` | If a Manufacturer Product Number (MPN) values that the manufacturer uses to identify this product exists for the catalog product. It is always returned if an MPN exists for the product. The Brand/MPN combo unique identifies a product.<br><br>For an XML sample, see the [Sample type attribute.](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-response-product-search-feed.html#Sample)<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `primaryCategory`<br><br>XML: `productDetails.primaryCategory` | The main eBay leaf category for this product.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `additionalCategory`<br><br>XML: `productDetails.additionalCategory` | This field is returned if the eBay catalog product can be listed in additional leaf categories besides the one indicated by **primaryCategory** field. One or more eBay categories associated with the product, not including the primary category, is returned.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `productURL`<br><br>XML: `productDetails.productURL` | The URL to the matching product’s product page on eBay.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `title`<br><br>XML: `productDetails.title` | The title of the product.<br><br>**Max length:** 80 characters<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `Attributes`<br><br>XML: `productDetails.attribute` container | Name-value pairs of product attributes. An attribute is often referred to an Item Specific or a product aspect. Example values include Brand, Model, Type, Color, and Features. The number of defined attributes vary by category, and some categories have much more than others. Some item specifics can have multiple values.<br><br>**CSV**<br><br>CSV uses the following syntax for name/value pairs: `name1=value1\|name2=value2\|name3=value3`.<br><br>Brand=Apple\|MPN=MGAL2LLA\|Processor=Dual Core<br><br>**XML**<br><br>For XML, an attribute container is included for each item specific name/value pair.<br><br><attribute><br>	<name>Brand</name><br>	<value>Apple</value><br></attribute><br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `PictureURL`<br><br>XML: `productDetails.pictureURL` | A stock photo defined for the catalog product. Most catalog products have at least one stock photo.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `description`<br><br>XML: `productDetails.description` | The description of the product, as defined in the eBay catalog product definition. Most catalog products have a description defined.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Message Type`<br><br>XML: `messageInfo.messageType` | The type of error or warning. This field is only returned if there was an issue with the product search.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Message ID`<br><br>XML: `messageInfo.messageID` | The unique identifier of the error or warning. This field is only returned if there was an issue with the product search.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Message`<br><br>XML: `messageInfo.message` | The actual text of the error or warning. This field is only returned if there was an issue with the product search. This field can include information about errors and how to correct them.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |

Sample type attribute (XML)
---------------------------

If an identifier exists for the catalog product, it will be returned as a **type** in the **productDetails.productIdentifier** container.

<productDetails>
	<channelID>EBAY\_US</channelID>
	<ePID>27056252015</ePID>
	<productVersion>6</productVersion>
	<productIdentifier type="BRAND">Apple</productIdentifier>
	<productIdentifier type="MPN">MQ903LLA</productIdentifier>
	<productIdentifier type="GTIN">0194253378877</productIdentifier>
	<productIdentifier type="GTIN">0400064872665</productIdentifier>
	<productIdentifier type="UPC">0194253378877</productIdentifier>
	<productIdentifier type="UPC">0400064872665</productIdentifier>
	<productIdentifier type="EAN">0194253378877</productIdentifier>
	<productIdentifier type="EAN">0400064872665</productIdentifier>
</productDetails>

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

.

# Payment method | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/add-payment-method.html#mainContent)

eBay automatically specifies the available electronic payment methods available for each marketplace.

However, if a new listing supports (or requires) an offline payment, sellers must specify one or more offline payment methods for the listing.

**Argument:** [Item.PaymentMethods](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html#Request.Item.PaymentMethods)

Determine offline payment methods for a category
------------------------------------------------

Use [GetCategoryFeatures](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/XML/docs/Reference/eBay/GetCategoryFeatures.html)
 to determine the offline payment methods that are allowed for a category on a marketplace. Offline payment methods will vary slightly per marketplace, but common methods include:

*   `CashOnPickup`
*   `MOCC` (Money Order or Cashier's/Bank Check)
*   `PersonalCheck`

For additional information about payment methods, refer to [Payment methods policy](https://www.ebay.com/help/policies/payment-policies/accepted-payments-policy?id=4269)
.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.

# ProductRequest - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ProductRequest
==============

Container for request data used in product search calls.

Type that uses ProductRequest:

[FindProductsRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/FindProductsRequest.html)

Call that uses ProductRequest:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)

Fields
------

[categoryId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html#categoryId)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[dataset](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html#dataset)
 ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ )

[datasetPropertyName](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html#datasetPropertyName)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[invocationId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html#invocationId)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[keywords](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html#keywords)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[paginationInput](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html#paginationInput)
 ( _[PaginationInput](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/PaginationInput.html)
_ )

[productStatusFilter](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html#productStatusFilter)
 ( _[ProductStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductStatus.html)
_ )

[propertyFilter](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html#propertyFilter)
 ( _[PropertyValue](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/PropertyValue.html)
_ )

[sortOrder](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductRequest.html#sortOrder)
 ( _[SortOrder](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/SortOrder.html)
_ )

  
  

categoryId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Specifies the category from which you want to retrieve product details. When categoryId is used with findProducts, the category must be catalog- enabled, or it will contain no product data, and the request will fail. When categoryId is used with findProductsByCompatibility, the category must be compatibility-enabled. A category must be catalog-enabled to be compatibility-enabled.  
  
Your request must contain categoryId and/or keywords.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#categoryId)
 to learn which calls use categoryId.

  

dataset ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This will define what are properties that will be returned for this catelog-enabled category.

**Dataset values:**

DisplayableProductDetails

Properies for use in a user interface to describe products.

DisplayableSearchResults

Properties for use in results for product searches.

Searchable

Properties for use in searches.

Sortable

Properties that are suitable for sorting.

User can either enter dataset name or enter individual property names (datasetPropertyName) from the dataset. Only one should be preset at a time.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#dataset)
 to learn which calls use dataset.

  

datasetPropertyName ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Individual property name from the dataset. You can either enter dataset name or enter individual property names from the dataset. Only one should be preset at a time.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#datasetPropertyName)
 to learn which calls use datasetPropertyName.

  

invocationId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A unique identifer for a given product request. Use invocationId to prevent the submission of duplicate data. In the event of a partial failure, the invocation ID can also be used to identify a product request that did not succeed.  
  
If the same invocationId is passed in more than once in the same request, then an error will be returned. The identifier must be a number .

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#invocationId)
 to learn which calls use invocationId.

  

keywords ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Use keywords to refine your product search query. Products whose catalog searchable property values contain these keywords will be returned. Returned products must match both the specified keywords and the name- value pairs constraint.  
  
Keyword searches are not case sensitive. Keywords are used for exact word matching only, wildcards or search string modifiers are not supported.  
  
Your request must contain categoryId and/or keywords.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#keywords)
 to learn which calls use keywords.

  

paginationInput ( _[PaginationInput](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/PaginationInput.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Controls the pagination of the result set. Child elements specify the maximum number of products to return per call and the page of data to return. Controls the number of products returned in the response, but does not specify the amount of data to return for each product.  
  
**Note:** No more than 10,000 products can be retrieved for a given search, regardless of how many matches are found. This limit is enforced by the maximum page number allowed (100) and the maximum entries per page allowed (20).

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#paginationInput)
 to learn which calls use paginationInput.

  

productStatusFilter ( _[ProductStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ProductStatus.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Specifies whether or not to filter out the products which are disabled for selling, product reviews, or use on half.com.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#productStatusFilter)
 to learn which calls use productStatusFilter.

  

propertyFilter ( _[PropertyValue](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/PropertyValue.html)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

One or more name-value pairs that describe the product.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#propertyFilter)
 to learn which calls use propertyFilter.

  

sortOrder ( _[SortOrder](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/SortOrder.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Sort Order for the result set. Any of the searchable property can be used to specify the sort order. If no sort order is specified then default sort order(popularity descending) will be applied.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#sortOrder)
 to learn which calls use sortOrder.

---

# CaseSearchResponse - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

CaseSearchResponse
==================

The base response type of the **GET /post-order/v2/casemanagement/search** method, which is used to retrieve cases based on filter criteria in the request.

Call that uses CaseSearchResponse:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement_search__get.html)

Fields
------

members ( array of _[CaseSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/CaseSummaryType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

An array of one or more cases that match the filter criteria in request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#members)
 to learn which calls use members.

  

paginationOutput ( _[PaginationOutput](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/PaginationOutput.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container consists of metadata related to the current page of results. It shows the total number of cases that match the filter criteria, the total number of cases to show per page of results, and the total number of pages in the results set.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#paginationOutput)
 to learn which calls use paginationOutput.

  

totalNumberOfCases ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This integer value indicates the total number of cases that were found based on the input filter(s) in the request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#totalNumberOfCases)
 to learn which calls use totalNumberOfCases.

---

# ReturnCountFilterEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnCountFilterEnum
=====================

This enumerated type lists all values that can be passed in the **return\_state** query parameter of the **GET /post-order/v2/return/search** method. These values specify the state of the return requests returned by a **search** request. These return state classifications will also get returned in the **countSummary** array in the **GET /post-order/v2/return/search** response.

Type that uses ReturnCountFilterEnum:

[CountSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CountSummaryType.html)

Call that uses one or more values of ReturnCountFilterEnum:

[/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| ALL\_OPEN | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve all open return requests. |
| ALL\_OPEN\_REPLACEMENT | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve all open return requests where the buyer has requested a replacement item.<br><br>**Note:** This value is not currently supported. |
| ALL\_OPEN\_RETURN | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve all open return requests where the buyer wants to return the item for a refund. |
| CLOSED | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve all return requests that have been closed. |
| EBAY\_PLUS | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve all open return requests where the line item is an eBay Plus item. |
| ITEM\_DELIVERED | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve return requests where a return item has been delivered to their destination. |
| ITEM\_SHIPPED | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve return requests where a return item has been shipped. |
| RETURN\_STARTED | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve newly-opened return requests. |
| SELLER\_ACTION\_DUE | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve all open return requests that are currently awaiting a seller action. |
| SELLER\_ACTION\_OVERDUE | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve all open return requests where a seller action is overdue. |
| SELLER\_NO\_ACTION | This enumeration value is set in the **return\_state** query parameter field if the user wishes to retrieve all open return requests where no seller action is currently due. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value should not be used in the **return\_state** query parameter field. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# EscalationInfoType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

EscalationInfoType
==================

This type is used by the **escalationInfo** container, which contains information related to the buyer's and seller's eligibility to escalate a return request to a case.

Type that uses EscalationInfoType:

[ReturnSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnSummaryType.html)

Calls that use EscalationInfoType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

buyerEscalationEligibilityInfo ( _[EscalationEligibilityInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/EscalationEligibilityInfo.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container indicates if the buyer is eligible to escalate the return request into a return case. If the buyer is eligible to escalate the return request into a return case, the time window during which this buyer must perform this action is returned under this container.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#buyerEscalationEligibilityInfo)
 to learn which calls use buyerEscalationEligibilityInfo.

  

caseId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The unique eBay-assigned ID of an eBay case. This field will only be returned if the return request was successfully escalated to a return case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#caseId)
 to learn which calls use caseId.

  

sellerEscalationEligibilityInfo ( _[EscalationEligibilityInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/EscalationEligibilityInfo.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container indicates if the seller is eligible to escalate the return request into a return case. If the seller is eligible to escalate the return request into a return case, the time window during which this seller must perform this action is returned under this container.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#sellerEscalationEligibilityInfo)
 to learn which calls use sellerEscalationEligibilityInfo.

---

, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

category ( _[ErrorCategory](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ErrorCategory.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

There are three categories of errors: request errors, application errors, and system errors.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#category)
 to learn which calls use category.

  

domain ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Name of the domain in which the error occurred.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#domain)
 to learn which calls use domain.

  

errorId ( _[long](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A unique code that identifies the particular error condition that occurred. Your application can use error codes as identifiers in your customized error-handling algorithms.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#errorId)
 to learn which calls use errorId.

  

exceptionId ( _[token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Unique identifier for an exception associated with an error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#exceptionId)
 to learn which calls use exceptionId.

  

message ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

A detailed description of the condition that caused the error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#message)
 to learn which calls use message.

  

parameter ( _[ErrorParameter](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ErrorParameter.html)
 ([string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
)_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Various warning and error messages return one or more variables that contain contextual information about the error. This is often the field or value that triggered the error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#parameter)
 to learn which calls use parameter.

  

severity ( _[ErrorSeverity](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ErrorSeverity.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Indicates whether the reported problem is fatal (an error) or is less severe (a warning). Review the error message details for information on the cause.  
  
If the request fails and the application is the source of the error (for example, a required element is missing), update the application before you retry the request. If the problem is due to incorrect user data, alert the end user to the problem and provide the means for them to correct the data. Once the problem in the application or data is resolved, resend the request to eBay.  
  
If the source of the problem is on eBay's side, you can retry the request a reasonable number of times (eBay recommends you try the request twice). If the error persists, contact Developer Technical Support. Once the problem has been resolved, you can resend the request in its original form.  
  
If a warning occurs, warning information is returned in addition to the business data. Normally, you do not need to resend the request (as the original request was successful). However, depending on the cause of the warning, you might need to contact the end user, or eBay, to effect a long term solution to the problem.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#severity)
 to learn which calls use severity.

  

subdomain ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Name of the subdomain in which the error occurred.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#subdomain)
 to learn which calls use subdomain.

---

# Quantity | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/quantity.html#mainContent)

A fixed-price listing may offer one item or a multiple quantity of items. A fixed-price listing ends when the last item has been sold.

An exception to this rule involves using the _Out-of-Stock_ feature. If this feature is enabled for the seller's account, fixed-price listings will remain active but hidden from search results even if the quantity has gone down to `0`. Refer to [Out-of-Stock Control](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/out-of-stock.html)
 for additional information.

**Arguments:**

*   **Quantity for _single-variation_ listings:** [Item.Quantity](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html#Request.Item.Quantity)
    
*   **Quantity for a specific variation in _multiple variation_ listings:** [Item.Variations.Variation.Quantity](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/XML/docs/Reference/eBay/AddFixedPriceItem.html#Request.Item.Variations.Variation.Quantity)
    

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.

---

# ShipmentType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ShipmentType
============

This type is used to provide detailed information on shipment of a return item.

Type that uses ShipmentType:

[ReturnDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnDetailType.html)

Calls that use ShipmentType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

allShipmentTrackings ( array of _[ShipmentTrackingType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container is an array of shipment tracking information for a return item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#allShipmentTrackings)
 to learn which calls use allShipmentTrackings.

  

payee ( _[ReturnUserRoleEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnUserRoleEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates whether the buyer or seller will be paying for shipment of item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#payee)
 to learn which calls use payee.

  

shipmentTracking ( _[ShipmentTrackingType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides detailed information on the whereabouts of a shipped item, including tracking number, origin and destination address, shipping status, estimated and actual shipping and delivery dates, and more.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#shipmentTracking)
 to learn which calls use shipmentTracking.

  

shippingLabelCost ( _[ReturnShippingCostDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnShippingCostDetailType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides the itemized list of costs associated with shipping the item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#shippingLabelCost)
 to learn which calls use shippingLabelCost.

# ReturnEligibilityItemPerCheckTypeResult - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnEligibilityItemPerCheckTypeResult
=======================================

This type is used by the **eligibilityResultsPerCheckType** container, which contains the results for each eligibility check type that was run against the order line item.

Type that uses ReturnEligibilityItemPerCheckTypeResult:

[ReturnEligibilityPerItemType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ReturnEligibilityPerItemType.html)

Call that uses ReturnEligibilityItemPerCheckTypeResult:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)

Fields
------

checkType ( _[ret:EligibilityCheckTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ret_EligibilityCheckTypeEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumerated value indicates the type of eligibility check that was run against the order line item. For the **POST /post-order/v2/return/check\_eligibility** method, multiple eligibility checks can be run against the order line item, but the **POST /post-order/v2/return** method only uses the `RETURN_CREATION` check.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#checkType)
 to learn which calls use checkType.

  

eligibilityInfo ( _[EligibilityResultType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/EligibilityResultType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container includes the results of the return eligibility check, including any errors that may have occurred with the eligibility check, as well as detailed information on the return request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#eligibilityInfo)
 to learn which calls use eligibilityInfo.

---

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)

-----

_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides detailed information about a buyer refund that was issued, or is scheduled to be issued for a cancelled order.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#actualRefundDetail)
 to learn which calls use actualRefundDetail.

# UserPartyEnum - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

UserPartyEnum
=============

This enumerated type lists the types of users who can possibly perform an action on an INR inquiry or a case.

Types that use UserPartyEnum:

[CaseDetailsResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/CaseDetailsResponse.html)
, [ClaimHistoryEventModel](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/ClaimHistoryEventModel.html)
, [InquiryDetailsResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/InquiryDetailsResponse.html)

Calls that use one or more values of UserPartyEnum:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| BUYER | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action is the buyer. |
| CSR | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action is an eBay customer support representative. |
| SELLER | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action is the seller. |
| SYSTEM | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action is the eBay system. |
| UNKNOWN | This enumeration value indicates that the initiator of the action cannot be determined. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# ActualRefundDetail - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ActualRefundDetail
==================

This type is used to provide details about buyer refund information for an order cancellation request.

Type that uses ActualRefundDetail:

[RefundInfo](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundInfo.html)

Call that uses ActualRefundDetail:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)

Fields
------

[actualRefund](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetail.html#actualRefund)
 ( _[ActualRefund](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefund.html)
_ )

[fundings](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetail.html#fundings)
 ( array of _[RefundFundingType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundFundingType.html)
_ )

[netChargeAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetail.html#netChargeAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

[outstandingAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetail.html#outstandingAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

[refundIssuedDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetail.html#refundIssuedDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[refundStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetail.html#refundStatus)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

actualRefund ( _[ActualRefund](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefund.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the total amount of the buyer refund that has been issued for the cancelled order. If a refund has not occured yet, this container will not be returned.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#actualRefund)
 to learn which calls use actualRefund.

  

fundings ( array of _[RefundFundingType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundFundingType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This array shows one or more seller payment sources used for the buyer refund.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fundings)
 to learn which calls use fundings.

  

netChargeAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the total amount charged to the seller for this refund. The value will be in the seller's default currency.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#netChargeAmount)
 to learn which calls use netChargeAmount.

  

outstandingAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows any outstanding dollar amount that is still due to the buyer for the refund. If a full refund has been successfully issued to the buyer, this value should be `0.0`.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#outstandingAmount)
 to learn which calls use outstandingAmount.

  

refundIssuedDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates the date and time when the refund was issued to the buyer. This field will not be returned if a buyer refund has not occurred yet.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundIssuedDate)
 to learn which calls use refundIssuedDate.

  

refundStatus ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the current status of the refund for the cancelled order. The value in this field should be `SUCCESS` if the refund has been successfully issued to the buyer.  
  
The list of enumeration values that may be returned in this field are defined in the [MoneyMovementStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/types/can_MoneyMovementStatusEnum.html)
 type definition.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundStatus)
 to learn which calls use refundStatus.

---

# ErrorMessage - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)

*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ErrorMessage
============

Information regarding an error or warning that occurred when eBay processed the request. Not returned when the **ack** value is **Success**.

Type that uses ErrorMessage:

[BaseResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/BaseResponse.html)

Calls that use ErrorMessage:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [consolidateShippingProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/consolidateShippingProfiles.html)
, [getConsolidationJobStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getConsolidationJobStatus.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [getVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getVersion.html)
, [removeOverrides](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/removeOverrides.html)
, [removeProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/removeProfile.html)
, [removeSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/removeSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Field
-----

error ( _[ErrorData](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Details about a single error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#error)
 to learn which calls use error.

# Add a picture | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/add-picture.html#mainContent)

Pictures make an item more appealing and provide buyers a better idea of an item's appearance.

At least one picture is required for each listing, however up to 24 pictures may be added to any listing in most categories at no cost.

**Note:** When posting a motor vehicle listing, the number of pictures included depends on the selected vehicle package. For complete information, refer to [Package pricing for vehicle listings](https://www.ebay.com/help/selling/fees-credits-invoices/motors-fees?id=4127#section1)
.

For additional information and requirements about adding pictures to listings, refer to [Add pictures](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/pictures.html)
.

For information about adding other media types to listings, refer to [Add media](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/media.html)
.

**Arguments:**

*   [Item.PictureDetails.PictureURL](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html#Request.Item.PictureDetails.PictureURL)
     specifies the URL for a picture.

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.

---

# Item location | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/location.html#mainContent)

When listing an item, sellers must specify the country in which the item is located by passing in a two-character code using the [Item.Country](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html#Request.Item.Country)
 field.

To determine which country codes are currently supported, issue a [GeteBayDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/GeteBayDetails.html)
 call. In addition to the supported codes, the English name associated with each code is also returned. For example:

<CountryDetails>
  <Country>AU</Country>
  <Description>Australia</Description>
  <DetailVersion>1</DetailVersion>
  <UpdateTime>2009-07-01T23:51:09.000Z</UpdateTim>
</CountryDetails>
...
<CountryDetails>
  <Country>KY</Country>
  <Description>Cayman Islands</Description>
  <DetailVersion>1</DetailVersion>
  <UpdateTime>2009-07-01T23:51:09.000Z</UpdateTime>
</CountryDetails>

**Note:** Most country codes used by eBay conform to the ISO 3166 standard (e.g., `US` for the United States). However, some codes in the ISO 3166 standard are not used by eBay. Additionally, the eBay list includes some non-ISO codes as well. These additional codes appear at the end of the code list and are noted as being non-ISO.

Specify the item location
-------------------------

In addition to specifying the country code for an item (i.e., using the [Item.Country](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html#Request.Item.Country)
 field,) sellers must further specify the item's location. Not only will this improve search results for buyers looking for items within a specified area, but the information may required when calculating shipping costs (e.g., calculated shipping option).

An item's location is specified using either of the following fields:

*   [Item.PostalCode](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html#Request.Item.PostalCode)
    
*   [Item.Location](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html#Request.Item.Location)
    
    **Item.Location** is a free-form text field with a maximum length of 45 characters. It is often used to indicate a City/State combination. For example:
    
    <Location>San Jose, CA</Location>
    

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.

# can:MoneyMovementDetailType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

can:MoneyMovementDetailType
===========================

This type is used to express details about all monetary transactions that may occur with an order cancellation request.

Type that uses can:MoneyMovementDetailType:

[CancelDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CancelDetail.html)

Call that uses can:MoneyMovementDetailType:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/{cancelId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation-cancelid__get.html)

Fields
------

[actualAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/can_MoneyMovementDetailType.html#actualAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

[creationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/can_MoneyMovementDetailType.html#creationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[moneyMovementType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/can_MoneyMovementDetailType.html#moneyMovementType)
 ( _[can:MoneyMovementTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/can_MoneyMovementTypeEnum.html)
_ )

[requestedAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/can_MoneyMovementDetailType.html#requestedAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

[status](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/can_MoneyMovementDetailType.html#status)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

  
  

actualAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the actual amount of the monetary transaction. The **actualAmount** field will only show up if the monetary transaction was confirmed to be successful (corresponding **status** value shows as `SUCCESS`. If the **status** value shows any value other than `SUCCESS`, the expected amount of the monetary transaction can be seen in the **requestedAmount** field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#actualAmount)
 to learn which calls use actualAmount.

  

creationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This timestamp indicates the date and time when the monetary transaction occurred.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#creationDate)
 to learn which calls use creationDate.

  

moneyMovementType ( _[can:MoneyMovementTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/can_MoneyMovementTypeEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the type of monetary transaction that is either in the process of being executed or has already executed.  
  
Monetary transactions include a seller's partial or full refund to the buyer, eBay refunding the buyer, or eBay recouping money from the seller for a buyer refund issued by eBay.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#moneyMovementType)
 to learn which calls use moneyMovementType.

  

requestedAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the expected amount of the transaction.  
  
This field is returned regardless of the value shown in the **corresponding **status** field. The **actualAmount** field displays only when the monetary transaction was confirmed to be successful (**status** value is `SUCCESS`.**

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#requestedAmount)
 to learn which calls use requestedAmount.

  

status ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the success or failure of the monetary transaction type (noted in the **moneyMovementType** field)  
  
The list of enumeration values that may be returned in this field are defined in the [MoneyMovementStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order//devzone/post-order/types/can_MoneyMovementStatusEnum.html)
 type definition.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#status)
 to learn which calls use status.

---

# ActualRefundDetailType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ActualRefundDetailType
======================

This type is used by the **actualRefundDetail** container, which shows the itemized refund information after the seller issues a refund to the buyer. Until the refund is actually successfully processed, only information on the estimated refund may be seen in the **estimatedRefundDetail** container.

Type that uses ActualRefundDetailType:

[RefundInfoType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundInfoType.html)

Calls that use ActualRefundDetailType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

[actualRefund](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html#actualRefund)
 ( _[RefundDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundDetailType.html)
_ )

[fundings](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html#fundings)
 ( array of _[RefundFundingType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundFundingType.html)
_ )

[netChargeAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html#netChargeAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

[outstandingAmount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html#outstandingAmount)
 ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ )

[refundInitiationType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html#refundInitiationType)
 ( _[ReturnRefundInitiatorEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnRefundInitiatorEnum.html)
_ )

[refundIssuedDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html#refundIssuedDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[refundStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html#refundStatus)
 ( _[Refund\_MoneyMovementStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Refund_MoneyMovementStatusEnum.html)
_ )

  
  

actualRefund ( _[RefundDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundDetailType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container consists of one or more **itemizedRefundDetail** containers, as well as the total amount of the actual refund (which is the total of each **itemizedRefundDetail.amount** value). This container is only returned if a refund has been issued for the return request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#actualRefund)
 to learn which calls use actualRefund.

  

fundings ( array of _[RefundFundingType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/RefundFundingType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This array shows more information about how the buyer refund was funded. There are multiple seller payment sources where the money can come from, and it is possible that the money can come from multiple seller payment sources.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fundings)
 to learn which calls use fundings.

  

netChargeAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows the total amount charged to the seller for this refund. The value will be in the seller's default currency.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#netChargeAmount)
 to learn which calls use netChargeAmount.

  

outstandingAmount ( _[Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Amount.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container shows any outstanding dollar amount that is still due to the buyer for the refund. If a full refund has been successfully issued to the buyer, this value should be `0.0`.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#outstandingAmount)
 to learn which calls use outstandingAmount.

  

refundInitiationType ( _[ReturnRefundInitiatorEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnRefundInitiatorEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumerated value indicates the initiator of the refund. `SELLER_INITIATED` indicates that the seller initiated the refund, and `AUTO_REFUND` indicates that the buyer's money was automatically refunded by eBay.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundInitiationType)
 to learn which calls use refundInitiationType.

  

refundIssuedDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the seller issued the refund to the buyer.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundIssuedDate)
 to learn which calls use refundIssuedDate.

  

refundStatus ( _[Refund\_MoneyMovementStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/Refund_MoneyMovementStatusEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The enumeration value in this field indicates whether or not the refund operation was successful. If the `PENDING` value is returned, it indicates that the refund may be occurring outside of eBay's system and there is no way for eBay to check the status of the refund. In this scenario, the seller should call **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/mark\_refund\_sent** to mark the refund as sent and/or the buyer should call **POST /post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/mark\_refund\_received** to mark the refund as received.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#refundStatus)
 to learn which calls use refundStatus.

---

# Delete inventory feed response definitions | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-response-delete-inventory-feed.html#mainContent)

A delete inventory feed response file tells you if all the data in an inventory feed was processed successfully or not. The following table explains the elements or fields in the delete inventory feed response file.

Sample response file:

*   [delete-inventory-response.csv](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/res/Resources/images/mip-user/mip-samples/ebay-response-csv/delete-inventory-response.csv)
    
*   [delete-inventory-response.xml](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/res/Resources/images/mip-user/mip-samples/ebay-response-xml/delete-inventory-response.xml)
    

**Note:** The fields in the table below are ordered according to their order in the CSV response file. The parent is provided for XML fields in order to make it easier to locate that field in the XML response file.

 
| Field name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CSV: `SKU`<br><br>XML: `productDetail.SKU` | The product's Stock Keeping Unit.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `Action`<br><br>XML: `productDetail.action` | The action requested. Valid values:<br><br>*   `DeleteListing`: Delete one or more listings from specified eBay sites, but the SKUs and associated information remain in MIP.<br>*   `DeleteInventory`: Delete one or more listings from all eBay sites, as well as the SKUs and associated information in MIP.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `Channel ID`<br><br>XML: `productDetail.channelID` | Included when the action is DeleteListing. The unique identifiers of the eBay sales channels, such as `EBAY_GB`. Blank if selling only on `EBAY_US`. See [Marketplace enumerated values](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-enum-marketplace.html)<br> for full list..<br><br>ChannelID is used only for the deleteListing option. It is not applicable to the deleteInventory option and will be ignored.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Format`<br><br>XML: `productDetail.format` | The listing format.<br><br>A string with this valid value:<br><br>*   `FIXED_PRICE`<br>    <br>*   `AUCTION`<br>    <br><br>**Occurrence**: Optional |
| CSV: `Status`<br><br>XML: `productDetail.status` | Returned for each individual SKU. Status results include:<br><br>*   `SUCCESS`: Indicates the specific SKUs that were deleted.<br>*   `FAILED`: Indicates the specific SKUs that were not deleted.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `Message Type`<br><br>XML: `messageInfo.messageType` | The type of error or warning. This field is only returned if there was an issue creating or updating the inventory.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Message ID`<br><br>XML: `messageInfo.messageID` | The unique identifier of the error or warning. This field is only returned if there was an issue creating or updating the inventory.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Message`<br><br>XML: `messageInfo.message` | The actual text of the error or warning. This field is only returned if there was an issue creating or updating the inventory. This field can include information about errors and how to correct them.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |

Additional container in XML response
------------------------------------

In addition to the fields called out in the table above, the feed response file will also have a **feedSummary** container. See [XML feedSummary container](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-overview-response-files.html#XML)
 for details.

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.
