# ReturnFileType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ReturnFileType
==============

This type is used by the **files** container, which contains detailed information about one or more files that are submitted and associated with a return request or return request draft.

Types that use ReturnFileType:

[GetFileResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/GetFileResponse.html)
, [ReturnDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnDetailType.html)

Calls that use ReturnFileType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId}/files GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid_files__get.html)

Fields
------

[creationDate](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#creationDate)
 ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ )

[fileData](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#fileData)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[fileFormat](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#fileFormat)
 ( _[FileFormatEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FileFormatEnum.html)
_ )

[fileId](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#fileId)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[fileName](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#fileName)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[filePurpose](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#filePurpose)
 ( _[FilePurposeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FilePurposeEnum.html)
_ )

[fileStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#fileStatus)
 ( _[FileStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FileStatusEnum.html)
_ )

[resizedFileData](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#resizedFileData)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[secureUrl](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#secureUrl)
 ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ )

[submitter](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnFileType.html#submitter)
 ( _[ReturnUserRoleEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnUserRoleEnum.html)
_ )

  
  

creationDate ( _[DateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/DateTime.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The timestamp in this container indicates when the file was attached to the return request or return request draft.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#creationDate)
 to learn which calls use creationDate.

  

fileData ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is the base64-encoded binary representation of the file associated with the return request or return request draft. This field is always returned for each file that is attached to a return request or return request draft.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fileData)
 to learn which calls use fileData.

  

fileFormat ( _[FileFormatEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FileFormatEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the file type (like .GIF or .JPG). This field is always returned for each file that is attached to a return request or return request draft.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fileFormat)
 to learn which calls use fileFormat.

  

fileId ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This is the unique eBay-assigned ID of a file that is associated with the return request or return request draft. This field is always returned for each file that is attached to a return request or return request draft.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fileId)
 to learn which calls use fileId.

  

fileName ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field contains the name of the uploaded file. This field will be returned if the user has specified a name for the file.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fileName)
 to learn which calls use fileName.

  

filePurpose ( _[FilePurposeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FilePurposeEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the file's purpose. Files attached to return requests or return request drafts may provide more information about the condition of the item, about the buyer's refund, or about shipment tracking or shipping label. This field is always returned for each file that is attached to a return request or draft.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#filePurpose)
 to learn which calls use filePurpose.

  

fileStatus ( _[FileStatusEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/FileStatusEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates the current status of the file. This field is always returned for each file that is attached to a return request or return request draft.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#fileStatus)
 to learn which calls use fileStatus.

  

resizedFileData ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is the base64-encoded binary representation of the file associated with the return request or return request draft. This field is returned for each file that is attached to a return request or return request draft that has been resized.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#resizedFileData)
 to learn which calls use resizedFileData.

  

secureUrl ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field contains the URL where the uploaded file can be viewed.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#secureUrl)
 to learn which calls use secureUrl.

  

submitter ( _[ReturnUserRoleEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnUserRoleEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates which party (buyer or seller) submitted the file. This field is always returned for each file that is attached to a return request or return request draft.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#submitter)
 to learn which calls use submitter.

# VersionDetails - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

VersionDetails
==============

Details about the current version of the metadata used for searching for compatibilities or products.

Type that uses VersionDetails:

[GetProductSearchDataVersionResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/GetProductSearchDataVersionResponse.html)

Call that uses VersionDetails:

[getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion.html)

Fields
------

updateTime ( _[dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Indicates the date and time the current search metadata was updated.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#updateTime)
 to learn which calls use updateTime.

  

version ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Version number for the current search metadata (product or compatibility). If you are maintaining search metadata locally, compare this value to the version of the search metadata you last downloaded to determine whether the data may have changed.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#version)
 to learn which calls use version.

# Locale enumerated values | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-enum-locale.html#mainContent)

This topic defines enumerated values that specify the language used for data in the listing.

 
| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `en_US` | The content is localized for US English. |
| `en_CA` | The content is localized for Canadian English. |
| `fr_CA` | The content is localized for Canadian French. |
| `en_GB` | The content is localized for UK English. |
| `en_AU` | The content is localized for Australian English. |
| `en_IN` | The content is localized for English but on the India site.<br><br>**Note:** eBay India is no longer a functioning eBay marketplace |
| `de_AT` | The content is localized for German speakers on the Austria site. |
| `fr_BE` | The content is localized for French speakers on the Belgium site. |
| `fr_FR` | The content is localized for French speakers on the France site. |
| `de_DE` | The content is localized for German speakers on the Germany site. |
| `it_IT` | The content is localized for Italian speakers on the Italy site. |
| `nl_BE` | The content is localized for Dutch speakers on the Belgium site. |
| `nl_NL` | The content is localized for Dutch speakers on the Netherlands site. |
| `es_ES` | The content is localized for Spanish speakers on the Spain site. |
| `de_CH` | The content is localized for Germany speakers on the Switzerland site. |
| `fi_FI` | The content is localized for Finnish speakers. |
| `zh_HK` | The content is localized for Chinese speakers on the Hong Kong site. |
| `hu_HU` | The content is localized for Hungarian speakers. |
| `en_PH` | The content is localized for English speakers on the Philippines site. |
| `pl_PL` | The content is localized for Polish speakers on the Poland site. |
| `pt_PT` | The content is localized for Portuguese speakers. |
| `ru_RU` | The content is localized for Russian speakers. |
| `en_SG` | The content is localized for English on the Singapore site. |
| `en_IE` | The content is localized for English on the Ireland site. |
| `en_MY` | The content is localized for English on the Malaysia site. |

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.

# ErrorMessage - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ErrorMessage
============

Information regarding an error or warning that occurred when eBay processed the request. Not returned when the ack value is Success. Run-time errors are not reported here, but are instead reported as part of a SOAP fault.

Type that uses ErrorMessage:

[BaseServiceResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/BaseServiceResponse.html)

Calls that use ErrorMessage:

[getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchNames](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchNames.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

Field
-----

error ( _[ErrorData](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Details about a single error.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#error)
 to learn which calls use error.

---

# FindCancelResponse - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

FindCancelResponse
==================

The base response type of the **GET /post-order/v2/cancellation/search** method. This response returns information about an order cancellation request.  
  
The order cancellation request(s) returned are the ones that match the filter criteria passed in the query parameter(s) of the request.

Call that uses FindCancelResponse:

[/post-order/v2/cancellation/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_cancellation_search__get.html)

Fields
------

cancellations ( array of _[CancelSummary](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CancelSummary.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

An array of one or more order cancellation requests that match the request criteria.  
  
This array is returned as empty if no order cancellation requests match the input criteria.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#cancellations)
 to learn which calls use cancellations.

  

paginationOutput ( _[PaginationOutput](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/PaginationOutput.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The result set from this method is paginated because the request can result in a large number of matches. This container consists of metadata related to the current response page.  
  
It contains the page number and the cancellation entries per page, as well as the total amount of response pages and cancellation entries. This container is not returned if no cancellation requests match the input criteria.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#paginationOutput)
 to learn which calls use paginationOutput.

  

total ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The total number of cancellation requests that match the input criteria. A value of `0` is returned if no cancellation requests match the input criteria.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#total)
 to learn which calls use total.

---

# Delete inventory feed definitions | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-delete-inventory-feed.html#mainContent)

The following table provides definitions for the fields in the delete inventory feed.

Sample feed files:

*   [delete-inventory.csv](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/res/Resources/images/mip-user/mip-samples/ebay-feed-csv/delete-inventory.csv)
    
*   [delete-inventory.xml](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/res/Resources/images/mip-user/mip-samples/ebay-feed-xml/delete-inventory.xml)
    

**Note:** The fields in the table below are ordered according to their order in the CSV feed file. The parent is provided for XML fields in order to make it easier to locate that field in the XML feed file.

 
| Field name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CSV: `SKU`<br><br>XML: `delete.SKU` | **Required.** The product's Stock Keeping Unit (SKU). The SKU must be unique across your entire inventory.<br><br>**Type:** string<br><br>**Maximum length:** 50 characters |
| CSV: `Action`<br><br>XML: `delete.action` | **Required.** The specified delete action with one of the following valid values:<br><br>*   `DeleteListing`: Deletes one or more listings from **the specified** eBay marketplace/sales channel. The SKUs and associated information remain in MIP.<br>    <br>*   `DeleteInventory`: Deletes one or more listings from **all** eBay marketplaces/sales channels. SKUs and associated information are removed from MIP.<br>    <br><br>When the DeleteInventory action deletes a SKU, if the SKU is in an active single-variation listing, the delete action ends the listing.<br><br>If the SKU is in a multiple-variation listing, it won’t end that listing, but it will remove the variation represented by the SKU.<br><br>Therefore, DeleteListing should be used if the SKU is part of an active listing, and DeleteInventory should be used if the SKU is not part of an active listing.<br><br>**Type:** string |
| CSV:`   Channel ID`<br><br>XML:`   delete.channelID` | **Conditionally required, when using the DeleteListing action to specify sales channels.** Because the DeleteInventory action deletes listings from all channels, specifying a channel with this option has no effect.<br><br>The unique identifier of the eBay marketplace/sales channel, such as `EBAY_US`, `EBAY_GB`, or `EBAY_DE`, and others. See [Marketplace enumerated values](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-enum-marketplace.html)<br> for full list. This is where the seller is listing the item.<br><br>**Type:** string |
| CSV: `Format`<br><br>XML: `delete.format` | **Optional.** The listing format.<br><br>A string with this valid value:<br><br>*   `FIXED_PRICE`<br>    <br>*   `AUCTION`<br>    <br><br>**Type:** string |

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.

# RefundStatusType - API Reference - Return Management API

eBay Return Management APIVersion 1.1.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/ReturnManagementAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

RefundStatusType ( [token](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token)
 )
========================================================================================================================

Enumerated type that indicates the current status of a seller's refund to a buyer.

Types that use RefundStatusType:

[ActualRefundDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/ActualRefundDetailType.html)
, [IssueRefundResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/IssueRefundResponse.html)

Calls that use one or more values of RefundStatusType:

[getReturnDetail](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/getReturnDetail.html)
, [issueRefund](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/issueRefund.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| FAILED | This value indicates that the seller's refund to the buyer failed. |
| PENDING | This value indicates that the seller's refund to the buyer is pending. This value is commonly returned if the payment method was eCheck. |
| SUCCESS | This value indicates that the seller's refund to the buyer was successful. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# BaseServiceResponse - API Reference - Product Metadata API

eBay Product Metadata APIVersion 1.7.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

BaseServiceResponse
===================

Base response container for all service operations. Contains error information associated with the request.

Calls that use BaseServiceResponse:

[getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchDataVersion.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchNames](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchNames.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValues](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValues.html)
, [getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/getCompatibilitySearchValuesBulk.html)

Fields
------

ack ( _[AckValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/AckValue.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Indicates whether or not errors or warnings were generated during the processing of the request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#ack)
 to learn which calls use ack.

  

errorMessage ( _[ErrorMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/ErrorMessage.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Description of an error or warning that occurred when eBay processed the request. Not returned if the ack value is Success.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#errorMessage)
 to learn which calls use errorMessage.

  

timestamp ( _[dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This value represents the date and time when eBay processed the request. This value is returned in GMT, the ISO 8601 date and time format (YYYY- MM- DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ). See the "dateTime" type for information about the time format, and for details on converting to and from the GMT time zone.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#timestamp)
 to learn which calls use timestamp.

  

version ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The release version that eBay used to process the request. Developer Technical Support may ask you for the version value if you work with them to troubleshoot issues.  
  
**Note:** The version in use is normally the latest release version, as specified in the release notes. Note that eBay releases the API to international sites about a week after the API version is released to the US site.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/product-metadata/CallRef/fieldindex.html#version)
 to learn which calls use version.

---

# Buy online and pick up in store | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-buy-online-pick-up-in-store.html#mainContent)

The buy online, pick up in store (BOPIS) feature is generally used only by large merchants who have physical stores where products are stocked and buyers can pick them up at the store.

When you are successful at listing an item with the In-Store Pickup feature enabled, prospective buyers within a reasonable distance (25 miles or so) from one of your stores that has stock available will see the "Available for In-Store Pickup" option on the listing, along with information about the closest store that has the item.

Requirements and restrictions
-----------------------------

*   At this time, the In-Store Pickup feature is generally only available to large retail merchants, and can only be applied to multiple-quantity, fixed-price listings.
*   The 'In-Store Pickup' feature is only available on the eBay US site. An equivalent feature, 'Click and Collect' is available on the eBay-UK, eBay-AU, and eBay-DE sites.

Before using this feature, you must:

*   In the US, be eligible for the In-Store Pickup feature to list an item that is eligible for In-Store Pickup
*   In the UK, Australia, and Germany, be eligible for the Click and Collect feature to list an item that is eligible for Click and Collect
*   Have inventory for the product at one or more physical stores tied to your seller account
*   Include your seller-defined SKU for the product(s) in each listing.
    *   In a single-variation listing, enter the SKU value in the Item.SKU field.
    *   In a multiple-variation listing, enter the SKU value(s) in the Item.Variations.Variation.SKU field(s).
*   Set an immediate payment requirement on the item.

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.

# Relist using different API versions | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/relist-api-versions.html#mainContent)

When relisting, you may get an error about certain elements not being specified. It is possible for this to occur when an item is initially listed with an older version and then relisted with a newer version.

There may be little or no mapping from older tags to newer tags and thus the old listing does not contain enough information to be successfully relisted with the newer version (for example, details related to shipping or to item specifics).

One solution would be to relist the item with the older version. However, that requires knowing what version is best, and you might not have been the one who originally listed the item.

Troubleshooting tips:

*   Try relisting the item with a lower version.
*   View or even relist the item via the eBay marketplace; sometimes the marketplace provides information not available via API, information that might give insights into a possible solution.

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

.

# Test/preview a relisting | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/trading-user-guide/relist-preview.html#mainContent)

An application can test/preview the relisting of an item by using [VerifyRelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/VerifyRelistItem.html)
 rather than [RelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/RelistItem.html)
.

[VerifyRelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/VerifyRelistItem.html)
 uses the same inputs, logic, and validation, and returns the same values as [RelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/RelistItem.html)
 without actually relisting the item. By using [VerifyRelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/VerifyRelistItem.html)
, an application can test the item definition for validity as well as preview the estimated fees before the item is actually relisted.

During testing, review the [Errors](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/VerifyRelistItem.html#Response.Errors)
 node returned in the [VerifyRelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/VerifyRelistItem.html)
 response for any indication that a [RelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/RelistItem.html)
 attempt will be unsuccessful. Specifically, review the following fields:

*   [Errors.ShortMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/VerifyRelistItem.html#Response.Errors.ShortMessage)
    
*   [Errors.LongMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/VerifyRelistItem.html#Response.Errors.LongMessage)
    

**Note:** Refer to [VerifyAddItem Returns Success with No Errors, but AddItem Returns Failure with Errors](https://developer.ebay.com/support/kb-article?KBid=912)
, which is based on the [AddItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/AddItem.html)
 and [VerifyAddItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/VerifyAddItem.html)
 calls, for more information.

Once tested, an application can then use the [ItemType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/types/ItemType.html)
 from testing as the input for the [RelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/xml/docs/Reference/eBay/RelistItem.html)
 call to actually relist the item.

For complete information refer to:

*   [RelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/XML/docs/Reference/eBay/RelistItem.html)
     reference documentation
*   [VerifyRelistItem](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/XML/docs/Reference/eBay/VerifyRelistItem.html)
     reference documentation.

# AckValue - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

AckValue ( [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
 )
==================================================================================================================

Indicates whether the error is a severe error (causing the request to fail) or an informational error (a warning) that should be communicated to the user.

Type that uses AckValue:

[BaseResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/BaseResponse.html)

Calls that use one or more values of AckValue:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [consolidateShippingProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/consolidateShippingProfiles.html)
, [getConsolidationJobStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getConsolidationJobStatus.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [getVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getVersion.html)
, [removeOverrides](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/removeOverrides.html)
, [removeProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/removeProfile.html)
, [removeSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/removeSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Enumeration Values
------------------

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Value | Description |
| Failure | eBay encountered a fatal error during the processing of the request, causing the request to fail. When a serious application-level error occurs, the error is returned instead of the business data. |
| PartialFailure | eBay successfully processed the request, but one or more non-fatal errors occurred during the processing. Inspect the message details and resolve any problems before resubmitting the request. |
| Success | eBay successfully processed the request and the business data is returned in the response. Note that it is possible for a response to return Success, but still not contain the expected data in the result. |
| Warning | The request that triggered the error was processed successfully but with one or more warnings. |

  \* See the [Enumeration Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/enumindex.html)
 to see exact use of each enumeration value in the API.

---

# Response files | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-overview-response-files.html#mainContent)

When you upload a feed to eBay, MIP processes the feed file and then generates a response file that confirms what you uploaded and reports whether MIP successfully processed the information.

Commonly used response file types include:

*   Product
*   Availability
*   Distribution
*   Combined

If an error occurs and the upload is not successful, MIP generates an error message that includes detailed information about the error and what caused it. This helps you identify the issue and correct the feed for subsequent upload attempts.

Response files are in the same format as the feed you upload. If your feed is a CSV file, the response file will also be CSV; if your feed is XML, the response file will be XML. See examples in [eBay response file samples](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-sample-files.html#ebay-response-file-samples)
.

Obtaining a response file
-------------------------

You can download response files either with the MIP user interface or with the SFTP file server (see [FTP setup](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-setup-ftp-setup.html)
).

To download response files with the MIP user interface:

1.  On the feed type page (such as Product), locate the table entry for the desired file upload.
2.  In the **Results file** column, click the **Download** link.
3.  If you are prompted to do so, choose whether to open the file in a browser or download and save the file to your computer.

To download response files from the MIP file server:

1.  In the feed type folder (for example, the Product folder), open the Output subfolder.
2.  Locate and open the subfolder labeled with the time stamp that corresponds to the time when you uploaded the feed file.
3.  Locate the response file for the upload. Use the FTP client to download the response file to your computer.

When to use a response file
---------------------------

eBay recommends that you download the response files after each upload to make sure that your listings are complete and displayed as intended.

Contents of a response file
---------------------------

Response files contain these elements:

*   RequestID (available only in XML files): The file identifier. If you encounter errors and need to contact technical support, this number will help them identify the feed file that didn't upload properly.

*   Status: Tells whether each item uploaded successfully or not. If an item has an error or warning, a message will describe the problem encountered.
*   Various elements that are unique to each feed type.

### XML feedSummary container

All XML response files return a **feedSummary** container. See the following sample.

<feedSummary>
    <status>SUCCESS</status>
    <feedID>40777</feedID>
    <feedType>AVAILABILITY</feedType>
    <inputFilename>availability.xml-Jul-20-2023-14-50-06-0700</inputFilename>
    <counts>
        <total>2</total>
        <success>2</success>
        <failed>0</failed>
    </counts>
</feedSummary>

The following table explains the elements in the container.

 
| Element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `status` | The value returned in this field indicates if the submitted feed request file was processed successfully. Value will either be `SUCCESS` or `FAILED`. |
| `feedID` | The unique identifier of the feed request file. Also referred to as the Request ID. |
| `feedType` | The type of feed files used to create or update the eBay listings. Different feed types are used that contain product details, price and quantity, and more. |
| `inputFilename` | The name of the Product feed request file, with a timestamp appended to the end of seller-defined filename.  <br>For example:`   ProductFeed123.xml-Jul-31-2023-13-50-32-0700` |
| `counts` | This container consists of the **counts.total**, **counts.success**, and the **counts.failed** fields. |
| `counts.total` | The total number of SKUs in the feed. |
| `counts.success` | The number of SKUs that were processed successfully. |
| `counts.failed` | The number of SKUs that failed to be processed successfully. |

.

# Marketplace enumerated values | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-enum-marketplace.html#mainContent)

This topic defines enumerated values that indicate the different eBay marketplace sites.

 
| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `EBAY_US` | The eBay Marketplace is the US site (ebay.com). |
| `EBAY_MOTORS` | The eBay Marketplace is the US eBay Motors site (ebay.com/motors). |
| `EBAY_CA` | The eBay Marketplace is the Canada (English) site (ebay.ca). |
| `EBAY_UK` | The eBay Marketplace is the UK site (ebay.co.uk). |
| `EBAY_AU` | The eBay Marketplace is the Australia site (ebay.com.au). |
| `EBAY_AT` | The eBay Marketplace is the Austria site (ebay.at). |
| `EBAY_BE` | The eBay marketplace is the Belgium (Dutch) site (www.ebay.be). |
| `EBAY_FR` | The eBay Marketplace is the France site (ebay.fr). |
| `EBAY_DE` | The eBay Marketplace is the Germany site (ebay.de). |
| `EBAY_IT` | The eBay Marketplace is the Italy site (ebay.it). |
| `EBAY_NL` | The eBay Marketplace is the Netherlands site (ebay.nl). |
| `EBAY_ES` | The eBay Marketplace is the Spain site (ebay.es). |
| `EBAY_CH` | The eBay Marketplace is the Switzerland site (ebay.ch). |
| `EBAY_TW` | The eBay Marketplace is the Taiwan site (ebay.com/tw). |
| `EBAY_CZ` | The eBay Marketplace is the Czech Republic site (ebay.com/sch/Czech-Republic). |
| `EBAY_DK` | The eBay Marketplace is the Denmark site (ebay.com/sch/Denmark). |
| `EBAY_FI` | The eBay Marketplace is the Finland site (ebay.com/sch/Finland). |
| `EBAY_GR` | The eBay Marketplace is the Greece site (ebay.com/sch/Greece). |
| `EBAY_HK` | The eBay Marketplace is the Hong Kong site (ebay.com.hk). |
| `EBAY_HU` | The eBay Marketplace is the Hungary site (ebay.com/sch/Hungary). |
| `EBAY_IN` | The eBay Marketplace is the India site (ebay.in).<br><br>**Note:** eBay India is no longer a functioning eBay marketplace. |
| `EBAY_ID` | The eBay Marketplace is the Indonesia site (id.ebay.com). |
| `EBAY_IE` | The eBay Marketplace is the Ireland site (ebay.ie). |
| `EBAY_IL` | The eBay Marketplace is the Israel site (ebay.com/sch/Israel). |
| `EBAY_MY` | The eBay Marketplace is the Malaysia site (ebay.com/my). |
| `EBAY_NZ` | The eBay Marketplace is the New Zealand site (ebay.com/sch/New-Zealand). |
| `EBAY_NO` | The eBay Marketplace is the Norway site (ebay.com/sch/Norway). |
| `EBAY_PH` | The eBay Marketplace is the Philippines site (ebay.ph). |
| `EBAY_PL` | The eBay Marketplace is the Poland site (ebay.pl). |
| `EBAY_PT` | The eBay Marketplace is the Portugal site (ebay.com/sch/Portugal). |
| `EBAY_PR` | The eBay Marketplace is the Puerto Rico site (ebay.com/sch/Puerto-Rico). |
| `EBAY_RU` | The eBay Marketplace is the Russia site (ebay.com/sch/Russia). |
| `EBAY_SG` | The eBay Marketplace is the Singapore site (ebay.com/sg). |
| `EBAY_ZA` | The eBay Marketplace is the South Africa site (ebay.com/sch/South-Africa). |
| `EBAY_SE` | The eBay Marketplace is the Sweden site (ebay.com/sch/Sweden). |
| `EBAY_TH` | The eBay Marketplace is the Thailand site (export.ebay.co.th). |
| `EBAY_VN` | The eBay Marketplace is the Vietnam site (ebay.vn). |
| `EBAY_CN` | The eBay Marketplace is the China site (ebay.com/sch/China). |
| `EBAY_PE` | The eBay Marketplace is the Peru site (ebay.com/sch/Peru). |
| `EBAY_JP` | The eBay Marketplace is the Japan site (ebay.co.jp). |

# Simple Schema Types - Return Management API

Return Management API

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/ReturnManagementAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Simple Schema Types
===================

The eBay Schema makes use of many simple schema types. Further, some eBay-defined types (e.g., [Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/Amount.html)
) are based on simple schema types.

Except where specified, the data types in the table below follow the conventions for XML Schema simple types as described by the World Wide Web Consortium in [XML Schema Part 2: Datatypes](http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/)
.

| Data Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| anyURI | Specifies a Uniform Resource Identifier (URI), a compact sequence of characters that identifies an abstract or physical resource.<br><br>The anyURI type is used most often to specify Uniform Resource Locators (URLs), such as a web page URL (e.g., ViewItemURL) or a file location URL (e.g., PictureURL), but can also be used for email addresses and other valid URI schemes. |
| boolean | Specifies binary-valued logic (true/false). Possible input values are true, false, 1, and 0. Possible output values are true and false only (not 1 or 0). |
| dateTime | A specific instant of time.<br><br>Date-time values are in the ISO 8601 date and time format. Hours are in 24-hour format (e.g., 2:00:00pm is 14:00:00). Unless otherwise specified, all date-time values are recorded in Universal Coordinated Time (UTC), also known as Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) or Zulu (because the time portion of the time stamp ends with a Z). That is, time values do not reflect the time zone shown on the eBay Web site. Here is an example of the date-time format:<br><br>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ (e.g., 2004-08-04T19:09:02.768Z)<br><br>See [Time Values](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#basics/DataTypes.html#TimeValues)<br> for information about how to convert between local time zones and GMT.<br><br>We use the dateTime data type to convey start and end times, the official eBay time, and other time values. |
| decimal | A decimal value of arbitrary precision.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type decimal can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Decimal values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>A whole number (i.e., without a period) can be passed as a decimal value.<br><br>We typically use the decimal data type to convey values related to units of measure. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type decimal. |
| double | A double-precision 64-bit floating point type.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type double can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Double values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>We typically use the double data type to convey values related to monetary amounts. See AmountType, below.<br><br>In some cases, a whole number (i.e., without a period) can be passed or returned as a double value. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type double. |
| duration | A length of time.<br><br>Duration values are in the ISO 8601 duration format, where P represents "Period", nY represents the number of years, nM the number of months, nD the number of days, T is the date/time separator, nH the number of hours, nM the number of minutes, and nS the number of seconds. So P2DT23H32M51S means "A period of 2 days, 23 hours, 32 minutes, and 51 seconds".<br><br>PnYnMnDTnH nMnS (e.g., P2DT23H32M51S)<br><br>We use the duration data type to convey values like the time left until a listing ends. For ended listings, the time left is P0DT0H0M0S (zero days, zero hours, zero minutes, and zero seconds). |
| float | A single-precision 32-bit floating point type.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type float can contain the digits 0-9, a hyphen to designate negative numbers as needed, and a period (".") for the decimal separator. Float values should never include a thousands separator.<br><br>We typically use the float data type to convey values related to percentages. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type float. |
| int | A 32-bit integer value between -2147483648 and 2147483647.<br><br>In the API, most fields of type int can contain digits 0-9, and hyphen to indicate negative numbers. Some input fields restrict the input to particular values or a range of values. These cases are noted in the associated topic for the specific input field.<br><br>We typically use the int data type for enumerations, counts (e.g., how many items are returned), quantities, certain kinds of IDs (other IDs are strings), and similar concepts. |
| long | A value between -9223372036854775808 and 9223372036854775807. |
| positiveInteger | An integer value greater than or equal to 1. |
| string | A string of characters. All characters in the Unicode character set are supported.<br><br>Character entity references are used for escaping markup-significant characters. For example, use &amp; for the & (ampersand) character, &lt; for the < (less-than sign) character, and &gt; for the > (greater-than sign) character. It is a good idea to escape such characters in requests instead of using a CDATA construct. Responses from eBay do not include CDATA constructs.<br><br>Note that messages from eBay may contain HTML or XML markup within the message text. In this case, the markup elements within the text are escaped (e.g., &lt;!\[CDATA\[ ... \]\]&gt;).<br><br>Some string input fields (e.g., [keywords](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/findItemsByKeywords.html#Request.keywords)<br>) do not have a known set of possible values. For these fields, the documentation specifies the maximum permitted length in characters. If you are using a URL for your request, and you need to use the & (ampersand) character as part of a string value (e.g. Bausch & Lomb in xxxkeywords), use &amp;.<br><br>xs:string is part of the XML schema namespace defined as:<br><br>xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" |
| token | A set of normalized strings.<br><br>In the API, this type of string contains no white space, by convention.<br><br>We typically use token to constrain a field to a specified set of normalized string values. For such a field, we define these values in an enumeration that uses token as its base type. |
| [Amount](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/types/Amount.html) | An eBay-defined complex type for specifying monetary amounts.<br><br>A double value (e.g., 1.00 or 1.0) is meaningful as an amount when accompanied by a specification of the currency, in which case the value specifies the amount in that currency.<br><br>In the API, an object of type Amount expresses both the value (a decimal) and the currency.<br><br>We typically use the Amount data type to specify details such as prices, fees, costs, and payments.<br><br>In some cases, a whole number (i.e., without a period) can be passed or returned as a monetary value. This is necessary to support certain currencies that are only expressed as whole numbers (like the Italian Lira and Spanish Peseta).<br><br>Because a double is used to represent the amount, this also means whole monetary amounts may be returned with only one 0 after the decimal. For example, a dollar value could be returned as 5.0 instead of 5.00 in calls like [findItemsByKeywords](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/return-management/CallRef/findItemsByKeywords.html)<br>. See individual topics for specific restrictions beyond this for fields of type Amount. |

Repeatable (Unbounded) Fields
-----------------------------

If the multiplicity of an element is defined as maxOccurs="unbounded" or a value greater than 1 in the schema, it means you can specify the field multiple times in a request (like you are specifying an array), or eBay can return the field multiple times in the response. In this case, the fields must be contiguous. That is, in a request, you cannot insert other fields between the repeating fields. Otherwise, some of the data will be dropped in an unpredictable manner.

For example, **findItemsByKeywords** can return multiple items. Here is the definition of the **item** element in the **searchResult** schema (SearchResult complexType):

<xs:element name="item" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" type="tns:SearchItem">

In the response, the **item** fields are always contiguous, with no other fields between them:

...
<searchResult count="100">
  <item>
    <itemId>170192529715</itemId>
    ...
  </item>
  <item>
    <itemId>330233123051</itemId>
    ...
  </item>
  <item>
    <itemId>320247274638</itemId>
    ...
  </item>
  ...
</searchResult>
...

If you are using URL-style input or Name-Value Pair input, repeatable fields can be expressed with a comma-separated format **in most cases**.

Multiplicity Notation
---------------------

| Sample XML notation | Corresponding doc notation | Meaning § |
| --- | --- | --- |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" | \[0..\*\] | Zero or more |
| minOccurs="0" | \[0..1\] | Zero or one |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" | \[0..1\] | Zero or one |
| minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="6" | \[0..6\] | Zero to six |

§  While this is the general meaning, be sure to read the description of the particular field: it may state an exception for a particular use.

---

# ShipmentType - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

ShipmentType
============

This type is used to provide detailed information on shipment of a return item.

Type that uses ShipmentType:

[ReturnDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnDetailType.html)

Calls that use ShipmentType:

[/post-order/v2/return POST](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return__post.html)
, [/post-order/v2/return/{returnId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return-returnid__get.html)

Fields
------

allShipmentTrackings ( array of _[ShipmentTrackingType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container is an array of shipment tracking information for a return item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#allShipmentTrackings)
 to learn which calls use allShipmentTrackings.

  

payee ( _[ReturnUserRoleEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnUserRoleEnum.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This enumeration value indicates whether the buyer or seller will be paying for shipment of item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#payee)
 to learn which calls use payee.

  

shipmentTracking ( _[ShipmentTrackingType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ShipmentTrackingType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides detailed information on the whereabouts of a shipped item, including tracking number, origin and destination address, shipping status, estimated and actual shipping and delivery dates, and more.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#shipmentTracking)
 to learn which calls use shipmentTracking.

  

shippingLabelCost ( _[ReturnShippingCostDetailType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnShippingCostDetailType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container provides the itemized list of costs associated with shipping the item.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#shippingLabelCost)
 to learn which calls use shippingLabelCost.

# Provides Return Address - eBay Post-Order API Reference

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

Provides Return Address
=======================

POST /post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/provide\_return\_address
--------------------------------------------------------------------

**Note:** This call has been deprecated and will be decommissioned on March 2, 2026.

This method can be used on behalf of sellers to provide the correct return address to the buyer. If applicable, and requested by the buyer, the seller can also provide the Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) number to the buyer through the request payload.  

This method is not supported in the Sandbox environment.

Input
-----

See also [Samples](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_provide_return_address__post.html#Samples)
.

#### Resource URI (production)

POST https://api.ebay.com/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/provide\_return\_address

#### URI parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required? | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **caseId** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Required | The unique identifier of a case. This URI parameter is required in order to identify the case for which the seller is providing a return address and or an RMA number. The case ID value is passed in as part of the call URI. |

  
  

#### HTTP request headers

All requests made to eBay REST operations require you to provide the `authorization` HTTP header for authentication.  
See [HTTP request headers](https://developer.ebay.com/ebay-rest/content/rest-request-components.html#HTTP)
 for details.

  
  

#### Authorization

This call uses standard authorization tokens. See [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
 for details.

#### Payload model

The following lists _all fields that could be included_ in the request.

#### Request field descriptions

  
  

| Input Container/Field | Type | Occurrence | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **firstName** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | The first name associated with the seller's return address, or if applicable, a business or store name.  <br>  <br>If a first name or business/store name is defined for the return address, this field should be included in the **POST /post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/provide\_return\_address** request. |
| **lastName** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | The last name associated with the seller's return address.  <br>  <br>If a last name is defined for the return address, this field should be included in the **POST /post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/provide\_return\_address** request. |
| **returnAddress** | [Address](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/Address.html) | Required | The full return address is provided in this container. This container is required to be in the **POST /post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId}/provide\_return\_address** request. |
| returnAddress.**addressLine1** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | The first line of the street address. The first line of the street address is always used by containers using the **Address** type. |
| returnAddress.**addressLine2** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | The second line of the street address. This field is not always used, but it should be provided if applicable. It might be used for something like 'Suite Number' or 'Apt Number'. |
| returnAddress.**addressType** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | This enumeration value indicates whether the address is a business, a personal address, PO Box, or other type of address. This field is not always used by containers using the **Address** type.  <br>  <br>The list of enumeration values that may be returned in this field are defined in the [AddressTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/AddressTypeEnum.html)<br> type definition.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**: See [AddressTypeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/AddressTypeEnum.html) |
| returnAddress.**city** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | The city of the address. This field is always used by containers using the **Address** type. |
| returnAddress.**country** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | This enumeration value indicates the country of the address. The three-letter country codes of the ISO 3166-1 Standard are used. This field is always used by containers using the **Address** type.  <br>  <br>The list of two-digit country codes that may be returned in this field are defined in the [CountryCodeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CountryCodeEnum.html)<br> type definition.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**: See [CountryCodeEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/CountryCodeEnum.html) |
| returnAddress.**county** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | The county of the address. This field is not always used, but it should be provided if applicable. |
| returnAddress.**isTransliterated** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Optional | This Boolean field is returned as `true` if language transliteration was required. |
| returnAddress.**nationalRegion** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | This string value indicates a specific region or metropolitan area within a country in which the address resides. Not all countries have defined national regions. |
| returnAddress.**postalCode** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | The postal code of the address. This field is always used by containers using the **Address** type. |
| returnAddress.**script** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | This enumeration value indicates the language that the address has been transliterated into. This field is only returned if transliteration was required (and the **isTransliterated** field is `true`. Transliteration is required for languages that use special characters, such as Japanese, Chinese, or Arabic.  <br>  <br>The list of language enumeration values that may be returned in this field are defined in the [LanguageScriptEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/LanguageScriptEnum.html)<br> type definition.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**: See [LanguageScriptEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/LanguageScriptEnum.html) |
| returnAddress.**stateOrProvince** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | The state or province of the address. This field is always used by containers using the **Address** type. |
| returnAddress  <br>  .**transliteratedFromScript** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | This enumeration value indicates the language that the address has been transliterated from. This field is only returned if transliteration was required (and the **isTransliterated** field is 'true'. Transliteration is required for languages that use special characters, such as Japanese, Chinese, or Arabic.  <br>  <br>The list of language enumeration values that may be returned in this field are defined in the [LanguageScriptEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/LanguageScriptEnum.html)<br> type definition.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**: See [LanguageScriptEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/LanguageScriptEnum.html) |
| returnAddress.**worldRegion** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | This string value indicates the region of the world in which the address resides. This field is not always used by containers using the **Address** type.  <br>  <br>The list of world region enumeration values that may be returned in this field are defined in the [WorldRegionEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/WorldRegionEnum.html)<br> type definition.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**: See [WorldRegionEnum](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/WorldRegionEnum.html) |
| **RMA** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | The Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) number associated with the item to be returned. An RMA number is a unique identifier that is provided by a manufacturer and authorizes the return of an item. This field is conditionally required if there is an RMA number associated with the item, and if the buyer is requesting an RMA number. |

  

Output
------

See also [Samples](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseId_provide_return_address__post.html#Samples)
.

#### Payload model

This call has no response payload.

null

  
  

Samples
-------

New to making API calls? Please see [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
.

Note: Some item IDs, user IDs, or other data in these samples might no longer be active on eBay. If necessary, you can substitute current eBay data in your requests.

Sample: Basic Call

Seller provides an alternate return address to the buyer.

**Description**

The buyer will be returning an item, but the seller does not want the buyer to return the item to the default return address listed for the seller. The seller uses this call to provide the return address where the buyer will ship the return item.

**Input**

The seller passes in the case ID value as a path parameter to identify the case for which an alternate return address is being provided to the buyer. The **firstName** field is used to provide the name that should appear on the return shipment. The **returnAddress** container displays the correct address that the buyer will use to return the item.

**URL format**. See also the [non-wrapped](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/post-order/Samples/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid_provide_return_address__post_basic_in_url_json.txt)
 version of this URL.

POST https://api.ebay.com/post-order/v2/casemanagement/5\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*3/provide\_return\_address
{ 
"firstName": "e\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*1",
"returnAddress":
    { 
    "addressLine1": "2\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*e",
    "addressType": "BUSINESS",
    "city": "San Jose",
    "country": "US",
    "postalCode": "9\*\*\*5",
    "stateOrProvince": "California"
    }
}

**Output**

There is no output payload with this response. A successful call contains an HTTP status code of 200.

**JSON format**.

| --- | --- |

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

BaseResponse
============

Base response container for all service operations. Contains error information associated with the request.

Calls that use BaseResponse:

[addSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/addSellerProfile.html)
, [consolidateShippingProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/consolidateShippingProfiles.html)
, [getConsolidationJobStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getConsolidationJobStatus.html)
, [getSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getSellerProfiles.html)
, [getVersion](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/getVersion.html)
, [removeOverrides](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/removeOverrides.html)
, [removeProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/removeProfile.html)
, [removeSellerProfiles](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/removeSellerProfiles.html)
, [setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

 to learn which calls use extension.

  

timestamp ( _[dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This value represents the date and time when eBay processed the request. The time zone of this value is GMT and the format is the ISO 8601 date and time format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ).

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#timestamp)
 to learn which calls use timestamp.

  

version ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The version of the response payload schema. Indicates the version of the schema that eBay used to process the request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#version)
 to learn which calls use version.

# Order fulfillment response file definitions | eBay Developers Program

[Skip to main content](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-definitions-response-order-fulfillment-feed.html#mainContent)

An order fulfillment response tells you if all the data in an order fulfillment feed was processed successfully or not.

Sample response files:

*   [order-fulfillment-response.csv](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/res/Resources/images/mip-user/mip-samples/ebay-response-csv/order-fulfillment-response.csv)
    
*   [order-fulfillment-response.xml](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/res/Resources/images/mip-user/mip-samples/ebay-response-xml/order-fulfillment-response.xml)
    

**Note:** The fields in the table below are ordered according to their order in the CSV response file. The parent is provided for XML fields in order to make it easier to locate that field in the XML response file.

 
| Field name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CSV: `Order ID`<br><br>XML:  <br><br>`orderFulfillmentDetail.OrderID` | The unique identifier of an order that was updated in an order fulfillment feed.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `Line Item ID`<br><br>XML:<br><br>`orderFulfillmentDetail.lineItemID` | The unique identifier of an order line item. Included if a lineItemID was processed in an order fulfillment feed.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Logistics Status`<br><br>XML:<br><br>`` orderFulfillmentDetail.`logisticsStatus` `` | This value indicates the logistics status updated applied to the order or line item.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `Status`<br><br>XML:<br><br>`` orderFulfillmentDetail.`Status` `` | The result of the action requested in the uploaded feed:<br><br>*   `SUCCESS`: The logistics action has been successfully applied to the order or line item.<br>*   `FAILED`: The logistics action applied to the order or line item has failed. Check if there is an available error **message**.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Always Returned |
| CSV: `Message Type`<br><br>XML: `messageInfo.messageType` | The type of error or warning. This field is only returned if there was an issue creating or updating the order fulfillment.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Message ID`<br><br>XML: `messageInfo.messageID` | The unique identifier of the error or warning. This field is only returned if there was an issue creating or updating the order fulfillment.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |
| CSV: `Message`<br><br>XML: `messageInfo.message` | The actual text of the error or warning. This field is only returned if there was an issue creating or updating the order fulfillment. This field can include information about errors and how to correct them.<br><br>**Occurrence**: Conditionally Returned |

Additional container in XML response
------------------------------------

In addition to the fields called out in the table above, the feed response file will also have a **feedSummary** container. See [XML feedSummary container](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/mip-user-guide/mip-overview-response-files.html#XML)
 for details.

#### Thank you for helping us to improve the eBay developer program.

While we are not able to respond directly to your comments, we will carefully review them to improve your experience with eBay developer program!

If you need help, contact [Developer Technical Support](https://developer.ebay.com/my/support/tickets)
.
