| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productDetails.value.number  <br>  .**unitOfMeasurement** | [token](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token) | Conditionally | Unit of measure for a numeric value.<br><br>**Allowed values:**<br><br>bps<br><br>Bits per second. A unit of data transmission.<br><br>kbps<br><br>Kilobits per second. A unit of data transmission.<br><br>bit<br><br>Binary digit. A unit of information storage.<br><br>byte<br><br>An ordered collection of bits. A unit of information storage equal to 8 bits in most contexts.<br><br>kb<br><br>Kilobit. A unit of information storage equal to 1024 bits.<br><br>MB<br><br>Megabyte. A unit of information storage equal to one million bytes.<br><br>mW<br><br>Megawatt. A unit of power equal to one million watts.<br><br>W<br><br>Watt. The base SI unit of power.<br><br>Hz<br><br>Hertz. A unit of frequency equal to one cycle per second.<br><br>kHz<br><br>Kilohertz. A unit of frequency equal to 1,000 cycles per second.<br><br>m<br><br>Meter. The base unit of distance.<br><br>mm<br><br>Megameter. A unit of distance equal to one million meters.<br><br>rph<br><br>Revolutions per hour. A unit of frequency.<br><br>hr<br><br>Hour. A unit of time equal to 3,600 seconds.<br><br>min<br><br>Minute. A unit of time equal to 60 seconds.<br><br>ms<br><br>Microsecond. A unit of time equal to one millionth of a second.<br><br>ns<br><br>Nanosecond. A unit of time equal to 10\-9 seconds.<br><br>sec<br><br>Seconds. The base unit of time.<br><br>g<br><br>Grams. A unit of mass equal to one one-thousandth of the SI base unit, the kilogram.<br><br>lbs<br><br>Pounds. The base Imperial unit of mass.<br><br>The allowed units of measure do not apply to all numeric values. For example, when specifying the number of doors, unitOfMeasurement is not needed. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productDetails.value.number  <br>  .**value** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | A numeric value. For example, physical dimensions of a product, such as height or weight, are specified as nummeric values. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productDetails.value.**text** | [StringValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/StringValue.html) | Conditionally | For string value type. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productDetails.value.text  <br>  .**value** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | A string value. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productDetails.value.**URL** | [URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html) | Conditionally | For URL value type. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productDetails.value.URL  <br>  .**value** | [anyURI](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#anyURI) | Conditionally | As part of request, this will be the URL to download media file. As part of response, this will be the URL where the media file is stored on eBay Picture Services (EPS).  <br>**Max length:** 400. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .**productIdentifier** | [ProductIdentifier](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ProductIdentifier.html) | Conditionally | The eBay produdt ID (ePID) or external IDs associated with the product. Use the Reference value as input to search for the same product in the future, or use the ISBN, EAN, or UPC value (if returned). The ISBN, EAN, and UPC values can also be useful as keys if your application is comparing products across different sites. Always returned when Product is returned. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productIdentifier.**EAN** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | European Article Number (EAN) value for books. (This is used more commonly in European countries.) If you know a book's EAN, you can use this instead of the eBay CatalogItem or Reference ID to search for that book.  <br>**Max length:** 13. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productIdentifier.**ePID** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | eBay Product ID. eBay's global reference ID for a catalog product. On the eBay Web site, this is known as the "ePID". A reference ID is a fixed reference to a product (regardless of version). One reference ID can be associated with multiple ProductID values. The value should start with "EPID", for example "EPID30971438" (without quotes). You can find reference IDs for products by using GetProducts (or FindProducts in the Shopping API). You can also find the ePID on eBay's Web site.  <br>  <br>If the primary and secondary categories are both catalog-enabled, this ID should correspond to the primary category (not the secondary category).  <br>**Max length:** 38. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productIdentifier.**ISBN** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | ISBN-10 or ISBN-13 value for books. (The string length of ProductID indicates whether the ID is 10 or 13 characters.) If you know a book's ISBN, you can use this instead of the eBay CatalogItem or Reference ID to search for that book.  <br>**Max length:** 13. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productIdentifier.**productId** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | eBay's unique identifier for a specific version of a product. This is the long alphanumeric ID that is returned from GetProductSearchResults and related calls. See the Trading API Guide for information about finding this type of product ID. (For the shorter ePID value that is displayed on the eBay Web site, see ProductReferenceID instead.)  <br>**Max length:** 4000. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productIdentifier.**UPC** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | eBay Product ID.  <br>**Max length:** 12. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .**productStatus** | [ProductStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ProductStatus.html) | Conditionally | Indicates whether or not a product is disabled for selling, product reviews, or use on Half.com. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productStatus  <br>  .**excludeForeBayReviews** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Conditionally | Product cannot be used in product review flows. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productStatus  <br>  .**excludeForeBaySelling** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Conditionally | Product cannot be used in selling flows on eBay. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .productStatus  <br>  .**excludeForHalfSelling** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Conditionally | Product cannot be used in selling flows on half.com. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .**stockPhotoURL** | [StockPhotoURL](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/StockPhotoURL.html) | Conditionally | Thumbnail and standard stockphoto URLs. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .stockPhotoURL.**standard** | [URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html) | Conditionally | Fully qualified URL for a stock image (if any) that is associated with a catalog product. The URL is for the image eBay usually displays in product search results (usually 70px tall). It may be helpful to calculate the dimensions of the photo programmatically before displaying it. Only returned if a URL is available for the product. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .stockPhotoURL.standard.**value** | [anyURI](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#anyURI) | Conditionally | As part of request, this will be the URL to download media file. As part of response, this will be the URL where the media file is stored on eBay Picture Services (EPS).  <br>**Max length:** 400. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .stockPhotoURL.**thumbnail** | [URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html) | Conditionally | Fully qualified URL for the thumbnail of a stock image (if any) that is associated with a catalog product. The URL is for the image eBay usually displays in product search results (usually 40px tall). It may be helpful to calculate the dimensions of the photo programmatically before displaying it. Only returned if a URL is available for the product. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .stockPhotoURL.thumbnail.**value** | [anyURI](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#anyURI) | Conditionally | As part of request, this will be the URL to download media file. As part of response, this will be the URL where the media file is stored on eBay Picture Services (EPS).  <br>**Max length:** 400. |
| productSearchResult.products  <br>  .**type** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | Indicates if a product is the top or main product in a family (Head) or a variant of the head product (Member). If a product is not part of a product family, it is also the Head.  <br>  <br>Sometimes several versions (or editions) of a product may be available. All versions of the same product are considered to be part of the same product family. Some product families may contain only one product; others may contain many products.<br><br>**Allowed values:**<br><br>Head<br><br>The product is the the head of the product family.<br><br>Member<br><br>The product is a variant of the head product. |

**Standard Output Fields**  

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **ack** | [AckValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/AckValue.html) | Always | Indicates whether or not errors or warnings were generated during the processing of the request.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**:  <br>  <br><br>Failure<br><br>The request was processed successfully, but something occurred that may affect your application or the user.<br><br>PartialFailure<br><br>The request that triggered the error was processed successfully but with some warnings.<br><br>Success<br><br>The request that triggered the error was not processed successfully. When a serious application-level error occurs, the error is returned instead of the business data.<br><br>Warning<br><br>The request that triggered the error was processed successfully but with some warnings.<br><br>  <br>Code so that your app gracefully handles any future changes to this list. |
| **errorMessage** | [ErrorMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorMessage.html) | Conditionally | Description of an error or warning that occurred when eBay processed the request. Not returned if the ack value is Success. |
| errorMessage.**error** | [ErrorData](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html) | Conditionally,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | Details about a single error. |
| errorMessage.error.**category** | [ErrorCategory](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorCategory.html) | Conditionally | There are three categories of errors: request errors, application errors, and system errors.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**:  <br>  <br><br>Application<br><br>An error occurred due to a problem with the request, with the most likely source being the application sending the request. For example, the request is missing a required data element or it contains an invalid field. The problem must be corrected before the request can be resent. Inspect the error message to find the cause of the problem. If the problem is due to an application error, modify the application and resend the request. If the error is due to invalid data, the source of the data must be corrected before you resend the resend request to eBay.<br><br>Request<br><br>An error occurred due to a problem with the request, with the most likely source being missing or invalid data in the request. The problem must be corrected before the request can be retried. Inspect the error message to find the cause of the problem. If the problem is a result of end-user data, alert the end-user to the problem and provide the means for them to correct the problem. Once the problem is resolved, resend the request to eBay.<br><br>System<br><br>Indicates that an error has occurred on the eBay system side. For example, a database or server could be down. Inspect the error message to find the cause of the problem. If the problem is on the eBay side, an application can retry the request as-is a reasonable number of times (eBay recommends twice). If the error persists, contact Developer Technical Support. Once the problem has been resolved, the request may be resent in its original form.<br><br>  <br>Code so that your app gracefully handles any future changes to this list. |
| errorMessage.error.**domain** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | Name of the domain in which the error occurred.<br><br>**domain values:**<br><br>Marketplace<br><br>A business or validation error occurred in the service.<br><br>SOA<br><br>An exception occurred in the Service Oriented Architecture (SOA) framework. |
| errorMessage.error.**errorId** | [long](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long) | Conditionally | A unique code that identifies the particular error condition that occurred. Your application can use error codes as identifiers in your customized error-handling algorithms. |
| errorMessage.error.**exceptionId** | [token](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token) | Conditionally | Unique identifier for an exception associated with an error. |
| errorMessage.error.**message** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | A detailed description of the condition that caused in the error. |
| errorMessage.error.**parameter** | [ErrorParameter](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorParameter.html)<br> ([string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)<br>) | Conditionally,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | Various warning and error messages return one or more variables that contain contextual information about the error. This is often the field or value that triggered the error. |
| errorMessage.error.**parameter**  <br>  \[ attribute **name** \] | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | Various warning and error messages return one or more variables that contain contextual information about the error. This is often the field or value that triggered the error. |
| errorMessage.error.**severity** | [ErrorSeverity](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorSeverity.html) | Conditionally | Indicates whether the reported problem is fatal (an error) or is less- severe (a warning). Review the error message details for information on the cause.  <br>  <br>If the request fails and the application is the source of the error (for example, a required element is missing), update the application before you retry the request. If the problem is due to incorrect user data, alert the end-user to the problem and provide the means for them to correct the data. Once the problem in the application or data is resolved, re-send the request to eBay.  <br>  <br>If the source of the problem is on eBay's side, you can retry the request a reasonable number of times (eBay recommends you try the request twice). If the error persists, contact Developer Technical Support. Once the problem has been resolved, you can resend the request in its original form.  <br>  <br>If a warning occurs, warning information is returned in addition to the business data. Normally, you do not need to resend the request (as the original request was successful). However, depending on the cause of the warning, you might need to contact the end user, or eBay, to effect a long term solution to the problem.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**:  <br>  <br><br>Error<br><br>eBay encountered a fatal error during the processing of the request, causing the request to fail. When eBay encounters an error, it returns error data instead of the requested business data. Inspect the error details and resolve the problem before resubmitting the request.<br><br>Warning<br><br>The request was successfully processed, but eBay encountered a non-fatal error during the processing that could affect the data returned. For example, eBay might have changed the value of an input field. In this case, eBay returns a successful response, but it also returns a warning. For best results, requests should return without warnings. Inspect the warning details and resolve the problem before resubmitting the request.<br><br>  <br>Code so that your app gracefully handles any future changes to this list. |
| errorMessage.error.**subdomain** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | Name of the subdomain in which the error occurred.<br><br>**subdomain values:**<br><br>MarketplaceCatalog<br><br>The error is specific to the Marketplace Catalog subdomain.<br><br>MarketplaceCommon<br><br>The error is common to all Marketplace services. |
| **timestamp** | [dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime) | Always | This value represents the date and time when eBay processed the request. This value is returned in GMT, the ISO 8601 date and time format (YYYY- MM- DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ). See the "dateTime" type for information about the time format, and for details on converting to and from the GMT time zone. |
| **version** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Always | The release version that eBay used to process the request. Developer Technical Support may ask you for the version value if you work with them to troubleshoot issues.  <br>  <br>**Note:** The version in use is normally the latest release version, as specified in the release notes. Note that eBay releases the API to international sites about a week after the API version is released to the US site. |

# getProductCompatibilities - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

getProductCompatibilities
=========================

**Note:** This call has been deprecated and will be decommissioned on April 27th, 2026. Users are recommended to migrate to the [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/sell/metadata/resources/compatibilities/methods/getProductCompatibilities)
 method of the **Metadata API**.

This call retrieves all available item compatibility details for the specified product. Item compatibility properties are returned as name-value pairs. The request requires a product identifier, either an eBay-specific identifier (e.g., ePID) or an external identifier, such as a Universal Part Code (UPC). Use this call to get complete compatibility details for products you find using **findProductsByCompatibility**. For example, if you searched for parts that fit a Honda Accord, you can use this call to see the years, engine, and trim for which the part is compatible, as well as any other compatibilities for the product.

Use either **dataset** or **datasetPropertyName** to restrict the name-value pairs returned in the response. The **dataset** value specifies the type of compatibility properties you want back. For example, if you specify Searchable, your compatibility details will be contain properties that can be used to search for products, such as Make and Model. Some compatibility properties, such as FitmentComments, are not intended for product searches.

**Note:** Parts Compatibility is supported in limited Parts & Accessories categories, for the eBay US Motors (100), UK (3), AU (15) and DE (77) sites only.

See also [Samples](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html#Samples)
.

| Argument | Type | Occurrence | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **applicationPropertyFilter** | [PropertyValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/PropertyValue.html) | Optional,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | This will contain the property name value pairs of the application, such as a particular vehicle make and model. Application property filters are specified as name-value pairs. |
| applicationPropertyFilter  <br>  .**propertyName** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | This is the canonical name of the property. Used to identify a property within a catalog that this category belongs to. |
| applicationPropertyFilter  <br>  .**value** | [Value](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/Value.html) | Optional,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | A value for the property. |
| applicationPropertyFilter  <br>  .value.**number** | [NumericValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/NumericValue.html) | Optional | For numeric value type. |
| applicationPropertyFilter  <br>  .value.number  <br>  .**unitOfMeasurement** | [token](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token) | Optional | Unit of measure for a numeric value.<br><br>**Allowed values:**<br><br>bps<br><br>Bits per second. A unit of data transmission.<br><br>kbps<br><br>Kilobits per second. A unit of data transmission.<br><br>bit<br><br>Binary digit. A unit of information storage.<br><br>byte<br><br>An ordered collection of bits. A unit of information storage equal to 8 bits in most contexts.<br><br>kb<br><br>Kilobit. A unit of information storage equal to 1024 bits.<br><br>MB<br><br>Megabyte. A unit of information storage equal to one million bytes.<br><br>mW<br><br>Megawatt. A unit of power equal to one million watts.<br><br>W<br><br>Watt. The base SI unit of power.<br><br>Hz<br><br>Hertz. A unit of frequency equal to one cycle per second.<br><br>kHz<br><br>Kilohertz. A unit of frequency equal to 1,000 cycles per second.<br><br>m<br><br>Meter. The base unit of distance.<br><br>mm<br><br>Megameter. A unit of distance equal to one million meters.<br><br>rph<br><br>Revolutions per hour. A unit of frequency.<br><br>hr<br><br>Hour. A unit of time equal to 3,600 seconds.<br><br>min<br><br>Minute. A unit of time equal to 60 seconds.<br><br>ms<br><br>Microsecond. A unit of time equal to one millionth of a second.<br><br>ns<br><br>Nanosecond. A unit of time equal to 10\-9 seconds.<br><br>sec<br><br>Seconds. The base unit of time.<br><br>g<br><br>Grams. A unit of mass equal to one one-thousandth of the SI base unit, the kilogram.<br><br>lbs<br><br>Pounds. The base Imperial unit of mass.<br><br>The allowed units of measure do not apply to all numeric values. For example, when specifying the number of doors, unitOfMeasurement is not needed. |
| applicationPropertyFilter  <br>  .value.number.**value** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | A numeric value. For example, physical dimensions of a product, such as height or weight, are specified as nummeric values. |
| applicationPropertyFilter  <br>  .value.**text** | [StringValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/StringValue.html) | Optional | For string value type. |
| applicationPropertyFilter  <br>  .value.text.**value** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | A string value. |
| applicationPropertyFilter  <br>  .value.**URL** | [URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html) | Optional | For URL value type. |
| applicationPropertyFilter  <br>  .value.URL.**value** | [anyURI](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#anyURI) | Optional | As part of request, this will be the URL to download media file. As part of response, this will be the URL where the media file is stored on eBay Picture Services (EPS).  <br>**Max length:** 400. |
| **dataset** | [token](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token) | Optional,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | This will define what are properties that will be returned for this catelog-enabled category.<br><br>**Dataset values:**<br><br>DisplayableProductDetails<br><br>Properies for use in a user interface to describe products.<br><br>DisplayableSearchResults<br><br>Properties for use in results for product searches.<br><br>Searchable<br><br>Properties for use in searches.<br><br>Sortable<br><br>Properties that are suitable for sorting.<br><br>User can either enter dataset name or enter individual property names (datasetPropertyName) from the dataset. Only one should be preset at a time. |
| **datasetPropertyName** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Required,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[1..\*\] | Individual property name from the dataset. You can either enter dataset name or enter individual property names from the dataset. Only one should be preset at a time. |
| **disabledProductFilter** | [ProductStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ProductStatus.html) | Optional | Specifies whether or not to filter out the products which are disabled for selling on eBay, disabled for product reviews, or disabled for selling on half.com. |
| disabledProductFilter  <br>  .**excludeForeBayReviews** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Optional | Product cannot be used in product review flows.  <br>**Default:** false. |
| disabledProductFilter  <br>  .**excludeForeBaySelling** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Optional | Product cannot be used in selling flows on eBay.  <br>**Default:** false. |
| disabledProductFilter  <br>  .**excludeForHalfSelling** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Optional | Product cannot be used in selling flows on half.com.  <br>**Default:** false. |
| **paginationInput** | [PaginationInput](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/PaginationInput.html) | Optional | Controls the pagination of the result set. Child elements specify the maximum number of products to return per call and the page of data to return. Controls the number of products returned in the response, but does not specify the amount of data to return for each product.  <br>  <br>**Note:** No more than 10,000 products can be retrieved for a given search, regardless of how many matches are found. This limit is enforced by the maximum page number allowed (100) and the maximum entries per page allowed (20). |
| paginationInput.**entriesPerPage** | [int](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int) | Optional | Specifies the maximum number of entries to return in a single call. If the number of entries found on the specified pageNumber is less than the value specified here, the number of items returned will be less than the value of entriesPerPage. This indicates the end of the result set.  <br>  <br>If entriesPerPage is set to a number greater than 100, the default value, 100, will be used.  <br>**Max:** 100. **Default:** 100. |
| paginationInput.**pageNumber** | [int](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int) | Optional | Specifies which subset of data (or "page") to return in the call response. The number of data pages is determined by the total number of items matching the request search criteria (returned in paginationOutput.totalEntries) divided by the number of entries to display in each response (entriesPerPage). You can return up to the first 100 pages of the result set by issuing multiple requests and specifying, in sequence, the pages to return.  <br>**Max:** 100. **Default:** 1. |
| **productIdentifier** | [ProductIdentifier](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ProductIdentifier.html) | Required | The ProductIdentifierType and a value for one of the supported ProductIdentifierTypes which are unique across all sites (ePID, MementoString) |
| productIdentifier.**EAN** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | European Article Number (EAN) value for books. (This is used more commonly in European countries.) If you know a book's EAN, you can use this instead of the eBay CatalogItem or Reference ID to search for that book.  <br>**Max length:** 13. |
| productIdentifier.**ePID** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | eBay Product ID. eBay's global reference ID for a catalog product. On the eBay Web site, this is known as the "ePID". A reference ID is a fixed reference to a product (regardless of version). One reference ID can be associated with multiple ProductID values. The value should start with "EPID", for example "EPID30971438" (without quotes). You can find reference IDs for products by using GetProducts (or FindProducts in the Shopping API). You can also find the ePID on eBay's Web site.  <br>  <br>If the primary and secondary categories are both catalog-enabled, this ID should correspond to the primary category (not the secondary category).  <br>**Max length:** 38. |
| productIdentifier.**ISBN** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | ISBN-10 or ISBN-13 value for books. (The string length of ProductID indicates whether the ID is 10 or 13 characters.) If you know a book's ISBN, you can use this instead of the eBay CatalogItem or Reference ID to search for that book.  <br>**Max length:** 13. |
| productIdentifier.**productId** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | eBay's unique identifier for a specific version of a product. This is the long alphanumeric ID that is returned from GetProductSearchResults and related calls. See the Trading API Guide for information about finding this type of product ID. (For the shorter ePID value that is displayed on the eBay Web site, see ProductReferenceID instead.)  <br>**Max length:** 4000. |
| productIdentifier.**UPC** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditional | eBay Product ID.  <br>**Max length:** 12. |
| **sortOrder** | [CompatibilitySort](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/CompatibilitySort.html) | Optional,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | We need to allow multiple sort orders. This is very specific to fitment. We allow the caller sorty by Make + Model + year. To start with we will only allow 5 sorts. |
| sortOrder.**sortOrder** | [SortOrder](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/SortOrder.html) | Optional | The property with its associated sort order, ascending or descending. |
| sortOrder.sortOrder.**order** | [OrderType](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/OrderType.html) | Optional | Order of the sort, either Ascending or Descending.  <br>**Default:** Descending.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**:  <br>  <br><br>Ascending<br><br>The results will be sorted by the specified attribute in ascending (low to high) order.<br><br>Descending<br><br>The results will be sorted by the specified attribute in descending (high to low) order. |
| sortOrder.sortOrder  <br>  .**propertyName** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Optional | Output can be sorted by one or more property names. You can look these values up using the **getProductSearchNames** call in the Product Metadata API.  <br>**Max length:** 100. |
| sortOrder.**sortPriority** | [SortPriority](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/SortPriority.html) | Optional | Indicates where in the sequence of ordering the property should ordered. There are five sorting steps, Sort1 through Sort5. When a property is assigned Sort1 as its sort priority, its values are sorted first. Values for the property assigned Sort2, are sorted second, and so on.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**:  <br>  <br><br>Sort1<br><br>Specifies that the associated property should be sorted first.<br><br>Sort2<br><br>Specifies that the associated property should be sorted second.<br><br>Sort3<br><br>Specifies that the associated property should be sorted third.<br><br>Sort4<br><br>Specifies that the associated property should be sorted fourth.<br><br>Sort5<br><br>Specifies that the associated property should be sorted fifth. |

| **compatibilityDetails** | [Product](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/Product.html) | Conditionally,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | Compatibility details for a single product. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .**compatibilityCount** | [int](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int) | Conditionally | **Coming soon.** The quantity of compatibilities that have been specified for the product. When returned, indicates that the product is Parts Compatibility eanbled. |
| compatibilityDetails.**notes** | [Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/Notes.html) | Conditionally | Optional notes that provide additional information about the product, such as the placement of the part or accessory on a vehicle or the type of vehicle a part fits. |
| compatibilityDetails.notes  <br>  .**noteDetails** | [PropertyValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/PropertyValue.html) | Conditionally,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | Additional comments about the product in the form of name-value pairs. |
| compatibilityDetails.notes  <br>  .noteDetails.**propertyName** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | This is the canonical name of the property. Used to identify a property within a catalog that this category belongs to. |
| compatibilityDetails.notes  <br>  .noteDetails.**value** | [Value](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/Value.html) | Conditionally,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | A value for the property. |

For example, physical dimensions of a product, such as height or weight, are specified as nummeric values. |
| compatibilityDetails.notes  <br>  .noteDetails.value.**text** | [StringValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/StringValue.html) | Conditionally | For string value type. |
| compatibilityDetails.notes  <br>  .noteDetails.value.text.**value** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | A string value. |
| compatibilityDetails.notes  <br>  .noteDetails.value.**URL** | [URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html) | Conditionally | For URL value type. |

As part of response, this will be the URL where the media file is stored on eBay Picture Services (EPS).  <br>**Max length:** 400. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .**productDetails** | [PropertyValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/PropertyValue.html) | Conditionally,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | Details about the product in the form of name-value pairs. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productDetails.**propertyName** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | This is the canonical name of the property. Used to identify a property within a catalog that this category belongs to. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productDetails.**value** | [Value](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/Value.html) | Conditionally,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | A value for the property. |

| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productDetails.value.number  <br>  .**value** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | A numeric value. For example, physical dimensions of a product, such as height or weight, are specified as nummeric values. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productDetails.value.**text** | [StringValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/StringValue.html) | Conditionally | For string value type. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productDetails.value.text  <br>  .**value** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | A string value. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productDetails.value.**URL** | [URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html) | Conditionally | For URL value type. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productDetails.value.URL  <br>  .**value** | [anyURI](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#anyURI) | Conditionally | As part of request, this will be the URL to download media file. As part of response, this will be the URL where the media file is stored on eBay Picture Services (EPS).  <br>**Max length:** 400. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .**productIdentifier** | [ProductIdentifier](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ProductIdentifier.html) | Conditionally | The eBay produdt ID (ePID) or external IDs associated with the product. Use the Reference value as input to search for the same product in the future, or use the ISBN, EAN, or UPC value (if returned). The ISBN, EAN, and UPC values can also be useful as keys if your application is comparing products across different sites. Always returned when Product is returned. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productIdentifier.**EAN** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | European Article Number (EAN) value for books. (This is used more commonly in European countries.) If you know a book's EAN, you can use this instead of the eBay CatalogItem or Reference ID to search for that book.  <br>**Max length:** 13. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productIdentifier.**ePID** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | eBay Product ID. eBay's global reference ID for a catalog product. On the eBay Web site, this is known as the "ePID". A reference ID is a fixed reference to a product (regardless of version). One reference ID can be associated with multiple ProductID values. The value should start with "EPID", for example "EPID30971438" (without quotes). You can find reference IDs for products by using GetProducts (or FindProducts in the Shopping API). You can also find the ePID on eBay's Web site.  <br>  <br>If the primary and secondary categories are both catalog-enabled, this ID should correspond to the primary category (not the secondary category).  <br>**Max length:** 38. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productIdentifier.**ISBN** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | ISBN-10 or ISBN-13 value for books. (The string length of ProductID indicates whether the ID is 10 or 13 characters.) If you know a book's ISBN, you can use this instead of the eBay CatalogItem or Reference ID to search for that book.  <br>**Max length:** 13. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productIdentifier.**productId** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | eBay's unique identifier for a specific version of a product. This is the long alphanumeric ID that is returned from GetProductSearchResults and related calls. See the Trading API Guide for information about finding this type of product ID. (For the shorter ePID value that is displayed on the eBay Web site, see ProductReferenceID instead.)  <br>**Max length:** 4000. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productIdentifier.**UPC** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | eBay Product ID.  <br>**Max length:** 12. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .**productStatus** | [ProductStatus](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ProductStatus.html) | Conditionally | Indicates whether or not a product is disabled for selling, product reviews, or use on Half.com. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productStatus  <br>  .**excludeForeBayReviews** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Conditionally | Product cannot be used in product review flows. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productStatus  <br>  .**excludeForeBaySelling** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Conditionally | Product cannot be used in selling flows on eBay. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .productStatus  <br>  .**excludeForHalfSelling** | [boolean](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#boolean) | Conditionally | Product cannot be used in selling flows on half.com. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .**stockPhotoURL** | [StockPhotoURL](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/StockPhotoURL.html) | Conditionally | Thumbnail and standard stockphoto URLs. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .stockPhotoURL.**standard** | [URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html) | Conditionally | Fully qualified URL for a stock image (if any) that is associated with a catalog product. The URL is for the image eBay usually displays in product search results (usually 70px tall). It may be helpful to calculate the dimensions of the photo programmatically before displaying it. Only returned if a URL is available for the product. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .stockPhotoURL.standard.**value** | [anyURI](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#anyURI) | Conditionally | As part of request, this will be the URL to download media file. As part of response, this will be the URL where the media file is stored on eBay Picture Services (EPS).  <br>**Max length:** 400. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .stockPhotoURL.**thumbnail** | [URIValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/URIValue.html) | Conditionally | Fully qualified URL for the thumbnail of a stock image (if any) that is associated with a catalog product. The URL is for the image eBay usually displays in product search results (usually 40px tall). It may be helpful to calculate the dimensions of the photo programmatically before displaying it. Only returned if a URL is available for the product. |
| compatibilityDetails  <br>  .stockPhotoURL.thumbnail.**value** | [anyURI](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#anyURI) | Conditionally | As part of request, this will be the URL to download media file. As part of response, this will be the URL where the media file is stored on eBay Picture Services (EPS).  <br>**Max length:** 400. |
| compatibilityDetails.**type** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | Indicates if a product is the top or main product in a family (Head) or a variant of the head product (Member). If a product is not part of a product family, it is also the Head.  <br>  <br>Sometimes several versions (or editions) of a product may be available. All versions of the same product are considered to be part of the same product family. Some product families may contain only one product; others may contain many products.<br><br>**Allowed values:**<br><br>Head<br><br>The product is the the head of the product family.<br><br>Member<br><br>The product is a variant of the head product. |
| **paginationOutput** | [PaginationOutput](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/PaginationOutput.html) | Always | Indicates the pagination of the result set. Child elements indicate the maximum number of compatibilityDetails nodes returned per response and which page of data has been returned. |
| paginationOutput  <br>  .**entriesPerPage** | [int](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int) | Conditionally | The maximum number of items that can be returned in the response. This number is always equal to the value input for paginationInput.entriesPerPage.  <br>  <br>The end of the result set has been reached if the number specified for entriesPerPage is greater than the number of items found on the specified pageNumber. In this case, there will be fewer items returned than the number specified in entriesPerPage. This can be determined by comparing the entriesPerPage value with the value returned in the count attribute for the searchResult field. |
| paginationOutput.**pageNumber** | [int](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int) | Conditionally | The subset of item data returned in the current response. Search results are divided into sets, or "pages," of item data. The number of pages is equal to the total number of entries matching the search criteria divided by the value specified for entriesPerPage in the request. The response for a request contains one "page" of data.  <br>  <br>This returned value indicates the page number of data returned (a subset of the complete result set). If this field contains 1, the response contains the first page of item data (the default). If the value returned in totalEntries is less than the value for entriesPerPage, pageNumber returns 1 and the response contains the entire result set.  <br>  <br>The value of pageNumber is normally equal to the value input for paginationInput.pageNumber. However, if the number input for pageNumber is greater than the total possible pages of output, eBay returns the last page of item data in the result set, and the value for pageNumber is set to the respective (last) page number. |
| paginationOutput.**totalEntries** | [int](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int) | Conditionally | The total number of entries found that match the search criteria in your request. Depending on the input value for entriesPerPage, the response might include only a portion (a page) of the entire result set. A value of "0" is returned if eBay does not find any entries that match the search criteria. |
| paginationOutput.**totalPages** | [int](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#int) | Conditionally | The total number of pages of data that could be returned by repeated search requests. Note that if you modify the value of inputPagination.entriesPerPage in a request, the value output for totalPages will change. A value of "0" is returned if eBay does not find any entries that match the search criteria. |

**Standard Output Fields**  

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **ack** | [AckValue](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/AckValue.html) | Always | Indicates whether or not errors or warnings were generated during the processing of the request.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**:  <br>  <br><br>Failure<br><br>The request was processed successfully, but something occurred that may affect your application or the user.<br><br>PartialFailure<br><br>The request that triggered the error was processed successfully but with some warnings.<br><br>Success<br><br>The request that triggered the error was not processed successfully. When a serious application-level error occurs, the error is returned instead of the business data.<br><br>Warning<br><br>The request that triggered the error was processed successfully but with some warnings.<br><br>  <br>Code so that your app gracefully handles any future changes to this list. |
| **errorMessage** | [ErrorMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorMessage.html) | Conditionally | Description of an error or warning that occurred when eBay processed the request. Not returned if the ack value is Success. |
| errorMessage.**error** | [ErrorData](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorData.html) | Conditionally,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | Details about a single error. |
| errorMessage.error.**category** | [ErrorCategory](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorCategory.html) | Conditionally | There are three categories of errors: request errors, application errors, and system errors.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**:  <br>  <br><br>Application<br><br>An error occurred due to a problem with the request, with the most likely source being the application sending the request. For example, the request is missing a required data element or it contains an invalid field. The problem must be corrected before the request can be resent. Inspect the error message to find the cause of the problem. If the problem is due to an application error, modify the application and resend the request. If the error is due to invalid data, the source of the data must be corrected before you resend the resend request to eBay.<br><br>Request<br><br>An error occurred due to a problem with the request, with the most likely source being missing or invalid data in the request. The problem must be corrected before the request can be retried. Inspect the error message to find the cause of the problem. If the problem is a result of end-user data, alert the end-user to the problem and provide the means for them to correct the problem. Once the problem is resolved, resend the request to eBay.<br><br>System<br><br>Indicates that an error has occurred on the eBay system side. For example, a database or server could be down. Inspect the error message to find the cause of the problem. If the problem is on the eBay side, an application can retry the request as-is a reasonable number of times (eBay recommends twice). If the error persists, contact Developer Technical Support. Once the problem has been resolved, the request may be resent in its original form.<br><br>  <br>Code so that your app gracefully handles any future changes to this list. |
| errorMessage.error.**domain** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | Name of the domain in which the error occurred.<br><br>**domain values:**<br><br>Marketplace<br><br>A business or validation error occurred in the service.<br><br>SOA<br><br>An exception occurred in the Service Oriented Architecture (SOA) framework. |
| errorMessage.error.**errorId** | [long](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#long) | Conditionally | A unique code that identifies the particular error condition that occurred. Your application can use error codes as identifiers in your customized error-handling algorithms. |
| errorMessage.error.**exceptionId** | [token](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#token) | Conditionally | Unique identifier for an exception associated with an error. |
| errorMessage.error.**message** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | A detailed description of the condition that caused in the error. |
| errorMessage.error.**parameter** | [ErrorParameter](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorParameter.html)<br> ([string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)<br>) | Conditionally,  <br>[repeatable](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#repeatable)<br>: \[0..\*\] | Various warning and error messages return one or more variables that contain contextual information about the error. This is often the field or value that triggered the error. |
| errorMessage.error.**parameter**  <br>  \[ attribute **name** \] | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | Various warning and error messages return one or more variables that contain contextual information about the error. This is often the field or value that triggered the error. |
| errorMessage.error.**severity** | [ErrorSeverity](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/ErrorSeverity.html) | Conditionally | Indicates whether the reported problem is fatal (an error) or is less- severe (a warning). Review the error message details for information on the cause.  <br>  <br>If the request fails and the application is the source of the error (for example, a required element is missing), update the application before you retry the request. If the problem is due to incorrect user data, alert the end-user to the problem and provide the means for them to correct the data. Once the problem in the application or data is resolved, re-send the request to eBay.  <br>  <br>If the source of the problem is on eBay's side, you can retry the request a reasonable number of times (eBay recommends you try the request twice). If the error persists, contact Developer Technical Support. Once the problem has been resolved, you can resend the request in its original form.  <br>  <br>If a warning occurs, warning information is returned in addition to the business data. Normally, you do not need to resend the request (as the original request was successful). However, depending on the cause of the warning, you might need to contact the end user, or eBay, to effect a long term solution to the problem.  <br>  <br>**Applicable values**:  <br>  <br><br>Error<br><br>eBay encountered a fatal error during the processing of the request, causing the request to fail. When eBay encounters an error, it returns error data instead of the requested business data. Inspect the error details and resolve the problem before resubmitting the request.<br><br>Warning<br><br>The request was successfully processed, but eBay encountered a non-fatal error during the processing that could affect the data returned. For example, eBay might have changed the value of an input field. In this case, eBay returns a successful response, but it also returns a warning. For best results, requests should return without warnings. Inspect the warning details and resolve the problem before resubmitting the request.<br><br>  <br>Code so that your app gracefully handles any future changes to this list. |
| errorMessage.error.**subdomain** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Conditionally | Name of the subdomain in which the error occurred.<br><br>**subdomain values:**<br><br>MarketplaceCatalog<br><br>The error is specific to the Marketplace Catalog subdomain.<br><br>MarketplaceCommon<br><br>The error is common to all Marketplace services. |
| **timestamp** | [dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime) | Always | This value represents the date and time when eBay processed the request. This value is returned in GMT, the ISO 8601 date and time format (YYYY- MM- DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ). See the "dateTime" type for information about the time format, and for details on converting to and from the GMT time zone. |
| **version** | [string](https://developer.ebay.com/DevZone/Product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string) | Always | The release version that eBay used to process the request. Developer Technical Support may ask you for the version value if you work with them to troubleshoot issues.  <br>  <br>**Note:** The version in use is normally the latest release version, as specified in the release notes. Note that eBay releases the API to international sites about a week after the API version is released to the US site. |

# MoneyMovementEntries - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

MoneyMovementEntries
====================

This type is used to provide details on any monetary transactions that occur in association with an INR inquiry or a case.

Type that uses MoneyMovementEntries:

[ClaimHistoryEventModel](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ClaimHistoryEventModel.html)

Calls that use MoneyMovementEntries:

[/post-order/v2/casemanagement/{caseId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_casemanagement-caseid__get.html)
, [/post-order/v2/inquiry/{inquiryId} GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_inquiry-inquiryid__get.html)

Field
-----

moneyMovementEntryList ( array of _[MoneyMovementEntry](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/MoneyMovementEntry.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This array contains details about one or more monetary transactions associated with an INR inquiry or a case. This array is only returned if monetary transactions have occurred with the INR inquiry or case.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#moneyMovementEntryList)
 to learn which calls use moneyMovementEntryList.

---

# SetSellerProfileRequest - API Reference - Business Policies Management API

eBay Business Policies Management APIVersion 1.0.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/guides/features-guide/default.html#features-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/BusinessPoliciesAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/index.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

SetSellerProfileRequest ( [BaseRequest](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/BaseRequest.html)
 )
===============================================================================================================================

Sellers use this call to modify one or more business policies. With one call instance, the seller can modify a payment policy, a return policy, a shipping policy, or any combination of the three policy types.

Call that uses SetSellerProfileRequest:

[setSellerProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/setSellerProfile.html)

Fields
------

paymentProfile ( _[PaymentProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentProfile.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Root container for a seller's payment policy. The **paymentProfile** container consists of payment information, the name and description of the policy, and the site and category group to which the payment policy will be applied.  
  
The **paymentProfile** container is conditionally required if the seller wants to modify an existing payment policy.  
  
Sellers only pass in values for the fields they want to change. To delete an optional field, sellers can pass an empty value into the field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#paymentProfile)
 to learn which calls use paymentProfile.

  

returnPolicyProfile ( _[ReturnPolicyProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyProfile.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Root container for a seller's return policy. The **returnPolicyProfile** container consists of return policy information, the name and description of the policy, and the site and category group to which the return policy will be applied.  
  
The **returnPolicyProfile** container is conditionally required if the seller wants to modify an existing return policy.  
  
Sellers only pass in values for the fields that they want to change. To delete an optional field, sellers can pass an empty value into the field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#returnPolicyProfile)
 to learn which calls use returnPolicyProfile.

  

shippingPolicyProfile ( _[ShippingPolicyProfile](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyProfile.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Root container for a seller's shipping policy. The **shippingPolicyProfile** container consists of shipping information, the name and description of the policy, and the site and category group to which the shipping policy will be applied.  
  
The **shippingPolicyProfile** container is conditionally required if the seller wants to modify an existing shipping policy.  
  
Sellers only pass in values for the fields they want to change. To delete an optional field, sellers can pass an empty value into the field.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#shippingPolicyProfile)
 to learn which calls use shippingPolicyProfile.

---

# GetSummaryResponse - API Reference - Post-Order API

eBay Post-Order APIVersion 2.8.0

*   [User Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/user-guides/static/post-order-user-guide-landing.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

GetSummaryResponse
==================

The base response type for the **GET /post-order/v2/return/search** method, which is used to search for return requests.

Call that uses GetSummaryResponse:

[/post-order/v2/return/search GET](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/post-order_v2_return_search__get.html)

Fields
------

countSummary ( array of _[CountSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/CountSummaryType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This array holds a count of return requests in different states. A separate **count** and **type** pair is returned for each return request state, such as `ALL_OPEN`, `RETURN_STARTED`, or `ITEM_DELIVERED`. The return request states that are returned depend on the use of the **return\_state** query parameter in the request. If this query parameter is omitted in the request, the count for every return request state is returned. However, if this query parameter is used, only the count for the specified return request state will be returned.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#countSummary)
 to learn which calls use countSummary.

  

members ( array of _[ReturnSummaryType](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/ReturnSummaryType.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This array provides information on one or more return requests that match the filter criteria in the request.  
  
This array is returned as empty if no return requests match the filter criteria in the request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#members)
 to learn which calls use members.

  

paginationOutput ( _[PaginationOutput](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/PaginationOutput.html)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This container consists of metadata related to the current page of results. It shows the total number of return requests that match the filter criteria, the total number of return requests to show per page of results, and the total number of pages in the results set.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#paginationOutput)
 to learn which calls use paginationOutput.

  

total ( _[integer](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#integer)
_ ) [\[0..?\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This field is not currently used. The total number of return requests and the number of return requests on the current page are shown in the **paginationOutput** container.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/post-order/fieldindex.html#total)
 to learn which calls use total.

---

# BaseServiceResponse - API Reference - Product API

eBay Product APIVersion 1.6.0

*   [Features Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/guides/ebayfeatures/index.html)
    
*   [Users Guide](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/ProductAPIGuide.html)
    
*   [Making a Call](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/MakingACall.html)
    
*   [API Reference](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/Concepts/LandAPIRef.html)
    
*   [Tutorials](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/HowTo/index.html)
    
*   [Release Notes](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/ReleaseNotes.html)
    

BaseServiceResponse
===================

Base response container for all service operations. Contains error information associated with the request.

Calls that use BaseServiceResponse:

[findProducts](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/findProducts.html)
, [getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)
, [getProductDetails](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductDetails.html)

Fields
------

ack ( _[AckValue](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/AckValue.html)
_ ) [\[1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Indicates whether or not errors or warnings were generated during the processing of the request.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#ack)
 to learn which calls use ack.

  

errorMessage ( _[ErrorMessage](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/ErrorMessage.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Description of an error or warning that occurred when eBay processed the request. Not returned if the ack value is Success.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#errorMessage)
 to learn which calls use errorMessage.

  

timestamp ( _[dateTime](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#dateTime)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

This value represents the date and time when eBay processed the request. This value is returned in GMT, the ISO 8601 date and time format (YYYY- MM- DDTHH:MM:SS.SSSZ). See the "dateTime" type for information about the time format, and for details on converting to and from the GMT time zone.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#timestamp)
 to learn which calls use timestamp.

  

version ( _[string](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#string)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

The release version that eBay used to process the request. Developer Technical Support may ask you for the version value if you work with them to troubleshoot issues.  
  
**Note:** The version in use is normally the latest release version, as specified in the release notes. Note that eBay releases the API to international sites about a week after the API version is released to the US site.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/fieldindex.html#version)
 to learn which calls use version.

---

GetSellerProfilesResponse ( [BaseResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/BaseResponse.html)
 )
===================================================================================================================================

Response container for the **getSellerProfiles** call.

Call that uses GetSellerProfilesResponse:

Fields
------

paymentProfileList ( _[PaymentProfileList](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/PaymentProfileList.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Container consisting of one or more payment policies that match the input criteria in the **getSellerProfiles** request. This container is not returned if no payment policies match the input criteria.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#paymentProfileList)
 to learn which calls use paymentProfileList.

  

returnPolicyProfileList ( _[ReturnPolicyProfileList](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ReturnPolicyProfileList.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Container consisting of one or more return policies that match the input criteria in the **getSellerProfiles** request. This container is not returned if no return policies match the input criteria.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#returnPolicyProfileList)
 to learn which calls use returnPolicyProfileList.

  

shippingPolicyProfile ( _[ShippingPolicyProfileList](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/ShippingPolicyProfileList.html)
_ ) [\[0..1\]](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)

Container consisting of one or more shipping policies that match the input criteria in the **getSellerProfiles** request. This container is not returned if no shipping policies match the input criteria.

See the [Field Index](https://developer.ebay.com/Devzone/business-policies/CallRef/fieldindex.html#shippingPolicyProfile)
 to learn which calls use shippingPolicyProfile.

GetProductCompatiblitiesResponse ( [BaseServiceResponse](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/BaseServiceResponse.html)
 )
==============================================================================================================================================

Response object for getProductFitmentDetails operation.

Call that uses GetProductCompatiblitiesResponse:

[getProductCompatibilities](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/getProductCompatibilities.html)

------

compatibilityDetails ( _[Product](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/Product.html)
_ ) [\[0..\*\]](https://developer.ebay.com/devzone/product/CallRef/types/simpletypes.html#multiplicity)
